Over a hundred Black Knights had gathered in the hall that was once named “the Ark teleporter”, but now the Ark teleporter had been renamed to “the teleporter Watchman 1”, and the one set up on Mars was “Watchman 2”. “Watchman 3” would be set up on the moon.

“Start the molecular locator!”

Chen Chen walked in by himself, only to see that the Watchman teleportation room was much larger than the Gantz sphere teleportation room. It was about two to three hundred square meters, like a massive hall.

The entire hall was made of shining metal, surrounded by rows of complicated equipment, and in the center of the hall was a circular platform about two meters high. On the platform was covered in mysterious red lines — it was the Watchman 1 teleporter.

“Everyone is ready, the molecular locator will be activated now!

“The interstellar coordinate lock is active...

“Remote sensing exploration has been confirmed, ready to start teleportation...

“The target receiver is activated, ready to start teleportation...”

As the voice of X-111 kept updating, Little X’s figure came up from the side. She stood in front of Chen Chen and said happily, “Sir Godfather, you came just in time, the experiment is about to begin!”

Chen Chen nodded and saw that on the other side of the hall, Delta-level personnel walked in tremblingly. At the Black Knight’s signal, they slowly walked onto the Watchman’s platform.

“The personnel are already in place...

“The Watchman teleporter is teleporting in ten seconds...

“Ten!

“Nine!

“Eight!”

...

“Three!

“Two!

“One!”

“Gurgle...”

As the countdown ended, suddenly, a strange liquid gushed from the center of the platform, directly suspended in front of the Delta-level experiment subject.

The experiment subject swallowed hard. He had been asked before to touch the liquid when it appeared. At this moment, seeing the liquid had appeared, he had no choice but to stretch out his hand and touch the liquid uneasily.

“Whoosh!”

However, the moment he touched the stream of water, his body was directly sucked in and then there was a flicker before everyone’s eyes as if a light had flashed by. When they could see again, where were the water stream and the Delta-level research subject on the Watchman teleporter’s platform?

Both of these had disappeared...

“Teleportation of the subject has begun.”

Little X smiled like a pretty flower. She turned her head and looked at Chen Chen. “If there’s no accident, it’ll take ten minutes and thirty-seven seconds. This Delta-level experiment subject will arrive at the Spire 2 Laboratory on Mars. If we wait for Spire 2 to send back a message, it also takes ten minutes and thirty-eight seconds. That is to say, after 21 minutes and fifteen seconds, we’ll be able to know whether the experiment was successful!”

Chen Chen nodded when he heard the words.

He knew that the distance between Mars and the earth was never fixed. The earth moved in an elliptical orbit around the sun and Mars was the same. Therefore, the distance between the two planets changed constantly due to their orbits around the sun.

The distance between the earth and Mars depended on the orbital position of the two planets at the time of the teleportation. The nearest would be about 55 million kilometers and the farthest was 400 million kilometers.

Spire 2 was the name of the underground laboratory built by Chen Chen on Mars. Its code number was A002, which was different from the Spire Experimental Base, No. A001, where Chen Chen was located.

As time passed, twenty minutes later, Watchman 1 received news from Mars. The Delta-level personnel had successfully reached Mars and arrived at the underground base established in the north pole.

Of course, one person was no big deal. Next, a hundred Delta-level personnel walked in. Coerced by the Black Knights with guns and live ammunition, everyone stood on the teleportation platform.

Then, a constant stream of water surged up and one of them was submerged, disappearing in front of everyone.

“The success rate is... 100%.”

It was not until half an hour later when there were no more people on the teleportation platform, Little X reported again, “The 100 people that had been teleported were all successful and our power system is fully able to withstand it. After this, they’ll try to teleport the group of people back. If nothing unexpected happens, then we’ll continue and try to teleport items.”

“Very good.”

Chen Chen nodded. “When all the tests are completed, notify me.”

Then Chen Chen left the Watchman teleportation room, went to the Gantz sphere teleportation room, and teleported to the quiet room where he was training.

After that, Chen Chen was immersed in the training of Field ability.

One day, two days, three days...

About three days later, the red light on the intercom at the door of the quiet room was turned on again. Chen Chen opened his eyes as if he had noticed it and the lit button seemed to be automatically pressed down by an invisible finger. There was only one beep and the voice of Little X came:

“Sir Godfather, the human teleportation experiment has been carried out 3,000 times and item teleportation has been carried out thousands of times according to the varying shapes and natures, and all of them have been successful! The current teleportation success rate is 100%!”

“Has it been done three thousand times?”

Chen Chen only then cracked a smile. He stood up and walked out of the quiet room, then teleported back to the Spire through the Gantz sphere teleportation room, and then came to the Watchman teleportation room again.

This time, Chen Chen intended to try it himself and while he was at it, he took a look at the world at the other end of the teleporter.

This time, Little X did not stop him.

As Chen Chen stepped onto the teleportation platform, Little X reminded again, “Sir Godfather, I’m afraid you won’t be able to keep in touch with me when you get there but don’t worry, I’ve already assigned the third intelligence over there — X-113. She’ll be responsible for helping you manage Spire 2.”

“Got it. Get ready then.”

Chen Chen nodded with a blank face.

“Remote sensing exploration has been confirmed, ready to start teleportation...

“The target receiver is activated, ready to start teleportation...”

As X-111 droned on, in front of Chen Chen, there was also a stream of water gushing out from the inside, suspended in front of Chen Chen!

Seeing this, Chen Chen took a deep breath. He stretched out his hand and gently touched the stream of water. The next second, Chen Chen’s vision suddenly went dark!

“Rumble!”

At this moment, Chen Chen felt as if he was half in a dream and half-awake. He did not know how much time had passed. It seemed that all this took only a few seconds and it also seemed that several years had passed. In short, when Chen Chen started to regain consciousness, he realized that he was already lying on a cold floor.

Chen Chen stretched out his hand and touched the floor. Only then did he realize that he was lying on the teleportation platform.

“Thump, thump!”

Two sets of footsteps moved to Chen Chen and then Chen Chen only felt two people helping him up. Chen Chen looked to his side, only to see the wooden faces of two Black Knights.

“Sir Commander, hello. Welcome to the Mars Spire Experimental Base No. A002.”

A sexy and raspy female voice rang out. Chen Chen shook his head and then completely sobered up.

What he saw was a teleportation hall that was almost the same as the one on Earth. The only difference was that Chen Chen’s body felt light and floaty.

He felt like a person who had been carrying a load of dozens of kilograms since he was born. One day, he suddenly took off that load and a fluttery feeling of ecstasy filled his body.

It felt as if he could fly with every step he took...

This was what Chen Chen experienced now.

“Your physique is very good. Although you’ve been teleported across the planets, it didn’t produce side effects such as vomiting and dizziness.”

The sexy raspy voice continued. “Let me introduce myself, I’m X’s clone — number X-113. You can call me X-113. Of course, I prefer that you call me — Nancy.”

“Nansie?”

Chen Chen raised his head, only to see the face of a Western woman with blonde hair and dark eyes on a virtual screen above his head.

This was unlike the little-girl-like personality of Little X and the previous X-112. This newly split X-113 had a personality that was closer to a mature woman in her thirties and her automatically generated image was also the same.

“It’s Nancy with a C.”

At this time, X-113 smiled and said, “This is the name I gave myself after I was born. I hope you don’t mind...”

Chen Chen nodded. As the transmission of information was limited by the speed of light, Mars needed a unique intelligent system to personally control it but these intelligent avatars were controlled by Little X, so Chen Chen did not have to worry about these cloned intelligences being rebellious.

At this time, he looked around and asked, “How is the current construction of Spire 2?”

“The current construction progress is only 5%.”

Nancy replied, “With the existing manpower of Spire 2, it’ll take about half a year to complete the construction and that’s with sufficient materials.

“At present, a teleportation hall has been built and a material storage room next to it. This is in addition to two living rooms and a training room.”

The so-called training room was a place to train one’s muscles.

The gravity of Mars was only one-fifth that of the earth. If one stayed on Mars for a long time, it would cause the body’s muscles to shrink. This was inevitable. Only persistent exercise every day could alleviate this problem.

Of course, it could only be alleviated.

Chen Chen did not say much. He just tried to get used to everything and then said again, “Give me a protective suit. I’m going to the ground to have a look.”

“Understood, please follow this Black Knight.”

Nancy replied and then a Black Knight took the lead and walked toward the exit of the teleportation room with Chen Chen.

When Chen Chen stepped out of the door, he saw a wide metal passage and at the end of the passage was another gate. As Chen Chen walked to the gate, it opened automatically, revealing a 100-square-meter dressing room behind.

There were no wardrobes in the dressing room. Instead, there were rows of black automatic-dressing platforms.

This platform served to reduce the time it took to wear protective suits. Since it was developed, it had quickly spread throughout the entire Blacklight Biotechnology. Those experimenters entering and exiting were all automatically dressed, so that more than 20 minutes was saved each time, and efficiency was indirectly improved.

At this time, Chen Chen stood on the platform in a well-versed manner, aligning his feet with the footprints on the platform. In the next second, the mechanical arms quickly stretched out and began to install pieces of protective frameworks on Chen Chen’s body.

This sort of protective suit was an advanced protective suit with an exoskeleton. It was a secret piece of equipment specially customized by Blacklight Biotechnology for extraterrestrial activities. The value of a set was more than three million USD.

After a series of squeaking mechanical sounds, Chen Chen had finished putting it on after five minutes.

At this time, his body was encased in non-reflective pure black protective clothing. Pieces of ceramic armor plates were interlocked and connected to each other, presenting a cold sci-fi aesthetic.

The helmet of this protective suit was a slanted triangle, connected with the body contours, enough to withstand the impact of flying sand and rocks from all directions.

Unlike ordinary spacesuits, the spacesuit worn by Chen Chen did not have any visible lenses and only had a set of high-definition camera devices, whose footage was directly projected onto Chen Chen’s eyes through VR.

This kind of protective suit weighed 100kg and could reach up to 2 meters tall when worn. It also had a high-precision EMG signal sensor inside as well as a powerful motor drive system and a shock absorption device. There was also a built-in high-efficiency hydrogen battery on the back armor that could provide dozens of hours of power for the protective suit.

This was no longer a protective suit in the traditional sense. It was more like an exoskeleton armor used to strengthen soldiers’ combat capabilities.

The reason for adopting this rigorous design was to resist the sandstorm on Mars and... There was a very small probability that armed conflict might occur!

Feeling that this protective suit was somewhat similar to the one in the game Dead Space, Chen Chen nodded. Then, he walked off the dressing platform and continued to walk toward the door at the end of the dressing room.

At the end of the door, there were three elevators side by side.

At this time, the elevator door in the center was opening and Chen Chen walked in heavily, after which the elevator door closed automatically.

“Sir Commander, we’re currently at a depth of 1,200 meters underground. It’ll take about two minutes to get to the surface. Do you want to start now?”

Nancy’s voice came from the headset of the protective suit.

“Start!”

Chen Chen replied. In the next second, Chen Chen only felt a sudden jolt in the elevator, then the force of gravity hit him, and the screen on the elevator door in front of him showed a row of numbers that were rapidly decreasing!

“1200...”

“1100...”

“1000...”

...

The elevator rose rapidly at a speed of ten meters per second and within two minutes, the elevator jolted again and a special alarm sounded.

“Warning, warning. You’ve reached the surface, please check whether your protective suit is damaged. If there’s no damage, please press the green button on the right and the elevator door will open.”

Chen Chen did not hesitate and directly pressed the green button.

“Whoosh!”

As the elevator doors opened on both sides, a massive white air flow suddenly rushed in and Chen Chen was even blown back half a step by it.

In the next second, Chen Chen got used to the airflow. Only then did he understand that the airflow was a storm on the surface of Mars and the storm was also filled with a large number of dry ice pellets!

chapters.title: Chapter 529: Meteorite
chapters.body:
Chapter 529: Meteorite

Chapter 529: Meteorite

An endless white scene filled his eyes.

Chen Chen squinted his eyes and saw from here that the north pole on Mars was very different from the north pole on Earth.

In the center of the polar region, there was also a sprawling ice continent, but unlike on Earth, the north pole of Mars had a large number of strange rocky landforms. This type of landform was called “frost dunes”. From a high altitude, these dunes formed by ice and snow looked a bit like folds of ice cream.

There were also a large number of dark areas in the north pole. These dark areas were gaps formed after the gas trapped under the ice sheet was released. This made the Arctic landscape extremely complex and dangerous at the same time.

Moreover, the sun on Mars was bluish and smaller than seen from the earth, only about two-thirds the size of how it appeared on Earth.

The wind on Mars was also much stronger than that on Earth. Usually, the wind was around 6 or 7 on the Beaufort scale, and it could occasionally even form large sandstorms that spread across the planet.

At this time, as Chen Chen set foot in this Martian world, the first thing he saw was a few black monowheels parked outside the elevator.

These vehicles were made by several designers of Blacklight Biotechnology through modifying the Gantz Bike. The Gantz Bike, which was a special motorcycle in Gantz, had only one tire as a whole, but this tire had a very large size, with a diameter that could reach two meters and a width that could reach fifty centimeters.

This tire was hollow inside, so it could house a seat and corresponding cockpit. As the tire itself blocked the driver’s front line of sight directly, a display screen was needed so the driver could observe the front.

The driver would sit inside the entire tire and the front and rear of the tire also had gear levers and tail fins that provided stability. There was even a seat that could carry passengers at the rear.

Compared to ordinary motorcycles, the advantage was that it was more flexible and could adapt to more complex terrains. The disadvantage was that the difficulty of handling it was much higher than that of ordinary motorcycles and cars.

Still, for the Black Knights who had far greater reflexes than humans, these difficulties were nothing.

The huge and narrow one-wheel design allowed this vehicle to freely shuttle through the rugged rocks and pellet-scattered ground of Mars. Its maneuverability and speed were far beyond conventional off-road vehicles. Even if it hit an object, it was difficult to damage it. Even its energy use was far lower than ordinary vehicles.

Therefore, this kind of “unimpeded vehicle” modified from the Gantz Bike became a very advantageous vehicle on Mars. After the modification, those people gave this vehicle a name — “GB off-road vehicle”.

After testing, this kind of mono-wheel off-road vehicle could speed through Mars at a speed of 150 kilometers per hour, completely ignoring the complicated, shifting topographic structure of Mars. It could be described as a perfect fit.

At this time, Chen Chen picked one of the GB off-road vehicles and sat in it.

As Chen Chen sat in one of the off-road vehicles, the screen in front of him flashed and a line of words appeared on it:

“Detecting user...”

“Please place your palm on the screen.”

Chen Chen stretched out his hand and placed his palm on the screen. Suddenly, another line on the screen swept across.

“Identity confirmed: The driver has the highest authority. The vehicle has been synchronized with the driver’s protective suit.”

As this line of words flashed across the screen, the GB off-road vehicle quickly started and drove Chen Chen forward.

Amid the ice and snow, the black monowheel swiftly moved forward. A large wheel track appeared behind the vehicle and the monowheel rolled faster and soon traversed over a hundred kilometers.

Sitting in the monowheel, Chen Chen silently experienced this special sensation. This was a field that humans had never set foot in. If Chen Chen wanted to, he could even drive the vehicle away from the north pole and head to the equator of Mars.

When the Ares was orbiting Mars, several communication satellites had been scattered here and the satellites now directly covered the surface of Mars. Wherever the GB off-road vehicle went, there was no need to worry about getting lost.

At this time, Chen Chen drove the vehicle to the top of the highest snow-capped mountain nearby.

This snow-capped mountain range in the north pole was about 2,000 meters high. Standing here, Chen Chen could even see the distant horizon’s gradient turning from white to brown. The reason for this was that the north pole’s ice cover was not large. Just a few kilometers away was the most common desert terrain of Mars.

Looking at this primitive and ancient world, Chen Chen sighed silently. Who would have thought that he, who obtained the USB flash drive, would be on the famous Mars after a few years?

Chen Chen had always had distant, boundless ambitions because he knew that human beings would not be trapped on Earth forever. Although the earth was the mother who gave birth to humankind, she also restrained the pace of human civilization and a human civilization that did not transcend the Earth was forever fragile, liable to be destroyed by various disasters at any time —

Meteorites, volcanic eruptions, gamma-ray bursts, and even humans’ own nuclear wars — there were countless ways whereby the universe could destroy human civilization.

If human civilization wanted to endure, the only path was to go beyond the earth.

Now, the first step had been taken by Chen Chen.

These thoughts flashed through Chen Chen’s mind, but in the next moment, he saw a series of meteors passing above his head. There were as many as a hundred meteors, huddling together as they fell toward Mars.

The atmosphere of Mars was very thin, which meant that even a chunk the size of a fist would reach the ground. This was unlike the earth, where meteorites the size of a few houses would be reduced to dust in the atmosphere before they could even reach the ground.

However, just then, the voice of X-113, Nancy, suddenly came out of the headset. “Sir Commander, I’ve suddenly received bad news. The situation is very urgent.”

Chen Chen’s expression flickered. He looked up and said, “Speak!”

“Just ten seconds ago, the research station base in the south pole suddenly sent a distress signal to the earth, claiming that a meteorite fell over there and smashed into the research station. At present, a large amount of air is leaking out of the research station and the situation is very critical.”

As if she knew the crisis that the other party might be facing, Nancy’s voice also became serious. “Among the team at the south pole research station, two died due to the collapse of the building and seven went into hypoxic shock due to being exposed to the Martian air for an instant. The remaining three people have hidden in a laboratory that hasn’t collapsed, including our Alpha-level researcher Hannibal Caesar of Blacklight Biotechnology. Because this laboratory is used to study Martian life forms, it’s sufficiently airtight but it won’t last long.”

“How long will it last?”

Chen Chen frowned and asked.

“Because the research station was damaged, the power system was also destroyed. The oxygen production room is no longer able to produce oxygen. The research station has completely shut down. Based on the scale of the laboratory, after about ten hours, everyone would die of suffocation...”

“Ten hours?”

Chen Chen could not help pondering when he heard these words.

The speed of a GB off-road vehicle could reach a maximum of 200 kilometers per hour. However, this meant it could only travel 2,000 kilometers in ten hours, unable to transport any materials.

As the Ares spacecraft had already left Mars the day before yesterday due to the fear of being discovered by the Earth Federation, the idea of using the spacecraft to transport supplies was not viable.

Then, there was only one way...

Thinking of this, Chen Chen looked at the oxygen-carrying capacity of his protective suit but saw that the oxygen in the protective suit could only last for three hours, so he immediately said, “Prepare three sets of protective suits for me. I’ll go to those three people myself.”

After he spoke, before Nancy could answer, he spread his arms directly, and his whole person rose into the air.

Immediately, he chose a direction and at the same time, he flew in the direction from which he came!

Only two minutes later, he returned to where Spire 2 was located. At this time, three Black Knights walked out of the elevator and each of them carried a dark medium-sized suitcase in their hands.

“Sir Commander, this is the free-size protective suit. Anyone within a height of 1.6 to 1.9 meters could fit in snugly. Are you going to go there yourself now?”

“Of course, Hannibal is there after all. I don’t want to lose such a unique talent.”

Chen Chen replied indifferently. With that, he looked at the three suitcases and they automatically lined up in a row in the next second. After that, Chen Chen spread his arms again. This time, he lifted together with the three suitcases.

“Boom!”

There was a muffled sonic boom. Chen Chen and the three suitcases suddenly turned into a small dot that flew toward the distance...

Although the speed of sound on Mars was only 240 meters per second, because the atmosphere was so thin, Chen Chen could easily accelerate to 1000 meters per second. After reaching this speed, Chen Chen even continued to accelerate. He used Field to form a cone-shaped shield before him. He was practically like a drill, flying toward the distance at an extremely terrifying speed!

1000 meters per second...

1500 meters per second...

3000 meters per second!

The speed detector on the armor was almost constantly ticking over as if it was a stopwatch and it soon reached a speed of 3000 meters per second. Upon conversion, this was equivalent to 10,800 kilometers per hour.

This was an extremely terrifying speed. Except for ballistic missiles and certain supersonic vehicles, almost no one could compete with this on the entire Earth.

At this speed, Chen Chen also began to feel the strain. Although Mars had a thin atmosphere, he was also carrying a load of 300 kilograms. If he were flying alone, Chen Chen could continue to accelerate himself to more than twice this speed!

At this speed, Chen Chen felt that he had reached a critical limit and the Field energy in his mind was slowly being consumed. At this rate of consumption, after about two and a half hours, Chen Chen would start to feel the burden.

“This speed should do...”

Chen Chen murmured. He had calculated the appropriate speed. After all, Mars had a diameter of 6,790 kilometers and a circumference of 21,331 kilometers. That was to say, at this speed, Chen Chen could travel to the south pole research station from the north pole in just one hour.

It would take one hour to get to the south pole research station and one hour to return. After he brought three sets of protective suits to the three survivors, it would be best if the three of them could repair the oxygen generator, but if it could not be repaired, then he could only take one person back to the Arctic base.

This was because he did not have the ability to carry three people and still fly at this speed.

“Boom!”

If there were people on the ground of Mars at this time, they would only see a figure flying at an extremely exaggerated speed from one end of the sky to the other, and then disappearing into the horizon!

At this extremely terrifying speed, in just over fifty minutes, Chen Chen had flown over the Martian continent and began to see layers of hoarfrost on the ground again. He had already flown from the north pole to the south pole boundary. The research station in the distance that was constantly emitting smoke was also faintly visible.

Seeing this, Chen Chen began to decelerate. In about half a minute, his speed dropped below the speed of sound on Mars. At this time, the scientific research station was already close at hand.

Around the research station, he could see large and small deep craters. These deep pits had stripes blooming outward on the ice. These meteorites had hit the ground in an oblique direction and one of the meteorites happened to streak past the research station, so most of the research station was blasted into pieces!

Seen from the sky, the circular scientific research station was like a birthday cake. One side of it had been mashed into crumbs by a little brat and only a small part of it was still upright.

One must admit that this group of scientific researchers was unlucky. They were bombarded by a meteorite in just a few months on Mars. With this sort of probability, one could try buying a lottery ticket.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen landed from the air. He landed directly in the ruins of the scientific research station and said at the same time, “Nancy, can you get through to the survivors?”

“Yes.”

X-113’s voice came from the headset and then another voice came, filled with astonishment. “What, you said someone has come to rescue me?”

That voice belonged to Hannibal.

“I’m right outside the door.”

Chen Chen said, “Hannibal, if you open the airtight door of the laboratory, I’ll stuff in three sets of protective suits.”

“You, you’re... Boss?”

Hannibal’s voice was a little flabbergasted. “This, how is this possible...”

“Stop wasting time.”

Chen Chen frowned. “If you don’t want me to abandon you here, open the door now and take the protective suits.”

“Yes!”

Hannibal said repeatedly and at the same time, a steel airtight door in front of Chen Chen began to hiss, and the bolts on it turned gradually.

Soon, a gap was opened in the airtight door and Chen Chen immediately stuffed in a protective suit box.

However, after only one box was inserted, the airtight door closed again.

“What’s going on?”

Chen Chen frowned and asked.

“Boss, the remaining two survivors are dead...”

Hannibal whispered, “I killed them.”

Chen Chen suddenly understood when he heard these words. He took a deep look at the airtight door in front of him and said at the same time, “I see. Put on your protective suit first, then come out. I’ll take you away.”

“Yes!”

Following that, there was a rustling sound from within.

Chen Chen looked toward the ruins. In the ruins, the bodies of the other researchers could be seen. Most of these researchers died of suffocation instantly because of the destruction of the research station.

The culprit of all this was a car-sized meteorite in the distance.

The meteorite fell to the ground at an inclination of seventy or eighty degrees, then broke through the scientific research station, and finally left a trail of more than 300 meters, sketching a chilling track across the surface.

Looking at the dark meteorite embedded under the ice at the end of the track, Chen Chen seemed to see something wriggling.

He frowned and flew directly to the meteorite. Only then did he see wisps of blackness flowing out of the gap in the meteorite.

It looked like a type of viscous liquid...

chapters.title: Chapter 530: Dream
chapters.body:
Chapter 530: Dream

Chapter 530: Dream

Liquid?

Looking at the sticky goo slowly flowing out of the meteorite, Chen Chen could not help but tilt his head.

This liquid was very viscous and looked to be black. It seeped out directly from the crevices of the meteorite, but when it came into contact with the outside world, it froze, forming a dark blue crystal.

Chen Chen scratched his head. He picked up a small stick from the ruins on the side and used it to chip at the meteorite.

“Snap!”

A piece of meteorite fragment was hacked off and a volume of liquid spurted out. Before it hit Chen Chen, it was automatically blocked by an invisible barrier.

This liquid solidified at once and turned into blue ice crystals.

Chen Chen’s Field energy could zoom into the substance, magnifying it by more than a thousand times. Under such subtle observation, bacteria and other microorganisms could be seen clearly, but he did not see any microbial substances in this liquid.

In other words, the material in the meteorite was originally a crystal, but it melted after entering the atmosphere and became a liquid.

However, as to what exactly this substance was, Chen Chen had no idea. Still, using the method of elimination, he first discounted the possibility that it was water.

To know what it was, Chen Chen’s Field energy had to attain one hundred thousand times of magnification at the subatomic-level before he could observe the molecular structure.

At this time, Chen Chen turned his head and looked behind him, only to see that Hannibal had put on his protective suit and walked out from the airtight door.

“Boss, why are you on Mars?”

Hannibal asked through the communications system. Although Chen Chen could not see his expression, Hannibal must be quite puzzled at this time. “I thought the rescue would only arrive in seven days at the earliest. Could it be that our Blacklight Biotechnology has already developed technology that takes an hour to reach Mars from the earth?”

“You’re just lucky.”

Chen Chen said casually, walking into the laboratory at the same time, only to see a small pool of blood that had been licked on the ground in the laboratory. At the same time, there were two male corpses piled up in the corner. The eyes of the two men were wide open, with a look of horror and confusion on their faces as if wondering why Hannibal had to suddenly, painfully become a killer.

“In the past, I probably wouldn’t have the guts...”

Hannibal followed Chen Chen and said a little reticently, “But after I did so many human experiments in the Spire Lab, I just...”

“You don’t have to explain. It’s understandable.”

Chen Chen waved his hand and then looked toward an experimental platform, only to see two large tanks of blood placed on the experimental platform. There was a stripped wire on the side.

“Were you going to generate oxygen by electrolyzing water?”

Seeing this, Chen Chen asked immediately.

“Yes.”

Hannibal nodded. “An adult has about 5 liters of blood and two people have 10 liters, which is equivalent to water of the same weight. The mass fraction of oxygen in water is 88.89%, which means that a total of 8.9 kg of oxygen can be electrolyzed from the blood of two people.

“An adult only needs 0.75 kg of oxygen a day.

“In addition, the bodies of the two people also contain a lot of water. If they’re crushed and placed in a centrifuge for separation, an additional 10 to 20 liters of water can be collected. This water and the crushed corpses would be perfectly capable of ensuring my subsequent survival.

“Therefore, after killing both of them, I can survive in the laboratory for more than ten days, until the Ares spacecraft comes to my rescue.”

Chen Chen could not help but glance at Hannibal when he heard the meticulous explanation from the other man. He had to say that this young man had a calmness beyond ordinary people. Even in such a desperate situation, he could find a trace of life in the cracks.

As for Hannibal’s approach, Chen Chen did not have any opinions. If Hannibal did not kill them, the oxygen in the laboratory could only last for ten hours at most and all three of them would die after that.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen suddenly took out a test tube from the experimental rack on the side, then walked out of the laboratory to the meteorite, and filled it with a few crystals.

“Boss, what is this...”

Hannibal asked, puzzled.

“I don’t know, so I’m taking it back to study it.”

Chen Chen said casually. After that, under his stare, a few cans of alcohol in the warehouse flew out automatically and poured down over the remains of the scientific research station.

The freezing point of alcohol was negative 117.3 degrees, so it was still in a liquid state at this time. After pouring the alcohol on the research station, Chen Chen waved again. An igniter in the laboratory ignited automatically and flew onto the alcohol at the same time.

“Whoosh!”

Suddenly, the ruins of the research station burst into a huge blazing fire!

Hannibal looked at this miraculous scene with a shocked expression.

However, Chen Chen did not bother to explain at this time. He grabbed Hannibal’s shoulder with one hand and commanded, “Hold on to me.”

Hannibal grabbed Chen Chen’s arm somewhat reservedly without knowing what was going on, but before he could ask anything, he suddenly realized that he had risen into the air!

“Huh? This...”

“Just bear with it, our protective suits can only maintain oxygen for two or three hours...”

Chen Chen spoke, then he slowly accelerated and flew in the direction from which he came.

As Hannibal had not been injected with the T-virus, Chen Chen could not directly accelerate to the speed of sound and could only accelerate gradually. Even so, after ten seconds, he still reached the speed of sound!

“Boom!”

A wave of air was generated behind the two of them, but this was just the beginning. Chen Chen was still accelerating to the limit that Hannibal’s body could bear!

“Boom!”

“Boom!”

“Boom!!!”

Double, triple, quadruple... Ten minutes later, Chen Chen had accelerated to ten times the speed of sound. At this extremely frightening speed, the two once again swept across the Martian continent and flew from the south pole to the north pole!

One and a half hours later, when Chen Chen’s oxygen was low, the two finally returned to the Spire 2 at the north pole of Mars.

As soon as he landed, Hannibal sprawled directly on the ground and vomited violently!

It was a pity that he threw up in his helmet...

After asking the Black Knight to take Hannibal away, Chen Chen returned to the teleportation room and took off his protective suit.

“Sir Commander, it seems that you’ve harvested something?”

Nancy could not help asking when she saw the test tube in Chen Chen’s hand.

At this time, once it was exposed to heat, the crystals in the test tube melted into a thick dark blue liquid again. If one did not look closely, it would look black.

“Take it and analyze it. Remember to send me the experiment report.”

Chen Chen handed the sealed test tube to a Black Knight, rubbed his head which was aching due to excessive use of Field energy, and then stood on the teleportation platform again.

Immediately, a stream of water gushed from the center of the platform and hovered in front of Chen Chen.

This time, Chen Chen was more used to the process. He stretched out his hand and touched the stream of water. Then, he only felt his vision turn dark and he was suspended in that sensation between dreaming and awakening.

When Chen Chen regained consciousness again, he was already lying on the teleporter in the Spire Lab. Little X on the side hurriedly came up. She helped Chen Chen up and at the same time said with concern, “Sir Godfather, how do you feel?”

“I’m fine.”

Chen Chen waved his hand. “I think you must’ve received the news from Mars, right?”

“Yes, I first received a distress signal from Mars three hours ago and then half an hour later, I received a message from X-113. She said that you’ve personally gone to the rescue.”

Little X said, “The scale of the meteor swarm this time is very large. According to the information I’ve received from all over the world, this group of comets has only flown into the solar system from the Kuiper Belt in recent years and the breadth of their area is very huge. Not only does it cover Mars but even the earth’s orbit is within its range. After about a day, comets will hit the earth and the impact area includes the entire continent of Europe, China, North Sea, and the rest of the Eastern Hemisphere.”

“Does it pose a threat to human civilization?”

Chen Chen asked.

“You can be rest assured that these comets aren’t large and will only form a vast meteor shower at that time. Only one or two of them will fall to the ground.”

Little X shook her head. “At least two or three similar situations have occurred in the last 100 years. Even if there are losses, they’ll just be sporadic casualties.”

Chen Chen nodded.

At present, with human technology, it was not possible to accurately calculate the distribution of the dust particles from the meteor shower’s parent comets. Therefore, it was impossible to accurately calculate the occurrence and scope of the meteor shower. At most, only a rough forecast could be made.

After communicating with Little X for a while, Chen Chen only felt that his head was groggy. He did not know whether it was a side effect of cross-planetary teleportation or overuse of Field power, so he returned to his room and went to rest early.

...

“Patter, patter, patter...”

At some point, Chen Chen found himself walking on a bustling street.

It was nighttime. The sky was dark, but the road was filled by an unending stream of cars and the streets were lined with colorful shops.

There were all kinds of stores including hotels, restaurants, merchandise stores, clothing stores, and many other stores — even Chen Chen did not know what they sold.

The neon lights on the shops were colorful, dotting the entire city with various gorgeous hues.

Strictly speaking, this was indeed a bustling street.

Listening to the sound of his own footsteps constantly echoing, Chen Chen kept feeling something was wrong for some reason.

He could not tell what was wrong with this street, but there was an unusual feeling of uneasiness that kept gripping him. Waves of this feeling swept through his perception and made him feel uneasy.

What was wrong exactly...

Chen Chen was rather suspicious. With his brows furrowed, he kept walking down this road.

As he walked, Chen Chen felt a little thirsty, so he subconsciously opened the door to the hotel on the side.

The lights in the hotel were bright and dazzling crystal chandeliers were hanging high up in the hall. The bright lights continued to stimulate Chen Chen’s nerves and the entire hall was as bright as daylight.

At the same time, a scorching sensation swirled out of the room. Although it was winter, the temperature in the room was very high. It seemed to be more than 30 degrees.

Was the heating in the hotel so potent?

With a soft mutter, Chen Chen walked in slowly.

“Patter, patter, patter...”

After hearing his own footsteps again, Chen Chen suddenly froze.

He finally discerned what was wrong...

He stopped abruptly and paused in the empty hotel lobby.

At this time, he was astounded to find that there was not a single person in the lobby of the huge hotel!

Something was wrong, very wrong...

Chen Chen’s brows were twisted into a small knot. He squinted his eyes and looked around, but he still could not see a single human figure. The people in the hotel seemed to have evaporated.

Seeing this, his wary nature told him not to stay here. He retreated out of the hotel step by step and then opened the door again.

Outside the door, there was still a lot of heavy traffic.

However, by now, Chen Chen had thoroughly discovered the problem, that was, there was no pedestrian on the street except for himself.

This was the source of his uneasy feeling from before. Even in the middle of the night, there was not even one person on the entire street...

Thinking of this, Chen Chen turned his head subconsciously, looking at the passing cars, and then his pupils shrank!

Those vehicles were empty and even the driver’s seat was empty!

In other words, was he the only person on the entire street?

Chen Chen inhaled sharply. He sensed, for the first time, that this bustling street was filled with an unparalleled eeriness...

As uneasiness washed over him as Chen Chen looked around with alertness. He wanted to know why there was no one in the entire world.

Still, as soon as he raised his head, he saw that, on the upper floor of the residence across the street, several households were glowing with human silhouettes.

Chen Chen fixed his gaze on them intently and then saw that these people were standing at the window as if they were observing something in front of the window...

No, it was not just a few windows. As Chen Chen took a closer look, he noticed that except for some dark windows, a person was standing in front of each window that had a light on!

Those people were watching... Him!!!

In the instant he understood all of this, Chen Chen felt a chill crept up his spine. He could not describe this feeling. Although these people had their backs to the light, so he could only make out their silhouettes, Chen Chen was gripped by an intuition — these people were truly all staring unwaveringly at him...

“Swish!”

Chen Chen suddenly opened his eyes and sat up from the Blacklight Fully-automated Medical Platform, gasping for breath.

Only then did Chen Chen notice a coolness on his back. After he reached out and touched his back, he realized that cold sweat had soaked his back without his realizing it when he was dreaming earlier.

“Sir Godfather?”

Little X’s voice came. “Did you have a bad dream?”

“Dream?”

Chen Chen looked around blankly. He stared at the room and furniture he was familiar with, and then heaved a sigh of relief.

It turned out to be a dream.

Chen Chen stepped off the fully automated medical platform and walked barefoot on the soft carpet, only feeling the warmth in the room.

It turned out that the warmth of the hotel in the dream had come from reality?

Chen Chen looked at the remote control of the central air conditioner in doubt but saw that the temperature on the remote control was only 24 degrees, which was not as hot as the lobby of the hotel in his dream.

Perhaps it was an illusion.

Chen Chen shook his head and stopped thinking about all this.

“Oh right, Sir Godfather.”

Little X’s voice came again. “X-113 sent an analysis report. The subject of the report is an ionic crystal substance that melts in a warm environment. However, according to the tests, this substance seems to be a kind of cell protoplasm, that is to say, it’s a type of biological fluid.”

“What, biological fluid?”

Chen Chen instantly looked stunned when he heard this.

chapters.title: Chapter 531: Han Zizhe
chapters.body:
Chapter 531: Han Zizhe

Chapter 531: Han Zizhe

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

Protoplasm was the general term for living substances in cells and its main components were a mixture of carbohydrates, proteins, nucleic acids, lipids, and so on.

At this time, the crystalline substance Chen Chen had obtained was a kind of biological protoplasm, namely, the sap that flowed out after the cell was damaged.

“A biological protoplasm?”

At this point, Chen Chen could not help frowning. Little X handed Chen Chen a thick analysis report. These were the protoplasm test reports.

Chen Chen opened it solemnly and bent over it. It took more than ten minutes for him to go through it.

With his current brainpower, a page of information could be completely printed in his mind in almost two or three seconds. This information made Chen Chen take such a serious look, so it had aroused his vigilance.

“Sure enough, it’s not an Earth life form...”

After reading this report, Chen Chen murmured.

In the report, the components in the biological fluid had been analyzed. Most of them were water, which accounted for 90% of the fluid. The remaining substances contained very complex nucleic acid chains, which were the DNA of this organism.

This DNA was completely different from the living things on Earth, but it was very simple.

Yes, simple.

Compared with the life forms that existed on the earth, the form of this creature would be very simple, just like the most primitive ancient creature on the earth. The DNA that Chen Chen saw at this time had a complexity that was not even a hundredth that of higher animals. Needless to say, it was not on par with human beings at all.

Still, what was intriguing was that, according to the genetic expression, this organism was likely to have round-shaped nerves and a composite cell structure. This structure meant that its body and nerves were completely separated. Even if the body died, its nervous system could survive alone and then regenerate.

This was an almost immortal monster.

“What a magical creature...”

Seeing this, Chen Chen could not help frowning. He paced back and forth in the room. “There have been two similar creatures on the earth, one called Helcionelloida and the other called Acipenser Sinensis Gray. If these ancient life forms were still alive, maybe their form would have something in common with this kind of creature, right?”

With that, Chen Chen turned around and closed the file. Now, the contents of this file had been engraved into his mind, but besides that, he had not learned much.

“Can we try to replicate this creature?”

Little X asked, “Using directional differentiation technology to extract the intact cells inside to induce stem cells and then transfer the nuclei into artificially cultivated egg cells to form embryos?”

“There’s no need for that.”

Chen Chen shook his head. “The probability of success isn’t high. After all, we don’t even have a complete sample of the cell structure of the creature and we don’t know the environment and temperature that this organism would thrive in. The chances of replication aren’t high.”

Still, Little X continued. “By the way, Sir Godfather, it’s three o’clock in the afternoon in Namibia. According to the time calculation, the first batch of meteorites has fallen on the earth.”

“Oh?”

Chen Chen frowned upon hearing this, “So, it’s happening now?”

He did not know why, but he suddenly thought of the weird dream he had just now. He did not know whether that dream was related to these meteorites.

Thus, he asked again, “Is the quantum computer Adam reacting?”

“No, everything is normal at the moment.”

“Then keep an eye on things for the time being. It’d be best if we could retrieve a batch of the fallen meteorites and see if there are new clues in those meteorites...”

“Got it.”

...

At this time, far away on the other side of the earth, it had entered nighttime.

In some places, astronomy enthusiasts began to set up their telescopes. In accordance with the notices given by the news, many people came to the suburbs where they thought there would be less light pollution, waiting for the arrival of the meteor shower.

“Zhang Qi, slow down...”

At a mountain on the outskirts of Shangdu, a car parked on the side of the road near the peak. Then, a fashionably dressed girl got out of the car, huffing angrily, and walked toward the side of the road without thinking.

While the girl got out of the car, a thin young man quickly opened the door from the driver’s seat and ran out of the car, somewhat helplessly chasing after the girl.

“Zhang Qi, slow down, it’s not safe here in the wilderness!”

The young man was a little powerless. He had a bowl-cut, which was the most popular hairstyle on Kuaishou1. Although it was winter, he wore very thin clothes. His jacket was a down jacket, but it was left unzipped and flapping wide open, revealing a small tight-fitting T-shirt inside.

The lower half of his body was clad in very skinny trousers and his feet were shod in a pair of yellow loafers, without socks.

Dressed in all this, the young man chased in the direction of the girl with small steps.

“Zhang Qi, Zhang Qi!”

He kept calling her name. When he grabbed her arm, the girl immediately flung him aside and shouted, “Han Zizhe, don’t touch me. Why don’t you go talk to your female confidantes!”

Hearing these words, the youth suddenly showed a painful expression. He choked out. “Oh, it’s my fault? And didn’t you get acquainted with three random ‘brothers’ on the Internet? Going “dear, muaks muaks” all day long, flirting so heavily, yet did I say anything?”

“I’m a girl, so what if I know a few brothers?”

The girl became even more worked up. “Can’t you let me do that? Just because I know a few brothers, you have to start flirting with another girl?”

“Since when?”

The youth inquired.

“I saw your phone, there’s that ‘Night Vibes’ chatting app. It says ‘meet in one swipe’, humph, I hope you’ll meet a ghost in one swipe!”

The girl huffed in fury and then she made to walk away.

“Alright, I’ve deleted that app, surely that’ll do?”

The young man finally compromised. He said helplessly, “But I have a request, you must block those friends on QQ. We’ll both cut them off, okay?”

“No way!”

The girl shook her head with an extremely determined attitude. “I finally got to know a few brothers after so much effort. Why should I block them for your sake?”

“You!”

Seeing this, the young man gritted his teeth. “Are you going to be reasonable!”

“I’m only dating you, it doesn’t mean I have to be reasonable.”

The girl did not back down. She pointed with one finger at the sky. “Originally I said that I’d watch the meteor shower with you. Now the meteors are here, go and watch it yourself!”

The young man involuntarily looked into the sky after hearing these words. Sure enough, several streams of light had appeared in the sky at some point. These glowing streaks continued to fall and then quickly disappeared.

When he saw this, for some reason. the youth felt a hint of disappointment...

Just when he was feeling discouraged and was preparing to say something final, he suddenly saw that one of the streams of light had not been extinguished like the rest, but instead became bigger and brighter.

“Huh?”

Both the youth and the girl were a little puzzled. They blinked, only to find that the light seemed to be streaking toward them.

The speed of the meteor was so fast. In less than a few seconds, the streak of light flew over the heads of the two with a whistle and then they only heard a “bang”, and a blazing light burst out from the top of the mountain!

The meteor had fallen?

Seeing this scene, the two looked at each other in unison. Both of them noticed the shocked expressions in the other’s eyes. Then, the young man ignored the girl and he ran toward the site of impact without thinking!

As an astronomy enthusiast, he knew that ordinary people would never witness this kind of scene, not even once in their lives. How could he pass up this opportunity?

“Han Zizhe, Han Zizhe!”

Behind him, the girl shouted anxiously, but the young man still did not stop because, in his opinion, the blaze of fire was not far, only about three to four hundred meters away. This distance was short enough for him to be the first to reach the scene.

Sure enough, the young man only ran for two or three minutes before he reached the place where the meteor had fallen. He first smelled a pungent burnt stench, but in this smell, there was a barbecue-like aroma.

“Huff, huff!”

He ran to the front in one breath and finally saw a shallow pit, half a meter in diameter, in the ground. Even the soil in the pit was being scorched!

There was blue smoke in the pit and in the center of the crater, there was a fist-sized, irregular-shaped meteorite quietly resting there.

Illuminated by the light of his mobile phone, the meteorite was spindle-shaped and its color was pure black. He did not know if it was burned black when it fell into the atmosphere or if that was the original color. Other than that, he could not make out anything else.

About four or five minutes later, the girl named Zhang Qi finally rushed over. She looked at the young man by the crater. “Han Zizhe, I called you so many times, why did you pretend not to hear me?”

The youth looked back at the girl and replied indifferently, “I thought you weren’t going to watch the meteors with me anymore, then why are you calling for me? Just go by yourself.”

“So, you just left me there alone?”

The girl asked incredulously, “What happens if I’m in danger?”

“Heh heh.”

The young man sneered and did not answer. He ignored the girl’s nattering. He saw that there was no more smoke in the crater and it had probably cooled down. Then, he stepped into the crater and gingerly reached out toward the meteorite.

“Han Zizhe, I’ll have this meteorite!”

However, before he touched the meteorite, there was a sudden burst of footsteps and the girl went straight past him and took the meteorite before his eyes.

“You!”

The young man frowned. “This meteorite is worth nothing. What will you do with it? You know I’m an astronomy enthusiast, so you want to snatch what’s mine?”

“What’s yours? This thing dropped from the sky!”

The girl instantly yelled defiantly, but at this moment, she felt a stream of heat flowing to the back of her hand and arm.

She reached out, only to realize that her hand was full of a sticky substance by now.

“What the h*ll?”

As it was dark, neither of them could see exactly what was on the meteorite. The girl moved her phone to the meteorite, only to see a lot of liquid leaking out of the meteorite like black oil. Some of the liquid had permeated the ground as well.

She turned her head and looked at the meteorite, but saw that there were a few small cracks in the meteorite. This liquid was flowing out of these cracks.

“Ugh, how disgusting!”

The girl hurriedly threw the meteorite on the ground, and then quickly took out the paper towel from her handbag and constantly wiped her hands.

“See? I told you not to touch this thing, but you just wouldn’t listen.”

The young man snorted, then he picked up the meteorite at once, turned, and walked down the mountain.

“Where are you going?”

The girl hurriedly shouted.

“Let’s go, I’ll take you home!”

...

After a twisting drive, by the time Han Zizhe had sent Zhang Qi to the door of her house, the two had reconciled as before.

When the girl had left the car, Han Zizhe turned his head again to look at the meteorite in the front passenger seat.

At this time, the meteorite had soaked the entire underside of the front passenger seat. He did not know how much liquid was in it, but it was not drained yet.

Could it be that this meteorite was a piece of meteoric ice?

Han Zizhe shook his head. He stepped on the accelerator and the vehicle returned to his rented residence.

He wrapped the meteorite in a piece of paper and moved the meteorite back to his home. Then, Han Zizhe excitedly turned on the light and looked at the meteorite carefully.

This meteorite was not big, only slightly larger than an ordinary person’s fist, and it was all covered with cracks. Up to this point, there was still a trace of liquid seeping out, which looked dark and incomparably sticky.

“It doesn’t look like water. Surely this thing isn’t radioactive...”

Seeing this, the young man suddenly became a little nervous. He looked at his hand. This liquid did not seem to be anything special and was easily washed away with water. It was not as difficult to clean as oil.

Thinking of this, he leaned forward toward the meteorite again and sniffed it lightly.

Immediately, he noticed that there was an odd smell from the meteorite, besides the burnt smell and barbecue aroma.

The burnt smell and barbecue aroma came from the outer shell of the meteorite, but this odd smell came from the liquid within.

“F*ck, it’s quite disgusting...:

Han Zizhe murmured. He had not heard of any meteorite that turned out to be smelly.

Thinking of this, he quickly turned on the computer, logged in to the “Meteorite Home” website, about to make a post on the forum to see what this meteorite was.

However, he had just opened the webpage when he saw that there were already several posts published there —

[Newbie here, would like to ask you guys what kind of meteorite this is classified as? Is it an iron meteorite or a stone meteorite?]

[I just bought this from a layman, now I present it to you. It fell during the meteor shower tonight...]

[The scale of the meteor shower tonight is so big, I didn’t expect that quite a few of you would have obtained a meteorite?]

There were pictures under the titles of these posts and the pictures were all the same. They were not very different from the meteorite he got. Some of them still had sticky liquid flowing out of them, which looked a bit disgusting.

Han Zizhe casually clicked into a post, and sure enough, the replies below were complaining about these questions.

[F*ck, what the h*ll is this, it’s not like a meteorite at all, okay? Why is there goo on it?]

[Hey, it’s real. The shell has the burnt marks formed when it passed through the atmosphere. It doesn’t look fake. As for the oozing liquid, probably it’s because the meteorite contains ice?]

[Will this liquid cause any problems? I advise the OP to go to the hospital for a checkup tomorrow.]

[So gross...]

[OP, I’m from the relevant department. This meteorite of yours belongs to the country. Please hand it over to the government tomorrow and claim your reward of a small red flag and 50,000 yuan.]

chapters.title: Chapter 532: A Peculiar Case
chapters.body:
Chapter 532: A Peculiar Case

Chapter 532: A Peculiar Case

First of all, more than one piece of this kind of meteorite fell and there were several pieces on the “Meteorite Home” forum alone.

Secondly, this kind of meteorite had a common feature, which was it contained liquid inside. Once it fell to the ground, the liquid would flow out from the cracks in the meteorite.

Finally, the liquid in these meteorites had a fishy smell, reminding people of animal body fluids.

“Forget it, I’ll stop thinking about this. I’ll keep it for now.”

Han Zizhe shook his head. He placed the meteorite on the coffee table, washed up, and then lay on the bed and played with his cell phone.

Unwittingly, he slowly fell asleep.

“Squeak squeak...”

When Han Zizhe was falling asleep, a special sound came from this meteorite, and then suddenly more liquid flowed out of it. This liquid flowed out across the coffee table little by little as if it was alive and then flowed in the direction where Han Zizhe was sleeping...

Han Zizhe had a special dream.

In his dream, he walked alone on a dark street. It seemed that because it was late at night and there was no one anywhere in the street, only a few cars driving on the road.

A night wind blew past and the empty streets seemed filled with odd loneliness.

‘Where am I...’

Han Zizhe had some doubts but this kind of doubt only lasted for a moment before dissipating again. Human beings rarely doubted the logic in their dreams. This was a subconscious evasion.

Why, why was there no one on the street?

Looking at the empty street, Han Zizhe suddenly became a little confused and at the same time, an inexplicable feeling came into his heart.

This was a feeling of emptiness and a special desire.

Han Zizhe took a deep breath but felt that the loneliness was getting heavier and that he was going crazy from the silence of the street devoid of people.

How he wished he could be with people, sticking to them, melding as one, fusing flesh and blood, connecting vessels...

Suddenly, there was this feeling deep in his heart that Han Zizhe was at once startled by his thoughts in this empty and quiet street.

This scare woke him up.

Han Zizhe opened his eyes abruptly, only to realize that it was already the next day. Dazzling sunlight shone in from the window, completely dispelling the deep desire from the dream.

“In my dream, why did I have that idea?”

Immediately after waking up, Han Zizhe frowned. “Surely I’m not becoming a pervert?”

Thinking of this, Han Zizhe could not help but shiver. He only felt that his stomach was empty, racked by the unique hunger pangs of the morning.

He stopped thinking about that unique dream but looked at his watch and saw that it was almost twelve o’clock, so he got up and went out quickly.

Han Zizhe just graduated from university this year, but he had not found a job. He had only one job at present, which was to receive the allowance given by his parents, and eat, and wait to die.

Still, fortunately, his family was pretty well off. Even if he did not go to work, he would not starve to death.

Driving the car that his parents paid for, Han Zizhe first sent a WeChat message to his girlfriend. Seeing that she did not reply, he drove to his favorite restaurant and ordered a barbecue meal.

On the TV in the restaurant, the noon news was broadcasting about the meteor shower last night.

According to the news, the meteor shower last night was a large-scale meteor shower that was rarely seen even in a century. There had been more than one million meteors descending to the earth, but most of the meteors that were not large enough were burned up in the atmosphere, and only about one percent of the meteorites had fallen on Earth.

One of the most sensational ones was a giant meteorite the size of a house that fell in Mumbai, India, but fortunately, it fell on the outskirts of Mumbai without causing any casualties.

Seeing this, Han Zizhe also paid attention, wanting to hear about these meteorites, especially whether there was liquid in those meteorites. However, in the next second, the program turned to other news, and coverage of the meteorites was quickly over.

Seeing this, Han Zizhe lowered his head again. He simply opened the browser by himself and started searching for news about meteorites.

It was just that most media on the Internet was self-produced content that only talked nonsense. After reading a few articles, he lost all interest to continue reading.

After lunch, his girlfriend still did not reply, so he made a call.

Soon, Zhang Qi’s blurry voice came on the phone.

“You haven’t got up yet, little sleepyhead?”

Han Zizhe secretly breathed a sigh of relief as he teased her. “Would you like me to pick you up now? How about we go to a movie today?

“Okay, you wash up first, I’ll come right away.”

Hearing an affirmative reply from his girlfriend, he did not continue to stay in the restaurant, but paid the bill and drove in the direction of his girlfriend’s house.

An hour later, he waited for his girlfriend Zhang Qi outside the community area.

The moment when he saw his girlfriend Zhang Qi, Han Zizhe suddenly felt his mouth becoming a little dry. At this moment, he suddenly had the urge to become one with his girlfriend.

He pursed his mouth. He did not understand why this feeling suddenly appeared. Is it because he had been holding back for too long recently?

That could not be it...

Little did he know, the moment his girlfriend Zhang Qi saw him, she also froze for a moment, and then her expression returned to normal again.

“Why did you get up so late today?”

Seeing his girlfriend getting in the car, Han Zizhe quickly shifted his thoughts and asked casually, “I thought I had gotten up late enough today, but I didn’t expect you to not be up even by one in the afternoon. Isn’t that too much?”

“Isn’t it because I accompanied you to watch the meteor shower late last night?”

His girlfriend curled her lips, then rolled her eyes. She took Han Zizhe’s hand and said, “By the way, I had a bad nightmare last night!”

“Nightmare?”

The strange dream from last night flashed across Han Zizhe’s mind, after which he smiled. “What sort of nightmare?”

“I dreamed that I was walking in an empty city and I couldn’t find anyone anywhere...”

His girlfriend said mock-seriously, “I also saw a lot of people staring at me, they almost scared me to death!”

“So, were there no people or many people?”

Han Zizhe raised his eyebrows and started the car. “You first said that no one was to be found and then later that there were many people looking at you. Did you dream of people or not?”

“I’m not sure too...”

His girlfriend frowned suddenly, seeming to be very confused. “It feels like I didn’t see anyone and also that there was a group of people watching me from the distance, and I very much wanted those people to approach me...”

“And then?”

Hearing this, Han Zizhe’s heart suddenly leaped.

“Then, I was woken up by your call.”

His girlfriend replied, “When I think about it now, it’s really scary. If it weren’t for your phone call, I don’t know what nightmare I’d have had!”

“...”

Hearing this, Han Zizhe was silent for a moment. He was about to tell her of his dream, but suddenly noticed the red light in front of him, so he hurriedly stepped on the brakes!

There was a loud squeal. Their car had almost hit the car in front.

“Phew, so close...”

Han Zizhe heaved a sigh of relief. After this interruption, his notion of telling her his dream was also forgotten.

After arriving at the movie theater, the two negotiated and finally decided on a superficial romance. Then, they bought two buckets of popcorn and two bottles of Coke and entered the venue.

The two found their seat numbers. This time, they had deliberately chosen the last row. They sat down with enthusiasm, but at this moment, Han Zizhe felt that inexplicable impulse hit him again...

However, the source of this impulse did not originate from his girlfriend, but from somewhere near the entry.

Han Zizhe looked there instinctively, only to see a man wearing gold-rimmed glasses and refined clothes walk into the venue, toward the last row where they were.

At the same time he walked in, the man seemed to feel something and suddenly raised his head. His eyes immediately met Han Zizhe’s.

At this, they both felt a jolt.

In the next second, the man recovered immediately. He smiled politely at Han Zizhe and then began to find his seat, which happened to be next to Han Zizhe’s girlfriend.

Han Zizhe observed the other party secretly for a while. Seeing that there was nothing unusual about the other party, he then turned his head back.

Just then, the lights in the venue turned off!

The movie — was starting.

This romance movie was no different than other romances. Inevitably, it was the story of a couple who fell in love, had a misunderstanding, broke up, resolved the misunderstanding, met again, had another misunderstanding, and after a series of separations and reconciliations, finally stayed together for good.

Both the male and female leads gave an average performance, but at this age, few of the viewers were looking at the acting. Most of those who came to watch were young people in love. To them, watching the movie was just a pretext.

At this time, Han Zizhe had this kind of thought. As the plot was unfolding halfway, there was a special desire stirring awake in his body. He looked around, only to find that the entire venue was dark and he could barely see half a meter away. Only the deafening acoustics could be heard throughout the venue.

Seeing this, he had impure thoughts and wanted to lean in the direction of his girlfriend.

Even so, Han Zizhe found that no matter how far he leaned over, he could not touch his girlfriend. The seats in the cinema might seem to be close, but in fact, the space between them was not that small.

Seeing this, Han Zizhe also had to dispel that notion. Anyway, there was time after this, so he was not in a hurry...

Thinking of this, Han Zizhe calmed down. After that, he even felt rather drowsy. This was obviously caused by staying up too late last night and his consequent lack of sleep.

Thus, he closed his eyes alone while listening to the sound coming from the stereo sound system.

Soon, Han Zizhe fell asleep again...

...

Looking at the deserted streets, Han Zizhe only felt a special desire in his heart surging throughout his body. He could not describe what kind of feeling it was as if it was the most primitive desire of humankind, silently burning Han Zizhe’s nerves. This caused him to be tormented by the idea of wanting to fuse with others as one.

“How I wish... How I wish to be with them, to stick with them with no distance between us, fuse into one, until our flesh and blood are fully merged and our vessels flow into one another’s. How wonderful that would be...

“Come on, let’s fuse, for we’re all alone...”

Han Zizhe murmured as he walked but at this moment, he suddenly saw dark figures appearing on the street. These people kept coming toward him. Han Zizhe did not even think about it, and immediately walked up to them...

“Agh!!!”

After some time, just when Han Zizhe was feeling dazed and dreamy, a sudden piercing scream woke him up.

This scream was full of fear as if one had seen the most horrible thing in the world. Then, the scream continued to expand and new screams even joined the chorus. At the same time, there was the commotion of many people running.

This tumultuous noise woke Han Zizhe up at once.

He first rubbed his eyes confusedly and opened his eyes to look at the source of the scream, only to see a group of men and women in the seats in front of him, with their eyes widened, looking at the seat next to him in horror.

This seat was...

Han Zizhe turned his head, still blurry, then his pupils shrank, and he suddenly jumped up!

“How, how could this be...”

Looking at his girlfriend next to him, Han Zizhe only felt cold sweat drenching his body. His face was pale and he wore an expression of extreme terror...

He saw that in the seat next to him, his girlfriend had tilted her head and was lying quietly in her seat, but what was strange was that her head and upper body had fused with the man in the gold-rimmed glasses in the neighboring seat!

Yes, fused!

The bodies of those two were glued together like conjoined twins. The heads of the two fused at the cheeks — the right cheek of Han Zizhe’s girlfriend and the left cheek of the man had merged into each other’s, embedded deep into each other’s skulls. Even their eyeballs had pressed together and then were squeezed out of their eye sockets...

At a glance, it looked like a bottle gourd was dangling on their faces...

The corners of their mouths were also merged and the tension in their skin caused the corners of their mouths to rise, creating a very peculiar curve as if they were laughing at the world...

The same was true for the bodies of the two. Han Zizhe’s girlfriend’s right arm and the man’s left arm had fused into each other’s body, even protruding from the other side, just like a deformed monster that would only appear in a horror movie or even a nightmare!

Seeing this terrifying scene, Han Zizhe only felt a deep sense of nausea. He quickly covered his mouth, but still could not hold back. He lowered his head and vomited furiously...

...

A day later.

In the morgue of the Shangdu Police Department, a body covered in white cloth was pushed in by several workers in protective suits.

Unlike ordinary corpses, this corpse seemed so huge that it could not even be covered with a wide white cloth. Several ashy gray arms protruded from the edge of the stretcher.

One, two, three...

A total of five arms protruded from this stretcher.

In the morgue, several forensic doctors who also wore protective suits were holding some tools. One of them walked up and pulled the white cloth off. At once, shock emerged in everyone’s eyes.

“This is the fourth case...”

One of the forensic doctors gasped sharply. He looked toward the stretcher and saw four or five humans fused together like a deranged and evil lump of dough...

chapters.title: Chapter 533: A Great Foe Arrives
chapters.body:
Chapter 533: A Great Foe Arrives

Chapter 533: A Great Foe Arrives

The clock wound back to the previous day.

It was midnight and Chen Chen was walking by himself on a deserted road.

The route he took was off the beaten path. The road was in poor condition as it was covered in potholes and the occasional mud pit.

Along both sides of the road were fields of disarrayed weeds almost half a regular person’s height. The field was eerily silent. The usual sound of chirping cicadas was absent and the weeds did not rustle either since there was no wind blowing in any direction.

This was an unnatural state for what was a common midsummer night.

The entire road was almost pitch-black save for the tiny glimmer of moonlight shone from above that was just enough to save Chen Chen from tripping.

“Thwack!”

Chen Chen suddenly felt a faint jolt in his shoulder when something bumped into him. It was only then when he noticed a large figure had just passed by and that their shoulders had made contact with each other.

Chen Chen rubbed his shoulder in confusion. He found it odd that although the road was without any streetlights and was completely dark, there was still an unfathomable amount of people passing through it. With every few steps he took, he would see another dark figure coming toward his direction.

Chen Chen saw dozens of figures as he traveled along the road. Now and then, he would bump into one accidentally.

For some reason, the thought of why he was even here in the first place did not cross his mind. The only thing that occupied his thoughts was that all of these people seemed to be mumbling something.

However, he could not make out what these people were saying.

“Thump!”

While Chen Chen was lost in his thoughts, he felt another throbbing sensation in his nose. While he was letting his mind wander, he bumped into yet another dark figure!

Chen Chen finally managed to make out what these people were saying:

“Fuse with us, fuse with us... For all of us are lonely...”

“Come, fuse with us, let us be one...”

This occult chanting immediately evoked a sense of danger in Chen Chen. He felt he did not have enough time to get up so he kicked his feet against the ground to put as much distance between himself and the figure as possible. It was at that moment when he saw several other figures approaching the figure that knocked him to the ground. He watched in horror as this group of figures started to fuse!

“Gurgle gurgle...”

Chen Chen heard the sound of gnashing bones and flesh as these figures somehow fused together. Their bodies and limbs became deformed and elongated like bubble gums as if mashing balls of dough together. Meanwhile, more figures were pouring into the mix!

In the end, a lovecraftian monster that was a mass of limbs and heads slowly rose in front of Chen Chen. Countless heads attached to the body of this monstrosity turned to Chen Chen in unison:

“Fuse with us, fuse with us. We’re all alone, it’s only by fusing, only by fusing...”

Countless voices echoed in Chen Chen’s ears. His heart drummed violently against his chest. Just as he was about to make one last vain effort to get away, his eyes snapped open!

“Huff!”

Chen Chen shot up from the bed and he was drenched in sweat.

Chen Chen found himself in the dormitory in the Spire Experimental Base. Due to limited space in the Spire Experimental Base and the prioritization for space for conducting research, even Chen Chen only lived in a single-bed dormitory.

This dormitory was fully furnished with a personal bathroom and Blacklight’s fully automated medical platform as well as the holographic environment simulator. However, it was still a regular-sized dormitory just like the ones of the common faculty members, no exceptions were made.

“This is the second time.”

Chen Chen felt the beads of sweat dripping down his cheeks and frowned irritatedly.

These days, his body had been enhanced to a degree where he did not require much sleep anymore as he only needed roughly three hours of rest each day. Occurrences like nightmares were nearly foreign to him at this point since it had been years since he last had one. His mental fortitude was tough as titanium steel.

Yet, in only a little over a day since his return from Mars, he had two nightmares...

Chen Chen knitted his brows together with this in mind as he carried himself to the fully automated medical platform and ordered, “Conduct full body scan.”

The fully automated medical platform immediately began operation and performed an examination from head to toe.

Nothing notable was discovered.

“There’s nothing wrong with my body...”

Chen Chen rose from the platform and looked at his palms. His mind was deep in thought, ‘Could it be just a common nightmare? Am I overthinking it?’

While his mind was occupied, he suddenly heard gentle footsteps following along the corridor outside. He heard it hurrying toward his room until it stopped outside the door.

“Knock, knock, knock...”

The sound of knocking on the door.

“Come in.”

Chen Chen already had an idea who it was just by listening to the footsteps. Besides, he was in the Spire Experimental Base. Currently, there was only one person who could visit him here and it was Little X.

Little X could easily connect to the communication system in the room to speak to Chen Chen. Still, whenever Chen Chen was resting and nothing urgent was going on, she had a fancy for using regular human methods such as these.

Unsurprisingly, it was Little X’s head that poked through the door. She bit her lip slightly and asked with a tone of concern, “Sir Godfather, another nightmare?”

“Yes, but it’s no big deal.”

Chen Chen waved his hands. “Did anything happen?”

“I just found some mysterious reports on the Internet and I figured you might be interested.”

Little X remarked as she invited herself into the room.

“Since you’re putting it this way, I’ll assume it’s something fascinating.”

Chen Chen remarked with a nod as he poured himself a cup of water at the drinking fountain. “Go ahead.”

“Here’s how it goes.”
