The wall was about 30 meters high and extended from in front of him to the end of his line of sight. This wall directly enclosed the entire city.

Unlike the run-down buildings in the city, this wall was made of some unknown material. Its entire surface was non-reflective and black, appearing to be extremely tough.

In the center of the wall, there was a steel gate of the same height. The gate was dark red and there was even a row of digital buttons on both sides of the gate.

Was this a combination lock?

Chen Chen’s expression became even more astonished. Seeing a code lock in a medieval-style city was like seeing a group of knights wearing plate armor and not wielding a longsword but an AK each. It was too anachronistic.

Chen Chen did not think about the problem of the password. He tried to rise again to see what was behind the wall but when he rose to the height of the wall, he bumped into the invisible barrier again.

Erm...

Chen Chen suddenly understood that this space was sealed and everyone was only allowed to move within the scope of this space. No one was allowed to leave here by any means other than opening the gate.

This rule not only restricted Chen Chen but also restricted the group of people from leaving here.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen could not help looking mystified. According to the unique nature of this type of game, if one wanted to get the password to open the gate, one must solve a puzzle to do so, but the opponent’s IQ was only 5...

Chen Chen once again recalled the character card that was created on the consciousness battlefield. There was a [3D6] hint in the attributes of the previous character. This digit could only be understood by those who had played tabletop role-playing games. It represented the value cast by three hexahedral dice. Among them, D meant dice and the preceding 3 meant that the number of dice was 3, and 6 meant that each dice was a hexahedron, which was the most common cube dice.

Due to this, the evaluation given to Chen Chen’s IQ of 18 points was the “human limit” because the highest number of points that could be obtained by three hexahedral dice was only 18 points while the average value of a normal person was only 9 to 11 points.

The reason Chen Chen’s other attributes could exceed 18 points was also that Chen Chen was not a normal human being, but a T-virus modified person.

The difference between these values was not as simple as 1+1 equals 2, but an exponential calculation method. Compared with a normal person’s 11-point strength, the difference between that and Chen Chen’s 21-point strength could be more than tenfold.

The higher the value, the greater the degree of difference.

If the character card produced by the consciousness battlefield was truly the real data of the character, then it would be more difficult for the Consciousness Stealer to solve the puzzle within 24 hours.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen immediately thought of a special ability of the other party — Mind Reading 99.

As the name suggested, mind reading was one of the abilities possessed by Consciousness Stealers. If it wanted to get the password or hide in the group of adventurers or acquire it from the minds of the adventurers, the 99 behind the skill represented the level of the skill.

Tabletop role-playing games were all the same. What this skill indicated was that the player would roll five dice with 20 faces each and the level number meant that as long as the sum of the points rolled by the five dice was lower than the skill level, the skill was successful. Otherwise, it would fail.

For example, Chen Chen’s psychology was 77, so as long as the sum of the points of the five dice was less than 77, it would be considered a success. However, if it was greater than 77, the skill would fail.

In this way, the higher the level, the higher the chance of success and level 99 was the full level. The skill would only fail if one’s luck was terrible to the extreme and a full 100 points were obtained by the dice.

His opponent’s mind-reading skill was 99, which meant it was almost impossible to result in a failure.

In other words, maybe it could open the door in other ways such as mind-reading?

When Chen Chen was deducing this, the sky had turned completely dark and the entire city was plunged into darkness.

“That’s not right...”

However, Chen Chen frowned because he found that the speed at which the sky was getting dark was too quick. With his brain power, he was certain that only about half an hour had passed since he had woken up all the way to the present, but time had seemed to be accelerated by three times.

Three times, was it?

Chen Chen secretly remembered this number. The task gave him 24 hours. If this was calculated at three times the speed of real-time, it meant that he had three nights to fulfill these goals. Each night was about three to four hours.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen had already figured out some patterns. He turned around again and looked into the city, only to see a light flashing by in a building not far away.

That was... The light of a candle!

Seeing this scene, Chen Chen’s heart jolted. He rushed directly toward the building, and within a few seconds, he was outside the building!

“Patter patter...”

Suddenly, there were a few raucous footsteps in this two-story building, which sounded like someone fleeing.

“They noticed me?”

Chen Chen looked even more surprised. He rushed into the room directly through the window and stepped off his feet, jumping down the stairs like a dexterous cat. At once, a young man dressed in a black windbreaker, looking like an explorer, was seen blocking the gate.

This was a typical Western young man with a high nose, blond hair, and blue eyes, but his skin was slightly dark. At this moment, seeing Chen Chen standing in front of him, his pupils suddenly shrank and he turned around and ran inside the room without thinking.

Chen Chen observed this guy with great interest, only to notice that he was far superior to ordinary people in terms of decisiveness and agility. His skills were extraordinary.

However, faced with Chen Chen, this agile skill was futile. The guy had run into the room and wanted to escape through the window, but the next second, he felt an invisible force grabbing him and dragging him back in front of Chen Chen!

Seeing this, a ruthless look came into the eyes of the young man. He drew out a dagger at his waist and stabbed Chen Chen!

“Clang!”

There was a sharp impact sound. When the dagger in the young man’s hand pierced toward Chen Chen, it was blocked by an invisible barrier and a loud sound erupted at the same time.

“Attacked by a dagger, determining the decision to defend... The decision to defend was successful!”

At the moment when the opponent attacked Chen Chen, Chen Chen suddenly heard the loud voice from before and at the same time, there was a clinking sound of landing dice as if someone was rolling dice. After that, there was a notification of a successful defense.

Chen Chen listened to the noises in his mind while he waved one hand and the dagger flew out of the guy’s hand!

The young man only felt an irrepressible force wrench the dagger away from his hand. At the same time, a cold hand was directly clasped around his neck!

“Do you want to live or die?”

Chen Chen calmly gripped his neck and asked in a low voice, “If you want to live, you must answer my question.”

Feeling the terrible power contained in Chen Chen’s grip, the young man scrabbled at Chen Chen’s arm and his face flushed. He nodded quickly.

Chen Chen released his hand. The young man at once squatted and covered his throat, coughing heavily.

Chen Chen waited slowly. When the other party had recovered, he asked indifferently, “Who are you?”

“We, we’re explorers from the Principality of Assadin...”

The young man lowered his head in fear and said softly, “We accepted the commission of the mercenary union, to come here to find the treasure of Dulles Chen, the vampire count...”

Dulles Chen, was that himself?

Chen Chen’s heart leaped and meanwhile, he stared at the man with an unfathomable look.

“Using the skill of psychology, determining use of skill... Psychology is used successfully!”

In the next second, there was another jingling sound by his ear and at the same time, Chen Chen also developed a kind of comprehension that the two lines the man said were not false.

“The Principality of Assadin, the mercenary union...”

Chen Chen paced slowly in front of the opponent. “Treasure hunting? I think you still have the task of destroying me, right?”

Hearing this, the young man immediately lowered his head and fell silent.

“How many people are there and how strong are you?”

Chen Chen then asked.

“We have four teams with twenty people in total...”

The youth immediately replied, “The strength of the other team members is unknown. I only know that there are five people in my team. Besides me, there are two paladins, a wizard, and a priest... Our captain is the paladin, Julius!”

“Paladin? Wizard?”

Chen Chen narrowed his eyes and scanned the young man before him again. “Are you a ranger or a rogue?”

“Your Excellency, I’m the rogue, Gil Cedric the rogue.”

Chen Chen nodded and then continued to ask, “Are you familiar with the rest of them?”

“I’m not very familiar with them. We were all commissioned by the mercenary union to form a temporary team.”

The young man suddenly added. “By the way, Your Excellency, the two Paladins came together. They have the ability to protect against evil and are a great threat to you!”

“Oh?”

Chen Chen secretly memorized these words in his heart and then asked again, “Do the other teams have the same configuration?”

“It’s almost the same...”

The youth nodded.

“Have you ever seen a strange person in your team or other teams?”

After thinking about it, Chen Chen finally asked, “For example, his intelligence isn’t that high or he looks strange or something?”

“This...”

The young man shook his head blankly. “I’m not sure about this because I only met the other teams once before I came to Wasteland City.”

“Wasteland City?”

Chen Chen muttered in a low voice and gradually fell into contemplation.

“Your Excellency, please, please forgive my offense...”

Seeing Chen Chen sinking into thought, the young man gritted his teeth, trying to fight for his last chance at life. “As long as you let me go, I’m willing to leave this place now...”

However, before he finished speaking, Chen Chen suddenly raised his hand. In the next second, the young man was slammed backward as if he was hit by some massive blow and his body directly hit the wall behind!

“Boom!”

The entire building seemed to tremble and a scattering of soil fell from the ceiling. At the same time, blood was splashed all over the place and the young man was reduced to a pulp on the wall behind him...

“Not this one...”

Without even looking at the other party’s body, Chen Chen shook his head and stared out the window thoughtfully. “Sure enough, it’s a Western Fantasy module similar to D&D. It even has classes such as Paladins and Wizards. So, the ones I’m facing aren’t just ordinary people?”

Thinking of this, Chen Chen’s Field directly swept the entire house and in the next second, he immediately detected a locked room upstairs.

Chen Chen went upstairs again and walked to the door of that room, only to see that the door lock of the room showed many traces of damage and these traces were very new. It was the young man’s handiwork earlier.

It looked like he had used a candle for lighting and wanted to open the door, but eventually, he was discovered by Chen Chen?

Thinking of this, Chen Chen’s Field energy extended immediately, turning into a thin line as it fitted into the keyhole of the door. In the next second, there was a click and the door lock opened in response.

Chen Chen used Field to protect himself while gently pushing open the door, only to see that the room was empty, with only one iron box placed in the corner of the walls.

This was...

Chen Chen waved his hand and the iron box in the corner suddenly opened, revealing a piece of yellowed cloth inside.

Chen Chen unrolled the cloth and saw a map layout on the cloth.

The map showed the city where Chen Chen was located and the drawing was fairly detailed. Every street and every house were marked and in the center of the map, a large circle had been drawn with a red pen.

This circle was the location of Chen Chen’s castle.

This was a prop for the group of adventurers!

chapters.title: Chapter 545: Hunting Game
chapters.body:
Chapter 545: Hunting Game

Chapter 545: Hunting Game

With Chen Chen’s memory ability, he imprinted the entire map in his mind in just a few seconds. Then, he tore the map to confetti before continuing to search for traces of other people.

“Since my opponents aren’t ordinary people, then my strategy has to change...”

Chen Chen left the house. He expanded his Field to the maximum and scanned for any clues within a radius of 50 meters. At present, it seemed that if he wanted to win the consciousness battlefield, he must kill all the invaders and find the Consciousness Stealer.

However, his opponents were not necessarily good people as well. Based on the skills of that rogue earlier, these people would not just be sitting ducks but have their own prowess.

Silently thinking about all this, Chen Chen lifted off again and began to patrol the entire sky.

The area of the entire Wasteland City was about ten square kilometers and the map showed it was a square city, which meant that the city had a side length of 3.3 kilometers. At Chen Chen’s current speed, it took about an hour and a half for him to do a thorough sweep. However, the main thing was that this group of people might not just be hiding in one fixed place, so it became more difficult to search for them.

Chen Chen felt that he still had to begin with these intruders.

“Whoosh!”

Thinking of this, Chen Chen flew right up. He wandered a little bit over the city, scanning every house he passed. With the power of Field energy, it was very simple to do this kind of thing and his total Field energy nowadays could sustain such activity as long as it was not a highly vigorous use of Field.

Soon, when Chen Chen was scanning a house, his heart suddenly leaped. Although he did not see anyone else, he found another yellowed tin box.

Chen Chen waved with one hand and the iron box flew out from the gap in the iron window grille and into Chen Chen’s hands.

This iron box was not as splendidly decorated as the previous one. Chen Chen thought for a while then started by wrapping Field around the iron box before he slowly opened it.

There was no mechanism in the iron box. Instead, there was a simple glass bottle half-filled with some purple potion.

“What is this...”

Chen Chen frowned. He opened the lid and sniffed it lightly, detecting an ink-like smell. Could it be that this was a bottle of ink?

Nonetheless, there was no description on this bottle of potion and Chen Chen was not certain what this bottle of potion was.

Perhaps only those invaders with their D&D background could identify this potion.

Chen Chen casually put the potion into his pocket and then continued his sweep. Soon, in a house, he found a trap in the form of two stone bricks.

He recognized this was a trap because one of the stone bricks had weird patterns on it while the other stone brick had rows of spikes.

Chen Chen tried to put the palm of his hand on the stone brick with weird patterns. Under this pressure, the spikes on the other stone brick immediately flew out, whistling harshly as they pierced the wall on the other side.

Chen Chen’s eyes lit up. He collected all the spikes and pressed them back into the hole of the second stone brick, only to see that the lines on the first stone brick had become a little dim, but it still contained some energy. Maybe it could be used one more time.

A spell trap?

Such a term flashed in Chen Chen’s mind.

Chen Chen collected these two stone bricks as well and resumed his inspection.

Ten minutes later, Chen Chen found a longsword.

This time, the place where he found this sword was just a very simple firewood shack. This shack was located in the space between two mansions. If he did not pay attention, he might even have overlooked the existence of this firewood shack. The box containing the sword was also very ordinary, much more ordinary than the first two boxes he found, but this iron box had an iron lock that the iron boxes before did not have.

When Chen Chen penetrated deep into the lock of the box, he immediately found that the lock of this iron box was extremely complicated. It was likely that even professionals would need to spend a lot of effort to crack it. Upon opening the box, he saw the long handle of the sword inside.

Like the iron box in which it was contained, this longsword was also very ordinary, without any embellishing patterns, just a simple blade and black handle. It even appeared to be inferior to the ordinary iron swords that had not been ground on Taobao.

However, when he saw this sword, Chen Chen frowned because he felt a mysterious burning sensation emanating from the sword.

Chen Chen landed into the room and tried to grasp the hilt, but the next second, a piercing heat stung him. This scorching pain even exceeded the time when he was exposed to the sun and at the same time, Chen Chen’s consciousness also became blurry.

“Clatter!”

The iron sword slid from Chen Chen’s hand and stabbed into the wooden floor with a “clang”.

Chen Chen looked a little anguished. He opened his right hand but saw that the palm of his hand had been burned and blistered. The rotten meat on his hand was constantly giving off white smoke as if what he had just grasped was a red hot soldering iron.

“This sword is strange...”

Chen Chen pulled back his palm and tried to use the Field energy to enclose this longsword, but was even more shocked to discover that even Field energy dissipated when it touched the longsword. Chen Chen could not wield this item at all.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen’s expression became gloomier. He picked up the iron box, put it over the sword, and scooped the sword back in, then buried the iron box into the ground.

After doing all this, Chen Chen waved one hand to smooth out the traces of the burying. He was about to leave but suddenly stopped because his Field energy detected that two humans had entered his sensing range.

They were heading in this direction...

Chen Chen raised his eyebrows and immediately slipped into the building next to him. He wanted to find out how they noticed there was movement here, but when they were about 40 meters away in Chen Chen’s range, the two of them halted at the same time!

Just when Chen Chen was puzzled, the two suddenly spun around, then scattered without hesitation, and fled in two directions!

Had Chen Chen been discovered?

Chen Chen looked even more astounded. He stopped hiding but chased one of them. With the power of the Field energy, the opponent could only run away for a few seconds before Chen Chen caught up!

It was not until then that Chen Chen could see that the one he was chasing was a girl in a cloak holding a wooden staff!

Wizard?

This word flashed in Chen Chen’s mind. Both the attire and the wooden staff resembled wizards in movies and Chen Chen had already obtained some data from the young rogue, so he had a flash of realization now.

“O—K—G!”

However, as Chen Chen swiftly approached her, there was a glint of perseverance in the girl’s eyes. She turned around and uttered a phrase of unknown meaning. At the same time, she raised the staff in her hand and aimed the top of the staff at Chen Chen.

Suddenly, an inexplicable spatial fluctuation rushed over him. Chen Chen had never felt this kind of fluctuation but he did not evade it. Instead, he gathered a large amount of Field energy to form an invisible barrier in front of him.

He was going to test the opponent’s strength with his strongest defense!

Although it might be easier for Chen Chen to dodge, he was now facing unfamiliar forces after all, and the total number of opponents exceeded 20. If they besieged him at once, he was afraid it would be difficult for him to evade them all. Instead of doing so, it was better to test out his opponent when they were alone, to better understand their abilities.

These thoughts zipped through Chen Chen’s mind. In the next second, a blazing white firelight flared up at the top of the staff and at the same time, seven or eight rays of light surged directly toward Chen Chen!

“Arcane Missile!”

Just as these rays of light struck Chen Chen, the girl on the other side also called out the name of this spell!

In the next second, Chen Chen only felt that his Field was corroded by those few streaks of flame, resulting in a few big holes. However, each one only burrowed a few centimeters forward before exhausting its energy and was extinguished.

This was...

Chen Chen raised his eyebrows. Although the thrusting force of this fire was lacking, its might was extremely impressive. Each ray was comparable to the power of an anti-material sniper rifle. Even so, it was a pipe dream for them to penetrate his own barrier. Now, the power of Field energy was simply not something this person could break through.

Thinking of this, he was about to crush his opponent to death but suddenly, there was a wave of fluctuations behind him. A faintly discernible phantom emerged from behind him while stabbing a dagger toward the middle of his back!

“Huh?”

Chen Chen looked even more puzzled. He abruptly turned around and at the same time, seized the opponent’s dagger in the palm of his hand with a backhand. Only then did he see this opponent. This person was completely dark as if he were a shadow!

Seeing that his fatal blow was fended off by Chen Chen, this dark shadow was also startled, but his reaction was extremely quick. He immediately gave up the dagger and leaped back again. He suddenly melted into the dark shadows around him, vanishing right in the perception of Chen Chen’s Field!

“So, you can blend into the shadows?”

Chen Chen squeezed furiously and there was a nauseating snap. Chen Chen had pinched the dagger into broken strands. Then, he stretched out his hand again and gestured, and the girl in the wizard robe flew toward Chen Chen at once!

“R—U—A!”

While she was still in the air, the girl hurriedly spoke again, but before she finished her words, she suddenly felt something bulky and invisible stuffed into her mouth, choking off all her remaining words!

“Ugh...”

The girl suddenly widened her eyes. She did not understand what was stuffed in her mouth, but she felt her throat swell and widen uncontrollably, and even her breathing was blocked!

“Save, save me...”

The girl’s throat was swollen until tears and snot were streaming over her face. She looked desperately at the shadows around her as if praying to the shadow man for help!

“Let her go!”

Suddenly, the shadow man loomed out of the darkness at Chen Chen’s bottom right and at the same time, with a decisive stroke, he aimed a dagger directly at Chen Chen’s waist...

“Clang!”

Still, this time, Chen Chen had long been prepared. The man only pierced into an unseen barrier but he could not go any further!

“Bang, splat!”

Immediately, there was a sound of a watermelon bursting and rupturing. The shadow man looked back blankly, only to see the girl’s head exploding, with red and white stuff splashing all over her body!

“Thump!”

The headless body of the girl dropped directly to her knees and the nerves of her body twitched unconsciously because of the loss of the brain center...

“Argh—!”

The shadow man suddenly unleashed a desperate roar, but in the next second, Chen Chen directly raised his hand and pressed it on his head, then pushed viciously!

“Plop!”

The shadow man’s snarl was cut off immediately, for Chen Chen had pushed his head into his chest cavity...

“The toughness and strength of his body are beyond that of human beings in reality...”

As if finishing a trivial task, Chen Chen shook off the blood on his hand and thought aloud in a low voice, “This is probably the factor of physique. These adventurers with magical backgrounds have physique and strength that surpass modern people, but not by much... Maybe because these two aren’t capable enough... But their skills are peculiar indeed. If it weren’t for my Field power, I’m afraid it’d take me a lot of effort to kill them...”

Just as Chen Chen was thinking about this, he suddenly sensed that three more silhouettes were approaching on the right front and at the same time, five figures had appeared on the left!

“Eight people?”

Chen Chen’s expression shifted because he sensed that when they were about 40 meters away from him, everyone suddenly paused. Then, as if someone had said something, the team of five continued approaching whereas the team of three on the other side stopped for a few seconds before making their way here at a lower speed.

“It seems that everyone can sense my presence when they are 40 meters away from me? To fight or not to fight?”

Being cautious by nature, Chen Chen had already noticed that when facing this group of adventurers, he did not seem to have the power to fully crush them easily, especially when there were too many opponents.

Chen Chen wore a thoughtful look, but after thinking about it for half a second, he quickly backed away, and then left the scene...

After going off for fifty or sixty meters, he only heard an angry roar from the area where he was fighting earlier. Someone from the eight people knew the two who were killed.

“Don’t worry, it’ll be your turn soon...”

Chen Chen smirked icily and then hurried toward the castle.

At this time, more than an hour had passed since the beginning. Anyone with the intention to do so might have come close to the location of the castle and Chen Chen dared not claim that this group of NPCs with magical abilities would not have the ability to find the fireplace tunnel.

A few minutes later, Chen Chen hurried back to the castle. The moment he entered the castle, his Field was fully activated, scanning everything in the hall like a radar, but he found nothing.

The wooden gate of the castle had decayed and collapsed for some reason, so the castle was, strictly speaking, defenseless, and anyone could enter. Chen Chen was afraid that he would fall into the encirclement of his opponents when he entered the castle.

However, it seemed that no one had entered this place for the time being.

In this case, Chen Chen looked at his feet and then he pried out a floor brick at the entrance of the castle, put the brick with runes below, and then re-laid the previous floor brick. As for the brick capable of shooting out spikes, Chen Chen hung it directly on the ceiling directly above the first brick.

In this way, if someone entered the castle and stepped on this, they would immediately be taught a harsh lesson by the spikes.

“It’s a pity I couldn’t bring in my nano poker cards.”

Chen Chen shook his head. If his poker cards could be brought into the consciousness battlefield, he would have been invincible even against the gods by now. Unless it was the Consciousness Stealer who had the same defensive ability, the wizards and rogues would be practically defenseless before him. They would have no ability to sense his attacks.

chapters.title: Chapter 546: Paladin
chapters.body:
Chapter 546: Paladin

Chapter 546: Paladin

While Chen Chen was standing in the shadows of the hall, thinking, he suddenly sensed two figures in his Field range. The two men were stooped low, surreptitiously approaching the castle gate. One of them seemed to be dressed as a wizard and the other was dressed as a knight in full armor.

The wizard’s body seemed to be in a completely transparent state, with only the distortion of light caused by his movements. Still, in this dark night, the distorted light was also difficult to detect.

Invisibility technique?

Chen Chen frowned. This invisibility technique that could only conceal through light rays had no effect on him. Under the detection of Field energy, this invisibility was much more conspicuous than that rogue’s ability to blend into the shadows.

At least when the rogue escaped into the shadows, even Chen Chen’s Field energy had trouble locating him.

Although the knight was not invisible, when he walked in the dark and stooped over, he could make himself silent.

Stealth.

Suddenly, such a word flashed in Chen Chen’s mind.

If Chen Chen was not wrong, this was a skill called stealth. The higher the stealth skill, the lower the user’s presence. With the help of some other methods, one could even attain the ability to blend into the shadows such as the rogue Chen Chen had killed previously.

Sensing the two people approaching slowly, Chen Chen thought for a while. Then, he simply stepped back, always keeping both of them at a distance of more than 40 meters.

Sure enough, at this distance, neither of them discovered his presence.

“In other words, everyone can notice me within forty meters? Is this the ability of props? Or is it their own ability?”

Chen Chen looked suspicious, but now it was not the time to bother about these things. Feeling that the two were approaching the castle area as if they were thieves, Chen Chen’s mouth twitched in a smile as he continued to retreat. When he reached a corner and could not continue to retreat, he flipped out directly from the side window.

Finally, the two of them walked into the hall of the castle.

When they entered Chen Chen’s castle, the nerves of the two were already highly strung. They moved more slowly and cautiously, but it was a pity that they did not step on the trap laid before.

They seemed to be looking for something.

Chen Chen frowned and did not make a move but continued to observe. There might be some clues that he did not know.

After going around the entire castle hall, the expressions of the two of them were more relaxed. Just then, the transparent wizard put down his hood, revealing a middle-aged face with a yellow beard. “Good news, it seems. The vampire count has indeed gone out hunting...”

“Don’t let your guard down, Golas.”

The knight in full armor on the side also heaved a sigh of relief. His body was not of a strong bulky build, but a streamlined slender body. At this moment, he stood up and rubbed his sore legs. “The vampire count may return at any time. Let’s look for the artifact as soon as possible. If we find it, this vampire is done for!”

“It’s a pity that our rogue was killed...”

The wizard immediately replied, “Otherwise it’ll be easier for us to find it. I hope the vampire count won’t trap us here...”

“It won’t be a problem.”

The knight chuckled. “The captain and the priest John are guarding outside the door. Even if the vampire count returns, he’ll be distracted by the captain and John first, leaving us with a chance to escape. Don’t worry!”

The two chatted casually as they began to look around the hall.

Hearing the conversation between them, Chen Chen could not help frowning. If he did not guess wrong, the artifact they mentioned was the simple longsword that Chen Chen found.

That sword had a powerful ability to damage Chen Chen and even the Field energy could not block the sword’s strike. This was the power of the sword. If it was used by an adventurer, it would not be difficult to kill Chen Chen. No wonder these people wanted to find that longsword.

Still, these people did not know that the sword had been buried deep in the ground by Chen Chen and it was not in the castle.

However, when they said that some people were lying in ambush outside the gate, Chen Chen immediately circled the castle to the front gate. Immediately, he detected that two people were hiding in a house in front of the castle.

Of these two, one knight also wore silver-white armor, but he was much more muscular than the previous knight, more like a warrior who liked to face things head-on. The other one was wearing a robe, but according to the previous conversation between the two in the castle, this person should be a priest.

In other words, the nanny.

Chen Chen kept the distance of forty meters in mind. He only approached those two at about fifty meters away and then observed them.

According to the conversation between the two before, this four-person team should be the team of the rogue that Chen Chen had first encountered. Rogues usually roamed about as scouts, so Chen Chen only met him by himself.

After losing the rogue, the four of them simply went to Chen Chen’s lair to look for an opportunity.

Moreover, according to the information that Chen Chen overheard, this warrior and the warrior who had entered the castle had the same profession, a class called paladin. This profession was extremely lethal toward vampires.

Although Chen Chen was not a vampire, he was indeed playing the role of a vampire now. This kind of paladin was used to deal with himself.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen did not hesitate anymore. He condensed a huge hammer directly using Field at a distance of more than 50 meters, then hurled it at the burly paladin captain!

Chen Chen’s Field power was immense. Although he was distanced by more than 50 meters, he still exerted a huge force. It was just a second before the hammer hit the paladin that this paladin seemed to have sensed something. He suddenly turned around and at the same time, raised the shield in his hand in front of him!

“Boom!”

There was a huge impact that boomed out as loudly as a large copper bell. The priest beside barely had time to gasp before he was slammed directly into the wall behind him, and then bounced back to the ground again. He was reduced to a crumpled pulp.

All the bones in his body were shattered!

The target of this attack, the paladin captain, was thrown through the wall behind him, but it seemed that because of his strong physique and the armor he wore, he only coughed up two mouthfuls of blood and kipped up while looking at the darkness ahead with amazement.

Earlier, he had sensed an indescribable chill coming from behind him. With a knight’s instinct, he immediately used a shield to protect himself. As a result, the priest beside him became collateral damage and died of the aftershock...

“D*mn it...”

The paladin captain gritted his teeth and he drew his sword with a sharp “clang”, looking as if he was ready to face death. At this moment, he had already planned to do everything in his power to block Chen Chen, to gain a chance for his companions who had gone deep into the lion’s den to escape!

Upon seeing this captain’s actions, Chen Chen had an even better impression of him. According to the rules of D&D, not just anyone could take on the profession of a paladin. It required extremely strict self-control and strong faith. That was to say, a person who could become a paladin was a person who pursued justice because a person who had evil in his heart could never activate the power of the paladin.

Nonetheless, the battle at this moment was enough to arouse the alertness of the two in the castle. In Chen Chen’s Field range, the two in the castle had already rushed out and were running toward the city gates on both sides without hesitation!

Did they want to flee?

Chen Chen had to stay here to deal with the paladin captain first, so instead, he used his Field power to fix the two directly in place. However, in the next second, a circle of gray light appeared around the wizard’s body, shrouding him completely.

Meanwhile, the slender paladin suddenly roared and then his body flashed with a hint of silver light, which corroded a portion of Chen Chen’s Field energy!

It was just like when Chen Chen was burnt while holding the longsword before. At this time, although this white light was not as strong as the sword, it still fended off Chen Chen’s Field energy attack!

“Is this the ‘protection against evil’?”

Chen Chen’s eyes flickered. At this time, he was almost certain that among those who were the greatest threat to him, there was the profession of the paladin. Thinking of this, Chen Chen did not want to let the opponent go. He accelerated and rushed toward the slender paladin.

The closer the distance, the more powerful and flexible Chen Chen’s Field became. He threw a punch toward the paladin’s head but at the same time, secretly concentrated the Field energy and smashed it toward the opponent’s lower body!

Seeing the vampire count was appearing in front of him, the paladin instinctively drew his sword and raised it. There was a sharp “clang” and the paladin had blocked Chen Chen’s fist accurately!

Chen Chen’s physical strength was even overpowered by his opponent’s counterattack. The longsword in the paladin’s hand flickered and instead slashed toward Chen Chen!

Chen Chen’s protective Field around his body would immediately melt like white snow in the spring under the attack of that white ray!

Seeing that the sword was about to pierce his body, Chen Chen just took a step back at will and in the next second, the Field energy had completely hit the opponent’s crotch!

“Bang!”

The paladin’s eyes widened suddenly in a dazed expression. A trace of blood trickled from the corner of his mouth and he slumped to the ground...

It seemed that although the light on the paladin had the power to defend against Chen Chen’s Field, it also had to be activated. Earlier, Chen Chen first turned his opponent’s attention toward protecting the face while using Field to attack the lower body, so it was difficult for the opponent to notice the latter.

Looking at the figure that was completely limp on the ground, Chen Chen knew that the other party was dead. This blow did not simply shatter his lower body but also everything in his abdominal cavity...

“Agh!!!”

However, just as Chen Chen killed the paladin, a heart-wrenching cry erupted at his right. Chen Chen turned his head calmly but saw that the paladin captain had arrived not far away and just so happened to see Chen Chen killing his companion...

Immediately, the paladin bit the tip of his tongue viciously and spat a large mouthful of blood on his longsword. Suddenly, a white gleam of light protruded from his body and he barreled toward Chen Chen with steely determination!

“Sacrifice!”

At the same time, the paladin let out a harsh anguished roar!

At this time, in the perception of Chen Chen’s Field energy, the aura of his opponent suddenly increased by a few levels. A force that Chen Chen must treat with caution emanated from his opponent’s body. This was a force of anger and madness, and at the same time, there was also a hint of death!

This guy had expended his life force to kill Chen Chen along with himself?

Chen Chen immediately understood the other party’s intention. With a slight curl at his lips, when the paladin was about to reach him, suddenly, Chen Chen pushed off lightly with his right foot and he floated backward as if he was weightless!

“Vampire count, come down and fight me!”

Seeing Chen Chen running away, the paladin captain was even angrier. He rushed toward Chen Chen while panting but after all, he was not of an agile profession. Even if he rushed forward with all his might, he could not compare to Chen Chen’s speed.

However, Chen Chen seemed to be deliberately angering his opponent. He did not confront him but did not retreat either. Instead, he kept circling the castle, silently draining the power of the paladin captain.

One minute, two minutes, three minutes...

Five minutes later, the paladin captain was showing signs of exhaustion. His face was blue and gray, and his lips also turned a strange white.

His life force was almost depleted...

The paladin saw that he could not catch up with Chen Chen and wanted to retreat, but just as he turned back, he felt another strong wind behind him. He quickly turned around and slashed, but he did not hit anything!

Seeing this, the paladin knew that Chen Chen wanted to expend his power, so he had to continue to retreat. Even so, he had not retreated far when suddenly there was a chill in his chest and it felt like his chest was bursting...

The paladin captain looked down blankly, only to see that a hand had penetrated his blind spot and was protruding from his chest, holding a heart that was still pounding restlessly...

“Your protection has disappeared.”

Behind him, the deep voice of the vampire count was heard and this voice was already close by!

“Ugh...”

The paladin captain gave a sorrowful smile. He only felt that the strength of his body was beginning to drain from his chest. He wanted to continue to raise his sword but that hand seemed to see through his thoughts and withdrew from his chest with a “swish”.

“Pfft!”

At once, a torrent of blood mixed with mushy flesh flowed out of the big wound in his chest...

A look of defiance flashed in the eyes of the paladin captain, but this expression gradually became frozen and then he toppled forward and fell completely to the ground.

The paladin captain was dead!

chapters.title: Chapter 547: Pale Justice
chapters.body:
Chapter 547: Pale Justice

Chapter 547: Pale Justice

“There’s still one more...”

After Chen Chen killed the paladin captain, he immediately remembered that another wizard had fled from here, but it was too late to chase after him. The other party had long since melded into the ruins of the city. Looking for him would be like finding a needle in a haystack.

However, up until now, there had been six people killed. Among them, only one wizard was left in this squad. A wizard and a rogue in the other squad had also died. It seemed that there should still be fourteen more survivors.

After thinking about it, Chen Chen searched the corpses of this group of people and found a lot of bottles and jars. In addition to some potions and gold coins, there were also some things that Chen Chen could not name.

For example, there were some spices and a weird badge, but after seeing the emblem on this badge, Chen Chen’s eyes suddenly brightened.

This badge was stitched with a blood-colored Japanese rose. Blood was still flowing on the stem and there was a string of numbers on the back of the emblem.

This was...

Chen Chen turned around and looked at the top of the hall. The emblem depicted on it was indeed the same as this badge!

“In other words, does this emblem belong to me?”

Chen Chen wore a thoughtful look. “Then, if my guess is right, this string of numbers should be the code for one of the four gates.”

Seeing this, Chen Chen simply packed up everything and put them in the attic of his castle.

If they wanted to find these props, they must go through Chen Chen first.

After doing all this, Chen Chen noticed that the horizon had started to light up slightly and a ray of morning light appeared at the end of the sky. One night’s time was about to pass and the next day was being ushered in.

At the same time, Chen Chen also felt that his strength seemed to be a little weakened. This was a mandatory buff. During the day, the power of a vampire would be reduced by 10 to 20%.

However, after Chen Chen thought for a while, he did not return to the passage in the fireplace. It would be like shooting fish in a barrel. If he was found by the other party, he might not be able to run. Therefore, Chen Chen went upstairs and found a room on the second floor with a wide view, then hid there.

In this way, if someone approached the castle, he would notice them at once and he could also prevent the opponent from finding the passage behind the fireplace.

After taking refuge, Chen Chen silently watched the sky turning from the pale whiteness of the fish belly to a brighter hue. The original indigo blue color quickly faded and the sky became sky-blue like glass, pure and flawless.

As the sun gradually rose, a plume of black smoke suddenly curled up at the western gate of the city.

The black smoke rose straight up, for the tall city wall blocked the wind from dispersing it. When he saw the black smoke in the distance, Chen Chen could not help frowning.

Was it because they knew it was daytime, so Chen Chen was restrained, that they dared to build a fire to cook a meal?

No, if these people were a group of well-trained mercenaries who had drawn blood before, then they would not make such a low-level mistake. At this time, the black smoke was more like a trap, one that was supposed to entice Chen Chen to head there.

Chen Chen had already noticed that although he was a vampire, even if the ultraviolet rays irradiated his body, it would only cause burns to the epidermal skin. At this time, the wounds on his arm had healed at an unknown time.

In other words, short-term exposure to the sun would only cause minor injuries to him and he would not turn into ashes by a single ray of sunlight like in movies or legends.

This group of people seemed to understand him better than he understood himself or rather, they understood vampires very well.

It was too bad that, besides immense physical strength, Chen Chen had an even stronger Field power. Most of those people before were killed by Chen Chen’s pervasive Field energy.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen was a little eager and tempted. He was contemplating whether he should use the opponents to measure his own strength and beat them at their own game, wiping out the team near the black smoke. Nevertheless, on second thought, he gave up on this.

There were still two nights and there was no rush at all unless the group found one of the other three badges.

However, this group of people came here after being commissioned to find treasures and kill Chen Chen. That was to say, unless they suffered heavy losses and believed that the mission was impossible, they certainly would not leave easily.

As for the Consciousness Stealer hidden among the crowd, Chen Chen did not have any special strategies for the time being. It was best to go with the flow and kill this group of NPCs at the same time, eliminating the creature.

The black smoke did not billow for long. As if they knew that Chen Chen would not be fooled, the fire was soon put out again and then there was silence as before.

Chen Chen could not see any signs of movement and no one stepped into the scope of the castle, but at some unknown time, he discovered that the doors of some nearby buildings had been opened and then closed again.

The group of people was still searching for clues against Chen Chen in various rooms in the city.

Three or four hours later, when the sun set again, Chen Chen stood up and stretched slightly.

The heat of the day faded little by little and the familiar power surged up in him again, permeating through Chen Chen’s body.

Chen Chen suddenly realized that he seemed to like the feeling of darkness arriving.

Even so, after night fell, he did not act immediately. Instead, he waited for a while in the castle. After sensing that no one had sneaked in, he walked out of the castle and approached the west gate where the black smoke had risen during the day.

This time, Chen Chen’s speed was not fast. He did not see until he walked to the gate that a bonfire was piled up on the ground in front of the western gate. At this time, the bonfire was extinguished and only a wisp of smoke was left. There was no sign of people cooking food or resting on the side.

As expected, the black smoke during the day was a trap.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen looked toward the surrounding houses. His Field energy swept through the houses without any blind spots and soon, he found clues in some rooms.

Chen Chen’s figure suddenly flickered and in the next second, he appeared on the first floor of that house, only to see a few scratches on the ground here. It seemed that someone in armor had lain here before and all the doors of the house were open. There were even signs that the locks were broken by force.

There was also an iron box in one of the rooms, but it had been opened by someone, and the object in it was gone.

One, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight...

Chen Chen found a total of eight traces in the surrounding houses.

If there were eight people, in other words, were these the two teams that he had sensed after killing the wizard and rogue?

At this time, Chen Chen already knew that there were four teams in this group of invaders. One of the teams was wiped out with only one wizard left and the other two teams were the eight people today. So now, it seemed, there was only one team left that Chen Chen had not encountered yet.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen had a faint intuition that maybe the Consciousness Stealer was hidden in the last team...

However, with its mere five points of intelligence, what would it do and why should it hide?

Chen Chen realized that he might be able to guess the thoughts of smart people, but he could not guess the thoughts of such monsters. No one knew what they were thinking.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen returned to the castle again but when he approached the castle, he suddenly got a strong whiff of blood. Chen Chen had never been so sensitive to the smell of blood, but now he was in the consciousness battlefield after all. Although the smell of blood was the same as usual, for Chen Chen, there was a hint of sweetness in this bloody stench.

However, this kind of diversion was not too tempting to the strong-willed Chen Chen. He flew straight to the castle and immediately saw a trace of blood flowing out of the inner floor of the castle.

Someone seemed to be injured.

Chen Chen glanced at the trap inside. Sure enough, in the brick above his head, all the spikes had flown out, and they were tightly nailed to the ground. At the same time, a few spikes were missing and on the ground, only a puddle of blood was left.

The track of blood trailed out from the castle, extending into the cluster of houses behind him.

Chen Chen’s expression flashed twice. He was about to chase after the blood trai, but once again he paused. “The spiked trap was launched from the top. If one was not prepared for it, one would die at once. It’s unlikely that someone would be injured but still survived, not to mention the amount of bleeding was not much... In other words, someone is setting up an ambush for this trap again?”

Thinking of this, Chen Chen’s mouth twitched into a smile. “Interesting...”

He did not follow the bloodstains directly but returned to the castle. At the same time, he stretched out his hand. The spikes popped out of the ground one by one, and then landed into Chen Chen’s palm like birds returning to their nests.

After that, Chen Chen walked to the other side of the second floor of the castle and vaulted over the window at the back.

At this time, his Field power was fully activated and he tried his best to detect signs of people around him. At the same time, he went around in a circle to explore the direction of the blood track from the side. Finally, after traveling over a hundred meters, he had reached a small square where a pile of bandages was left after being used to dress a wound.

In the surrounding houses of the small square, several adventurers were waiting quietly. Sure enough, this was still a trap.

Chen Chen first spotted two warriors with scruffy beards. One of them was only about 1.6 meters tall, but his head was twice as large as an ordinary person, with a big nose and two giant axes in his hands. Chen Chen immediately recognized that this was a dwarf warrior.

The other person was an ordinary human warrior and not a paladin because Chen Chen did not feel the threat of a paladin from his presence.

In a residential building near these two people, there was a man in black who was raring to go. He was dressed fully in black clothes, revealing only a pair of eyes, and he was not wearing any armor. Only two scimitars hung at his waist and he looked just like an assassin.

There were three of them in these two houses.

Chen Chen did not know where the remaining people were, but it seemed obvious. The remaining people were in the houses that surrounded the small square, but at this time, these people were acting as support by guarding the rear. It was impossible for Chen Chen to enter the small square to locate the remaining people, and if he continued to approach these people, he would be noticed by them.

Chen Chen was still keeping in mind the distance of 40 meters.

Unless Chen Chen could head in and slaughter them without attracting too much attention...

Thinking of this, Chen Chen immediately narrowed his eyes. He first selected the assassin in black and then he approached this opponent step by step. Although the limit of Field’s range was 50 meters, his control over Field at this distance was greatly diminished both in accuracy and strength.

Forty-five meters, forty-four meters, forty-three meters...

When Chen Chen was forty-three meters away from the black-clothed assassin, the assassin suddenly shuddered. He quickly stood up and was about to draw the scimitar at his waist but found that there was only air!

“Swish!”

There was a sound of metal slicing flesh and the black-clothed assassin only felt a chill at his neck. Following that, a large volume of blood sprayed from his neck!

“Hiss, hiss, hiss...”

The black-clothed assassin struggled constantly but he could not break free from the invisible shackles. He opened his mouth but his scimitar had already cut his throat, making him unable to utter a sound.

After half a minute, the black-clothed assassin had become limp, approaching death little by little.

Although Chen Chen was far away, he knew that there must be a bloody stench emanating from the scene and he had to kill as many as possible before others could smell the blood.

Thus, Chen Chen did not stop. He once again targeted the two warriors who were stationed together. At the same time, with a flick of his finger, four spikes flew toward the house of the two warriors at a leisurely speed.

“Hum...”

The four spikes were like four feathers. They drifted into the building through the gaps in the door and the window and silently hovered over the two people’s heads. Just then, Chen Chen raised his right hand, then abruptly closed it into a fist!

“Whiz!”

In an instant, the four spikes suddenly accelerated and penetrated the open eyes of the two at a lightning speed!

“Argh!”

“Aghh!!”

Two screams came from inside the house. Chen Chen did not proceed to kill them but immediately retreated and re-entered the shadows behind him.

“Roar!”

Sure enough, outside the small square, a furious cry suddenly burst out from one of the hidden buildings and then a warrior surrounded by a white halo rushed out. This soldier was brawnier than the paladin captain killed by Chen Chen. His silver-white halo was also more compact.

At this time, Chen Chen was already hiding in the shadows. He stoically watched the paladin rushing forward but when he saw the weapon wielded by the paladin, his pupils suddenly shrank.

The paladin was holding the simple longsword without any embellishments that Chen Chen had first discovered?

Thinking of this, Chen Chen’s expression darkened instantly. This longsword was prepared for the paladin and that was why it caused such grievous harm to Chen Chen.

Now, the longsword was found by his opponent, so the danger level had soared exponentially. Chen Chen was not even sure that his Field could stop the paladin’s slash!

chapters.title: Chapter 548: Return
chapters.body:
Chapter 548: Return

Chapter 548: Return

As Chen Chen beheld the humanoid creature constantly extending its tentacles outward, a harsh, piercing gaze appeared in his eyes. Despite his injuries, he forced himself to form a drill-shaped Field and launched it at the enemy at full strength!

“Psst!”

The Consciousness Stealer was blown up into a fine splatter of blood and flesh that painted the hall of the castle red!

“Huff... Huff...!”

Chen Chen stood in place, beginning to feel worn out by this point. He fixed his gaze at the mash of meat splattered all over the ground. Suddenly, they turned into countless spots of green light and flew toward him...

Chen Chen quickly stumbled backward and instinctively raised a barrier of Field to stop the advance of these strange spots of light. However, these strange objects did not seem to have a physical form and managed to effortlessly slip through his Field and fuse into his body!

The moment they fused into Chen Chen, he saw countless visions. He saw the luminous night sky, a vast field on an alien planet, and a glimpse of an enormous dark figure...

The magnificent figure rose over the horizon and loomed over the sky of the planet. Due to the alignment of the sun, he could barely make out the silhouette of the figure. It was a figure with what seemed like tens of thousands of tiny strands of tentacles crawling over its body, a monster made up of massive carpets of flesh and blood!

It drowned out the light of the sun. With just a glance, Chen Chen felt overwhelmed with a sense of despair and madness!

“What... Is that...”

“The Consciousness Battlefield has come to an end!”

A bright voice shattered the darkness and snapped Chen Chen awake as he heard the same voice continue declaring. “Participant Chen Chen, you’ve successfully devoured the opponent, prepare to return.”

Chen Chen began to relax upon hearing this familiar voice. He collapsed weakly onto the ground and tried to detect the changes in his consciousness. Before he had time to properly catch his breath, he suddenly noticed cracks forming out of thin air in front of him, like reality itself shattering and crumbling away. The sharp sound of breaking glass filled his ears.

Chen Chen rubbed his eyes in confusion and yet the strange phenomenon was still occurring. With one final break, the glass in front of him had completely shattered and his world was yet again plunged into complete darkness.

Something was not right!

Chen Chen was shocked by what was happening around him. The darkness filled him with a peculiar sense of dread that he could not identify the root cause of. It felt as if his very consciousness was split into two identical halves and somehow each half was feeling different things.

What was going on?

Chen Chen was suddenly filled with an extreme sense of tiredness. The moment he tried to sit up, he banged his head hard into the metal plate above, which immediately resulted in a bloodied head.

Chen Chen tried to rub the pain away and as he opened his eyes, he found himself in a suffocating, almost claustrophobic space.

After further inspection, he realized he was sleeping inside a space that seemed like the inside of a coffin. He was surrounded by metal walls with two rows of faint red LED lights along the sides of his body.

‘Am I inside a cryogenic chamber?’

Chen Chen looked around, puzzled by what was happening. He was having a harder time focusing as his mind was invaded by another sweeping sense of drowsiness. However, he refused to submit to this sleepiness. He realized that he was seeing two separate images at a time.

It felt as if one half of his consciousness was looking at a space faintly lit by two rows of dim red light while the other half of his consciousness was staring at the pitch-black, ominous void.

What was going on, was this caused by the Consciousness Battlefield?

Chen Chen was dismayed by this thought. Before he could further study his situation, another wave of sleepiness came over him.

Before long, Chen Chen had lost consciousness...

While this was happening, in the Spire Experimental Base faraway, the USB drive suddenly emitted a blinding white glow and began to melt away like a bar of soap while basking amid the blinding light!

By the time the light faded out, the shape of the USB drive was only a faint remembrance of what it once was. It continued to evaporate into thin air and by the time Little X tapped into the camera inside the charging room, the USB drive had completely vanished!

Nobody knew where the USB drive could have gone...

...

Seemingly out of nowhere, the strange virus that could potentially devastate the entirety of mankind had disappeared.

It was as if somebody pressed the pause button. The virus that was still spreading furiously had instantly disappeared. After disposing of the final batch of corpses, the remaining infected people managed to regain their sanity. Meanwhile, the ones who were identified as at risk of infection due to exposure were out of the woods as well since there were zero signs of unnatural observation when they were put into quarantine.

In the beginning, the Earth Federation was not certain that it was over. However, as time went on and they arrived at a month’s mark without any new cases of infection or deaths reported, everyone took it as a sign that the nightmare was finally over.

To ensure that the infection was truly gone, the Earth Federation made sure to check with the quantum computer, Adam. The answer Adam provided was simple and elaborate, being that mankind was currently not facing any potential world-ending crisis...

Adam’s reply was relayed to all the world leaders but most of them took the answer with a mixture of bitterness.

It seemed like Adam’s prophetic qualities were slowly degrading.

Nobody knew what the cause for this was, but what was happening here could not be any more clear. There seemed to be some sort of error with the quantum computer Adam, otherwise, it would have at least issued a warning regarding this crisis they faced instead of remaining silent until directly enquired.

With this, the terrible plague event which spanned for two and a half months, spreading all over the world and claiming more than three million lives in the process had finally come to an end.

The infectious disease went away as quickly as it came, without any forewarning.

Similarly, all of the Consciousness Stealer clones cultivated by countries all over the world from the meteorite protoplasmic liquid sample died a month prior. After that, nobody could create clones from the protoplasmic liquid anymore.

Even the protoplasmic liquid itself followed the trend of rapid deterioration and lost all of its potency.

While the world was busy celebrating the end of this plague, nobody noticed the sudden disappearance of a certain person who had been at the frontline, holding back the Consciousness Stealer all this while.

Life went on.

One month, two months, three months...

There was a beautiful girl who ritually sat next to a blue, metallic coffin and waited quietly by herself every day. She would curl herself into a ball and wait for the slightest bit of movement from within the coffin.

“Beep, beep, beep...”

The girl’s phone suddenly started to ring. She slowly opened her eyes and answered the call.

“Little Chen, it’s already so late, are you not coming home yet? Aren’t you going to at least check in with us?”

There was an aged and worn-out voice on the other end of the phone. Although the person on the phone sounded spent, her voice made her sound like she could not be anywhere older than thirty years old.

“Mom, I’ve been a bit busy recently. You take care of yourself with Dad, I’ll be back sometime soon...”

The girl answered, but the voice that came out of her lips was that of a man’s.

“Alright then, don’t stress yourself out now, child. Make sure to rest if you get tired. Your father misses you as well.”

The mother continued. “Look, we were to blame for what happened last night. We won’t force you to look for a partner anymore, please don’t be angry at us alright?”

“It’s no big deal.”

The girl smiled, her expression lucid. “Really.”

“That’s good...”

“Beep.”

The call ended.

With that, the girl gently set the phone on the ground next to her and continued waiting with her back against the coffin.

Nobody knew who or what she was waiting for...

“Thump...”

Just as she slowly closed her eyes and set herself to meditate against the chamber, she seemed to sense what could be the sound of faint knocking against the metal chamber.

“Thump, thump...,

It sounded like someone knocking against the inside of the chamber. She immediately widened her eyes and turned around to look at the chamber expectantly.

“Click, click!”

There was suddenly a light noise heard from inside the chamber as if someone had triggered a mechanism. Shortly after that, the lid of the chamber shook for a moment before it began to slide open!

“Ah!”

The girl exclaimed in surprise. She quickly went to look inside the chamber and saw a young man with his eyes squinted as he slowly lifted the cover. When the light from outside seeped into the chamber, the person inside shielded his eyes from the brightness of the sun.

“Sir Godfather!”

The girl could not be any more delighted to see this. She leaned over the chamber and became extremely emotional. “You’ve finally awakened from your slumber!”

Chen Chen was still trapped in a dizzy state. He only looked at the celebrating Little X and did not elicit any distinct response. His eyes were still colored with a palate of strangeness and confusion.

“Where am I...”

Chen Chen did not sit up. He held his hands in front of his face and inspected it as if he was looking at a never-before-seen curiosity.

“This is the teleportation hall.”

The girl froze slightly upon noting this remark and noticing Chen Chen’s foreign expression. She stumbled backward ever so slightly. “Are you Sir Godfather?”

“I am.”

Chen Chen finally set down his palm and looked at the slightly tense and unsettled Little X as his lips finally broke into a light smile. “Don’t worry, I’ve already devoured the Consciousness Stealer, you can rest easy.”

Little X nodded firmly, it was true. There were no longer any infected people in the world. This person she was facing could not be the Consciousness Stealer either.

Still, there was a strange look that lingered in Chen Chen’s eyes. He kept trying to blink away this occult feeling in his mind.

From his perspective, he seemed to be looking at two separate images.

One image was of himself opening the cover of the cryogenic chamber. He saw Little X beside himself and he saw the familiar ceiling of the teleportation hall.

Inside another image was nothing but the all-consuming darkness.

Chen Chen felt as if his consciousness was split into two clean halves. One half had fully awakened but the other half was still slumbering.

It was an extremely bizarre, unnatural feeling. It made him feel like he was losing grip of what was real and what was not, like the strands of reality and illusion were intertwined in an unexplainable manner.

“Sir Godfather, are you feeling any discomfort?”

Little X asked concerningly.

“No, it’s just...”

Chen Chen tried to elaborate but realized he did not know how to put what he was feeling into words. He decided it was better to not try to explain his predicament. Instead, he reached out to hold the edges of the cryogenic chamber and pulled himself up.

The moment he got up, he felt the touch of a foreign object on his left hand. He held it up and saw that it was none other than a pitch-black USB drive, one without any sparkling spots on it.

“The USB drive disappeared three months ago.”

Little X immediately noted. “At the time, I also wondered if the USB drive had returned to your hands again.”

“Three months? I’ve been sleeping for so long...”

Chen Chen frowned upon hearing this. This was not the first time the USB drive had disappeared suddenly so he was not notably fazed by the news. He stepped out of the cryogenic chamber, still feeling rather weak and tired.

After that, a host of Black Knights entered and escorted Chen Chen out of the teleportation hall and into the Spire Experimental Base.

After conducting a full body checkup on Chen Chen, it was quickly concluded that there were no notable changes in his body apart from a mild case of malnutrition.

Chen Chen was also informed that Hannibal, who was infected at the same time as he was, had awakened three months before him.

At the time, Hannibal was struggling holding himself together since his willpower was not anywhere close to Chen Chen’s. It was Little X who made the sound judgment of freezing him and waited to thaw him until after Chen Chen had completely devoured the Consciousness Stealer.

At this point, Hannibal had returned to operating at his usual post.

After being supplemented with nutrients, Chen Chen went to a sanatorium in the Spire Experimental Base. The three-month-long slumber had put his body into a state of hiatus and burnt out a significant amount of nutrients. His primary objective now was to regain these nutrients.

Lying on the soft bed, Chen Chen felt another wave of sleepiness sweeping over him. He closed his eyes and submitted himself to sleep...

Something strange happened to Chen Chen again. As soon as he fell asleep, another part of his consciousness jolted awake. He opened his eyes in a state of confusion and found himself back inside the cryogenic chamber instead of lying on top of a soft bed cover.

What was going on?

Chen Chen’s mind suddenly snapped awake with a grand realization.

“What’s going on? This is something that only occurs after a cryogenic sleep. Have I been sleeping for up to a century?”

Chen Chen was filled with uncertainty as he felt his mind plunged into a web. He could recall himself waking up earlier and stepping out of the cryogenic chamber. In that case, how did this explain his current situation?

Was it a dream?

Was what happened earlier a dream and was he back in reality now?

Chen Chen felt like he had lost the ability to form clear, concise thoughts in his mind. Just as he prepared to tap a button next to him to open the chamber, he heard rough rumbling coming from outside the chamber.

It sounded like someone was trying to forcefully tear open the cryogenic chamber...

Chen Chen frowned. The high-offset armor was the toughest known matter in the solar system. Unless directly hit by a nuclear bomb, it was impossible for it to be destroyed by any other destructive force, whether from the natural world or man-made. The only way it could be opened was from the inside. There was no chance that Little X did not know about this.

In other words, it was not Little X who was currently trying to open the cryogenic chamber from outside...

If it was not Little X, who could it be?

With this in mind, Chen Chen’s eyes began to fill with madness and craze...

chapters.title: Chapter 549: I Like Your Spirit
chapters.body:
Chapter 549: I Like Your Spirit

Chapter 549: I Like Your Spirit

On a vast dune formed of yellow sand, hundreds of thin figures could be seen digging away at ancient ruins.

Looking from above, one could see that they had dug up what seemed to be a rectangular structure. The structure was buried up to a hundred meters deep. Its steel structure had long corroded after being worn away by the sands of time.

This structure appeared to be a hall buried underground. Most of the dome had collapsed and buried deep underground. Only a small part of the dome remained clinging to the top of the structure.

The entire structure seemed to be made of steel, which seemed to be the aim of these people excavating the ruin.

“Shais, we must complete mining out this ancient ruin within three months.”

A man in a black robe with three gold rims wrapped around his waist remarked with a hoarse voice, “All the mined steel must be handed over to the high priest. You shall not emit any sinful thoughts of greed or else you’ll face the condemnation of the High Priest. Your body will be fed to God and your soul shall be plunged into the fiery depths of hell...”

The man in the black rob even had black gauze fabric covering his face. When the red desert wind blew in his direction, the fabric would flutter and briefly reveal his face underneath the fabric.

It was a hideous, disfigured face, filled with foul blisters and scars.

“We’ll follow the guidance of the High Priest and Master Deacon...”

Standing in front of this robed man was another man wearing a similar black robe. However, this man’s appearance was significantly fairer. He only had a small amount of lingering scars and blisters that he had almost completely recovered from.

This other man only had one gold rim around his robe. He bowed to show respect to the deacon.

“Hm, excellent.”

The deacon nodded, pleased with this reception. He then turned around and headed toward an airship nearby. There was another group of people all dressed in black robes next to him but unlike the laborers tasked with excavating the ancient ruin, these people were mighty, tall giants. The robes they wore could barely cover the bulging mass of muscles underneath. They were far taller than the average person with some of them standing up to three meters tall.

They looked like a group of brazen beasts.

“A pleasure, Deacon Armas...”

When the other man in black robe saw the deacon departing, he bowed respectfully again and smiled courteously to bid farewell to the deacon. The moment he lowered his head, a sudden cold glimmer appeared in his eyes.

Just as the deacon took another step toward the airship, he suddenly heard a round of urgent footsteps quickly approaching. A frail-looking man in a black robe approached. “Master Shais, the laborers... They, they found something strange!”

“Hm?”

The deacon stopped upon hearing of this. He slowly turned to address the frail-looking man in the black robe who approached Shais. “What did you guys dig up?”

“It, it’s something that looks like a coffin.”

The frail-looking man in the black robe sounded nervous. “It’s very heavy and sturdy. We couldn’t open it...”

Shais eyed the frail-looking man up and down, then turned around to address the deacon who seemed to have his interest piqued by this sudden discovery. “These laborers have no idea what they’re dealing with. Chances are it’s just some common mechanism in this ancient structure. I doubt it’s anything important at all...”

“Couldn’t we figure it out by just taking a look at whatever this item is?”

The deacon’s mouth twisted into a sly grimace as he began to head toward the direction of the ancient ruin. “Shais, you’re not trying to take possession of the High Priest’s tribute to God, are you?”

“Of, of course not!”

Shais immediately shook his head at the notion, then quietly trailed after the deacon.

Shortly after, they had arrived at the edges of the gigantic ruin. From this side, they could see that the ruin was the size of a regular football field. They also saw hundreds of laborers working tirelessly at the ruin. The laborers used a variety of steel tools to hammer away at the steel-framed structure of the ruin. At the moment, there was a large group gathered at the bottom section of the ruin. They seemed to be observing something.

“Tell them to carry that thing outside.”

The deacon ordered.

“Hurry, tell the laborers to take that thing over here and offer it to Master Deacon Armas!”

Shais quickly commanded.

Several large, muscular supervisors relayed Shais’ orders to the laborers below and similarly ordered them to carry the large metallic object from the bottom of the ruin to the surface.

“Huff... Huff...”

The yellow-faced laborers who were all skin and bones had to exhaust every ounce of strength left in their bodies to carry the item to the surface and present it to their masters.

Upon closer inspection, they noted that it was a metal object shaped like a coffin. The coffin was wide on the top and narrow on the bottom, and looked speckless and undamaged, looking as if the last several hundred years had gone completely unnoticed by it.

“Hm?”

The deacon noticed faint blue fluorescent light glowing from inside it and remarked gladly, “This must be something valuable. There’s no trace of corrosion despite being buried underneath the ruin for all these years. It must be made of powerful material that can be similarly used to produce high-quality weapons.”

“No need to rush just yet, Master Deacon Armas.”

Shais smiled. “This coffin-shaped object does seem like a box of some sort, which means that there must be something even more treasured hidden within. Perhaps this is the tomb of a royal in ancient times, there may be jewelry contained within...”

“Jewelry?”

The deacon squinted at the suggestion, his eyes shone with greed. “In that case, what are we waiting for? If it does contain jewels inside, you and I shall receive great blessings from the Divine after we offer it to the High Priest.”

“Yes.”

Shais turned to the previous frail-robed man and commanded, “Find some well-bodied laborers and have them pry this coffin open!”

“Yes!”

The frail-looking man nodded and quickly ran to the other side of the ruin and gathered several slightly larger and stronger laborers.

“You guys, open it together!”

The group of laborers was brought to the coffin and made to lift the lid off the metal coffin.

“One, two, three!”

They counted down together and tried to pry the lid off at the same time. However, no matter how hard they pushed or pulled at the lid, it would not budge at all.

“What’s going on, are your muscles for decoration?”

Shais frowned, visibly irritated by this poor display. Upon noting that the deacon was beginning to show signs of irritation as well, he immediately barked more orders, “Get the tools, pry it open with tools!”

The laborers stopped trying with brute force and went to procure crowbars from the other workers. They tried to jam the crowbars into tiny gaps in between the lid of the coffin to pry it open by force.

After ten minutes of this futile attempt, it was quickly evident that it was no good as well.

“Master, there are no identifiable openings in the coffin that allow us to fit the crowbars into...”

One of the laborers mustered the courage to inform the masters.

“Useless maggots!”

Shais ridiculed them and pushed them out of the way. He approached the coffin and began inspecting it. He noticed that most of the crowbars were bent and damaged at this point but there was not the slightest hint of a scratch formed on the surface of the coffin.

What a resilient material!

Shais could not help but be amused by this strange structure but he knew better than to let his amusement show. Still annoyed, he turned to the deacon and remarked, “Master Deacon Armas, take a look at this...”

“Hmph, I’ve never heard such a thing as a coffin that cannot be opened.”

The deacon smirked. “How about the chainsaw? Couldn’t this thing be cut open with a chainsaw?”

“Right, the chainsaw!”

Shais suddenly remembered about the chainsaw and slapped his head in a comedic manner. Shortly after, the chainsaw was brought over and this time, Shais decided that he would do it himself. He held up the old chainsaw and placed it over the midsection between the body of the coffin and its lid, and revved the chainsaw!

“Screech-!”

There was a harsh, deafening screech accompanied by brilliant sparks that sprayed in all directions.

Shais held the chainsaw over the coffin for what seemed like forever and still could not see the barest signs of damage on the coffin. When his hands could not bear the tremendous vibration of the chainsaw anymore, he finally let go. What he saw after that completely baffled him.

The parts of the coffin he sawed over with the chainsaw still looked brand new, but the saw teeth of the chainsaw, on the other hand, were completely shaven clean!

It was not just Shais, even Armas and the others observing were floored by the sight of this.

It could not be cut open with a chainsaw nor pried open with crowbars. What were they supposed to do about this thing?
