The Black Knight immediately followed him. “Sir Commander, what should we do with this group of people? Also, after this fierce battle across the entire city, many people are already thinking of escape.”

“Escape? Where would they escape to?”

Chen Chen asked.

“It may be Survivor City in the North Sea Continent or it may be Fantasy Night City also in the North Sea Continent.”

X-112 replied, “These two cities aren’t far away from us.”

Hearing this, Chen Chen could not help but hesitate and then said, “I’m here to take advantage of the strength of the Joint Resistance Army, so I can’t swoop in and take over just like that. In that case, for the sake of legitimacy and the maintenance of the city’s order, you have to prepare a public trial assembly.”

“A public trial assembly?”

X-112 tilted his head, seemingly puzzled.

“That means, I’ll judge them myself.”

Chen Chen gave a cold smirk. “Collect all the evidence of the crimes of this group of people and publicly expose all the crimes they have committed over the years. Don’t tell me that this group of people hasn’t committed any crime over more than 300 years?

“Of course, that’s impossible.”

X-112 shook his head immediately. “On the contrary, most of them have committed crimes, many, many crimes...”

“Then, what are you waiting for?”

Chen Chen replied and walked forward step by step, moving into the core of Blacklight City, the Blacklight Building, with the air of a victor.

chapters.title: Chapter 560: Trial
chapters.body:
Chapter 560: Trial

Chapter 560: Trial

The battle of Blacklight City shook up the entire Joint Resistance Army

Early the next morning, when Chen Chen woke up from Blacklight Fully-automated Medical Platform, he immediately saw a familiar girl appear in front of him.

This girl had breathtaking looks, with a hint of playfulness and a hint of obedience, and her hair was as smooth as silk. The moment he saw the girl, Chen Chen was in a daze.

“X?”

Then, Chen Chen frowned. The girl in front of him was naturally not X.

“This is X-112, Sir Commander.”

The girl lowered her head. “This body was X’s and it had been frozen like the Black Knights.”

Chen Chen nodded.

“Oh right, Sir Commander.”

X-112 continued. “The venue for the public trial assembly has been prepared. In terms of the Joint Resistance Army, the other four forces contacted us early in the morning. They have received the news that Blacklight City has been seized. Those people want to call you and hope that you’re taking the present situation seriously.”

“There’s no need for calls. I’ll naturally go to them in the future.”

Chen Chen asked coldly, “Anything else?”

“Also, your cousin Chen Shan and your cousin Chen Zheng who have settled down in Survivor City have also returned and they want to see you too.”

“Chen Shan and Chen Zheng?”

Chen Chen frowned, feeling a headache coming on. “So, are they also alive? They aren’t involved in the politics of Blacklight City?”

“No.”

X-112 shook her head. “Everything here was established by Chen Zeyu. In the beginning, they were expelled from the management core of Blacklight City. Chen Zheng also held a position in Blacklight City for a period of time but retired over two centuries ago. Most of those arrested today are descendants of Chen Zeyu and only very few are descendants of Chen Shan and Chen Zheng.”

“That’s good.”

Chen Chen nodded. “They must want to meet with me to intercede for a descendant or another. Tell them that I’ll punish those who should be punished, but I won’t kill those who don’t deserve to die, so they may rest assured.”

With that, Chen Chen waved X-112 away, went downstairs and got in the car, and headed toward the prepared venue for the public trial.

On the other side, in the prison near Blacklight City...

...

“Uncle Chen Zheng, Aunt Chen Shan, I beg you, speak to Uncle Chen Chen on our behalf so that we’ll be released! I’m your favorite, Xinghua, after all!”

Near a cell, Chen Zheng and Chen Shan were standing in front of the door and inside the cell was a middle-aged man with a pale face and a panicked face.

This middle-aged man was named Chen Xinghua and he was the youngest son of Chen Zeyu.

“Fool!”

There was a hint of anger in Chen Zheng’s eyes. “Everything you have is inherited from Blacklight Biotechnology and Blacklight Biotechnology was built single handedly by Chen Chen. You still wanted to hog it all? The core intelligence of the city all belonged to him, it’s just that he had not come back. Well, now that he has returned, how dare you?”

“Uncle, I was wrong...”

Chen Xinghua had tears of regret leaking from his eyes. “It’s not that I wanted to go against Uncle Chen Chen, but the family committee forced us to do it! Aunt Chen Shan, say something!”

“Sigh...”

Chen Shan shook her head. She was now dressed like a young woman except that she exuded a noble and elegant aura. “We haven’t seen Cousin Chen Chen for nearly four hundred years. I’m not sure if our words would still be effective.

“You could still try!”

Detecting the softening in Chen Shan’s words, Chen Xinghua burst into tears of joy. He put his face against the transparent square until it was deformed. “Also, my father is the brother of Uncle Chen Chen. Surely he won’t put us to death with no regard for that brotherly bond?”

Chen Shan was even more silent upon hearing this.

“Let me ask you something now and you must answer me honestly.”

Chen Zheng frowned and inquired, “After Chen Chen showed up, did you guys have any plans to kill him?”

“No, absolutely not!”

Chen Xinghua shook his head vigorously until his snot and tears were flung away. “I have absolutely no such thoughts!”

“Then you might have hope!”

Chen Zheng’s eyes only lit up now. He would still try his best to preserve some of the Chen family’s bloodline if he could.

A sorry look flashed across Chen Shan’s face too.

In the past three hundred years, their heirs had bloomed everywhere and naturally, they had watched some of them grow up. Chen Xinghua was the one they loved best.

When Chen Xinghua was young, he often visited the two of them. He was born smart and quick-witted. When he grew up, he was extremely filial and respectful to the two of them. The two grew even fonder of Chen Xinghua.

Today, Chen Xinghua was less than three hundred years old. He was still a child. The two elders naturally could not bear the thought that Chen Xinghua might be executed.

However, Chen Zheng and Chen Shan would not be unreasonable. They only intended to intercede for the younger generations of the Chen family. As for Chen Chen’s younger brother, Chen Zeyu, neither of them intended to intercede for his sake.

Besides the fact that Blacklight City daring to attack Chen Chen was thanks to Chen Zeyu’s instruction. The most important factor was that Chen Zeyu was Chen Chen’s younger brother after all. No matter how cruel Chen Chen was, he could not possibly kill his own younger brother, right?

In both of their opinions, Chen Chen would, at most, place Chen Zeyu under house arrest or demote him directly and make him an ordinary person.

Nevertheless, at this moment, the door of the prison suddenly opened and a group of Black Knights walked in with numb faces. They opened the doors of the cells one by one, brought out the Chen family members detained inside, and sent them to the prisoner car outside the prison.

“Uncle, Aunt! Save me!”

Chen Xinghua burst into tears and when the Black Knight led him away, he grabbed the edge of the gate with all his strength, displaying an imploring expression to the two of them.

“Cousin...”

Chen Shan could not help but grab Chen Zheng’s sleeves with a disconsolate look.

“Don’t worry, as long as you haven’t done anything to Chen Chen before, we’ll keep you safe even if we have to grovel on our knees!”

Chen Zheng resolutely said, “Come on, Cousin Chen Shan, let’s look for Chen Chen now!”

The two hurried to the Blacklight Building with hope, but when they arrived at the Blacklight Building, they were informed by X-112 that Chen Chen had left for the scene of the public trial.

The public trial assembly was arranged by X-112 to be held in the stadium of Blacklight City.

As they rushed to the stadium, they immediately saw the sound trucks on the street. These vehicles were fitted with loudspeakers and blasting a mechanical message.

“At 2 o’clock this afternoon, in the Blacklight Stadium, the new city lord will hold a public trial assembly. All the members of the Chen family are involved in the public trial. Citizens of the city are welcome to come and watch... At the same time, the Blacklight Channel will also broadcast the entire process. Stay tuned!”

It was not only the sound trucks but also the sirens everywhere in the city that were broadcasting this. To stabilize the order of the public trial assembly, X-112 even ordered an army to be dispatched to patrol the city.

Hearing that the trial turned out to be judging the lofty Chen family in Blacklight City, countless residents immediately rushed to the streets, gleefully racing in the direction of the stadium. All at once, the entire town had shown up.

At this time, the roads near the stadium had been closed. If anyone wanted to enter, they could only go on foot. However, after an hour, the stadium that could accommodate over ten thousand people was already full. Thus, X-112 set up a holographic projection device outside the stadium, allowing those outside to see the scene inside the stadium directly on the big screen.

When Chen Zheng and Chen Shan arrived, the road was blocked by the flow of people. They could only search for soldiers nearby, show their identities, and ask the army to deploy a helicopter to transport them to the venue.

Still, when the two arrived, the time was already ten past two in the afternoon, and the trial assembly was commencing...

At the scene of the assembly, Chen Chen walked to the podium step by step. He first glanced around the crowd, then, without any opening remarks nor ceremony, he just said, “Now, the public trial assembly shall begin!”

At Chen Chen’s order, more than a thousand men and women with pathetic expressions were led by the army to the rostrum of the stadium. Most of these people were tender-skinned and at first glance, they appeared pampered, in sharp contrast with the ordinary people in the city.

Ordinary people were of course not qualified to use the Blacklight Fully-automated Medical Platform and because of the shortage of resources in these apocalyptic times, the residents in the city did not have access to the pre-fusion age-reversal treatments. Therefore, the life span of ordinary people was still only 70 or 80 years.

On the other hand, the Chen family seemed to be standing high up in the clouds overlooking all living beings. They could live more than three hundred years old if they liked. With such a contrast, the positions of the two sides appeared to be in extreme opposition but the Chen family maintained a strong modern force, so riots rarely occurred.

Moreover, Chen Zeyu did have some skills. He was able to establish this city through solid governance. Achievements were rewarded and mistakes were punished. A special family rule system had also been established. Therefore, although there had been countless murky and unjust cases in the family during the three hundred years, most people could still survive, and because of this, the order in Blacklight City had always been stable.

Nonetheless, for X-112, as long as anyone was involved in a fraudulent or unjustified case, it could be tracked down.

At this time, the Chen family stood in front of the rostrum under the watch of the Black Knights and the army. Some of these people looked dazed, some had spite in their eyes, and some were weeping in a low voice. After they appeared, the entire stadium was so quiet for a while that one could hear a pin drop.

The three-hundred-year rule of the Chen family had long been deeply rooted in the hearts of the people. Although they had fallen from their pedestals at this time, the people still dared not act too brazenly for fear of retaliation.

At this moment, Chen Chen’s gaze swept across the venue like a god and then he glanced at Chen Zeyu who was standing at the forefront. However, at this moment, there was a sudden rush of footsteps and Chen Chen’s Field swept behind him. He immediately saw the anxious expressions of Chen Zheng and Chen Shan.

“Stop them!”

Chen Chen waved his hand. The Black Knight on the side stopped the two of them and they could only worry helplessly.

Ignoring the desperate shouts of the two, Chen Chen said solemnly, “Chen Xinghua, step out!”

The two Black Knights immediately marched Chen Xinghua to the forefront of the crowd.

“Chen Xinghua, the third mayor of Blacklight City. He has taken office for fifty-three years.”

Chen Chen spoke somberly, his voice directly transmitted over the entire stadium through the microphone. “Since you were an adult, to satisfy your lust, you have made many secret arrangements and forcibly seized 86 young women, leading to their family’s destruction. In the past three hundred years, more than a thousand lives have been ended by you directly and indirectly. Do you plead guilty?”

“Wah!”

A violent clamor broke out among the crowd in the auditorium because the things Chen Chen had said were a huge secret, so they did not even know all this.

“No, I don’t, I’ve been wronged, Second Uncle!”

Chen Xinghua suddenly sobbed out loudly. “I was wronged. If you don’t believe me, ask my father or ask my uncle and aunt!”

“Hmm?”

Chen Chen narrowed his eyes when he heard these words and a murderous intent gleamed in his eyes. “You dare to deny it. Do you think the urban intelligent control system is just for decoration?”

As he spoke, Chen Chen waved his hand, and on the big screen next to the rostrum, segments of surveillance records began to play. There were text labels under these records, indicating what happened in which year and month.

However, after this was played for dozens of seconds, Chen Xinghua’s face was ashen.

Faced with the ironclad facts, Chen Xinghua finally could not keep up his denial. Chen Zheng and Chen Shan who were in the background also stood there in stunned silence. Although they also knew that Chen Xinghua’s hands were not clean, they did not expect that he had killed this many.

“Relying on your position in the Chen family, you acted in derangement without regard for law or morality!”

Chen Chen’s voice boomed. “Murder means a death penalty. I sentence you to death! This shall be carried out immediately on the spot!”

“No... No, not this!”

Seeing the two Black Knights walking to the front with guns, Chen Xinghua trembled all over and came back to his senses again. His face was gripped by extreme fright. “Chen Chen, I’m your nephew! Dad, Dad, come and save me!”

Chen Zeyu could not help closing his eyes, with a look of agony.

The Black Knights ignored Chen Xinghua’s torrent of pleas. One of the Black Knights, with a casual push, just made him kneel to the ground while the other Black Knight picked up the pulse rifle and pressed it coarsely against Chen Xinghua’s forehead.

“Dad... Uncle, Aunt...”

Chen Xinghua’s eyes were dazed. He stared at the muzzle in front of him, his entire body trembled like a wheat stalk. Some people even smelled of feces and urine.

“Bang!”

With a gunshot, Chen Xinghua’s skull flew back and a large volume of white and red substance splashed all over the Chen family behind him!

“Whoosh!”

Suddenly, countless people burst into a tsunami-like commotion throughout the venue. The residents of Blacklight City had never thought that the Chen family, who was originally so lofty, would die in front of them so easily at this time, and executed by a firing squad, no less!

It was known that even the death penalty in Blacklight City only included lethal injection. Moreover, they rarely heard of the Chen family being punished. This notion had been crystallized in their heads over three hundred years. The Chen family was like the royal family in the feudal period. They were existences beyond that of ordinary people!

Now, their framework of understanding was completely shattered with this gunshot!

At this moment, countless people converged their gazes upon the figure on the podium. He was a slender young man who seemed to be only in his twenties but it was this person who appeared to be from the Chen family himself, yet was able to execute a family member mercilessly!

Some people have a gleam of panic in their eyes but more people had a glint of excitement and a touch of fanaticism in their eyes!

Still, just as the crowd burst into a tsunami-like uproar, Chen Chen continued to speak. “Chen Lin, step out!”

The two Black Knights were expressionless as they dragged out a plump middle-aged man.

“You even killed your nephew, you’ll die a miserable death!”

As if knowing his fate, Chen Lin, who was in charge of the internal affairs of the entire Blacklight City, had a mad look in his eyes. He shouted, “Chen Chen, you’ll die a...”

“Snap!”

Before he finished speaking, a Black Knight immediately pinched Chen Lin’s chin. There was a clean “snap” and Chen Lin’s chin was directly removed, resulting in a dislocated jaw.

“Chen Lin, 98 years old, Secretary-General of the Interior Affairs of Blacklight City.”

Facing the curses of his family, Chen Chen’s eyes still did not waver. He said with a calm expression, “During thirteen years in office, due to your selfish desires, 58 civilians were slaughtered secretly and 36 people were killed to be silenced. Death penalty, execute him immediately!”

“Agh—!”

Up to this point, even though Chen Lin had had his chin dislocated, he still unleashed a heartrending roar with a wild expression, the last display of madness in his lifetime!

“Bang!”

There was another gunshot and Chen Lin’s voice came to an abrupt end. His bloated and plump body slowly fell to the ground. At this moment, a flash of despair finally appeared in the eyes of all the Chen family members present.

chapters.title: Chapter 561: Time Machine
chapters.body:
Chapter 561: Time Machine

Chapter 561: Time Machine

“Chen Zhankui, Chen Shengtian, Chen Zhuowei, Chen Fengxi, Chen Zihan...”

As Chen Chen spoke mercilessly, one by one, the members of the Chen family, whose bodies had gone limp, were dragged out like dead dogs by the Black Knights.

“Chen Zhankui, to sell your goods to the forces of the Joint Resistance Army, you wantonly killed off competitors. What a heinous crime, the death penalty!

“Chen Shengtian, as the director of the Military Aircraft Department, you’ve embezzled military salaries, amounting to tens of millions of the Joint Resistance Army’s currency, causing many soldiers to freeze to death. Death penalty!

“Chen Zhuowei, death penalty!

“Chen Fengxi, you raped women frequently, death penalty!

“Chen Zihan, you covered up the crime for your brother and killed many citizens out of jealousy. Death penalty, to be executed immediately!”

Chen Chen’s voice was like the commanding voice of Hades. Every time he called out a name, a Black Knight rushed forward, dragged that person out of the Chen family members, and then killed the individual on the spot like slaughtering a pig!

“No, don’t kill my daughter!”

However, when the two Black Knights dragged forth a young girl with a beautiful face, a middle-aged woman on the side immediately broke down. She wailed and hugged her daughter, then suddenly leaned down and kept kowtowing to Chen Chen, not even noticing when there was bleeding on her forehead. “Grandpa Chen Chen, Grandpa Chen Chen, I beg you, don’t kill Han’er, she’s your great-granddaughter!”

“Pfff!”

Still, the Black Knights on both sides did not care at all. One of them directly took up the handle of a gun and hit the woman’s head severely. At once, the woman fell on the ground silently and a large pool of blood flowed out from under her...

“Mom!”

The beautiful girl suddenly let out a heart-piercing cry.

The two Black Knights continued to grab the girl’s hair and drag her down the podium.

At this time, the clearing was already in a mess. Endless blood and white brain tissue were mixed together and several corpses had heads that exploded. The girl was dragged here and then one of the Black Knights aimed at the girl’s head.

At this time, the girl’s body was trembling violently. A terrified gleam shone in her eyes and she murmured unconsciously, “I want to go home...”

“Bang!”

Her headless body dropped down!

At this time, the entire venue had become tense and silent.

The masses in the stands were no longer in an uproar. Even those who were full of hatred for the Chen family were completely shocked by Chen Chen’s merciless killing. This scene was now deeply imprinted in their minds and became an indelible sight.

Gradually, the corpses were accumulated. More than two hundred corpses had been heaped up under the rostrum. A breeze swirled by and the strong smell of blood diffused and filled the entire stadium.

In the stands, many people were already vomiting.

Nonetheless, Chen Chen watched all this with a cool expression. He lowered his head and looked at the last person on the death penalty list. That person was none other than his brother, Chen Zeyu.

“Chen Zeyu... Death penalty!”

Chen Chen spoke slowly, spitting out the last few words.

Chen Zeyu closed his eyes in anguish. His head had become covered with white hair in one night and he looked completely different from the young Chen Chen on the podium at this time.

Now, Chen Zeyu had watched as his children and grandchildren were slaughtered wantonly but he could not do anything. A rush of immense resentment filled his heart.

Watching the two Black Knights coming forward, Chen Zeyu’s eyes flashed madly and he suddenly screamed, “Chen Chen, I curse you!”

“May you be lonely forever, abandoned by your wife and children!

“May you die a long, miserable death after a long, miserable life!”

While Chen Zeyu unleashed a heartrending roar, he was dragged to the corpse pile by the Black Knight but he still glared at Chen Chen with eager hatred. “Chen Chen, you’ll die a miserable death!!!”

“Bang...”

Chen Zeyu’s bitter voice was cut off abruptly.

As if he was a little tired, Chen Chen tossed the list in his hand to a clerk on the side. The clerk quivered all over and caught the list shakily, giving Chen Chen a frightened glance.

Seeing Chen Chen nod, he stammered, “All those on the death penalty list have been executed. Next, those who won’t receive the death penalty...”

As the clerk said, he began to read out the names and the sentences the prisoners should receive. These sentences ranged from one year to one hundred years, and those with more than one hundred years were life imprisonment.

After the entire afternoon had passed, finally, the public trial assembly came to an end. This also represented that Chen Chen was officially stationed in Blacklight City as the lord of the city.

As for those positions that had been vacated, Chen Chen asked X-112 to start recruiting for people throughout the city to make good use of their capabilities. Surely, after having seen this scene where the Chen family, who was once so lofty, had fallen off their pedestals, these people would not dare to stuff their own pockets for decades.

On the following day, the other four forces of the Joint Resistance Army began to tentatively contact Chen Chen. Chen Chen harbored no other intentions here. He told them that the previous offensive and defensive alliance was still in force, but when they asked him questions about his psionic abilities, he avoided the topic.

A few days later, Chen Chen went to sleep again under the strict protection of the Black Knights.

When Chen Chen regained consciousness, he had returned to reality again.

“Sir Godfather.”

X appeared beside Chen Chen’s bed. “You’ve been sleeping for about a week. Have you gained anything in that parallel dimension?”

“More or less.”

Chen Chen nodded. “I regained control of Blacklight City. In addition to that, I’ve learned new uses of psionic energy.”

With that, Chen Chen talked to Little X about transforming himself into the G-virus form again and went to the laboratory to transform himself.

After this transformation, several tentacles appeared on Chen Chen’s hands again. These tentacles seemed to grow from the palms and they could be retracted in a controlled manner.

Even in this state of transformation, Chen Chen could still suck the flesh and blood of his prey. After he devoured a few domestic pigs that had been prepared, when he transformed back again, his body remained healthy and lost a great amount of fat.

“This is a complete transformation ability.”

Little X looked delighted and surprised. “This isn’t only the power of the G-virus but more importantly, the power derived from psionic energy. It’s incredible. Although this psionic energy isn’t as capable of manipulating electromagnetic force as Field energy, its performance in self-preservation exceeds that of Field energy.”

“Indeed, psionic energy and Field energy can be said to have their own strengths.”

Chen Chen nodded. “The most terrifying thing is that I can portion out a minute part of the power and bury a psionic seed in others so that they can obtain a kind of secondary psionic power. If I die later, I’ll be able to be reborn in their body.”

“Isn’t this an imitation of the Consciousness Stealer?”

Little X was immediately surprised. “It’s no wonder that Consciousness Stealers can infect human spirits and be resurrected out of human corpses. Now you’re practically a replica of the Consciousness Stealer and moreover, you’re even stronger!”

“This isn’t the time to talk about these.”

Chen Chen shook his head. “I’m considering whether I want to transfer my power from reality to completely solve the trouble of the Consciousness Stealer.”

“Can it be done?”

Little X was a little doubtful. “If we could go up against that Consciousness Stealer, that world wouldn’t have appeared in the first place.

“Despite this, we were only defeated by the Consciousness Stealer because we were caught off guard that time.”

Chen Chen thought for a while and said, “If I were not the first to be infected, humans wouldn’t have been so passive. If we think about it carefully, we still have a lot of trump cards that we haven’t used.”

“Then how do we get to that dimension?”

Little X asked again, her eyes spinning. She figured it out immediately after seeing Chen Chen’s smiling eyes and laughed. “By using Terminator!”

“Yes, Terminator!”

Chen Chen nodded. “If there are any props in all of science fiction cinema that can carry out cross-dimension teleportation, it’s the time machine in Terminator.”

Chen Chen had thought about the teleportation device he should use. Only “quantum mechanics” and “M-theory” could resolve the so-called “grandmother’s paradox” and the reason for this was due to the existence of parallel universes.

The entire universe was diversified and the multiverse itself was composed of countless parallel universes while parallel universes branched out from a certain original universe and were both similar and different from the original universe.

All the movie dimensions accessed by Chen Chen through the USB drive were parallel universes. This had been confirmed. In other words, the time machine of the Terminator dimension did not send people back to the past but instead sent people to a parallel universe that was almost the same as the original universe.

The timeline of a parallel universe was different from reality, being either in the past or the future, which resolved the grandmother’s paradox. Namely, the time teleportation device of the Terminator dimension had the ability to transport people to parallel worlds.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen immediately got up and went to the charging room. After getting the USB flash drive, he opened it with a laptop and dragged the movie Terminator Genisys into it.

Terminator Genisys connected the first and second movies, but the plot was a bit complicated and many people could not understand it. Aside from the plot, the true form of the time machine in this movie was revealed for the first time. It was a huge device whose size was comparable to a hill. After the humans broke into the Skynet headquarters, they happened to see T-800 being teleported back to the past to assassinate Connor’s mother.

However, more than one time machine appeared in the movie. In the middle of the movie, Connor’s mother and T-850 made another time machine device together. This device was much smaller. The only thing it lacked was a very powerful chip.

Still, this problem was solved by removing a chip from a T-800.

Moreover, when the two people were working together to make the time machine, the year was 1984. That was to say, if the production technology of this manual version of the time machine could be obtained, Chen Chen was also fully capable of replicating it.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen waited no longer. He directly opened the movie in the USB flash drive, chose the first mode of the USB flash drive, and directly extracted the time teleportation device.

“Boom!”

With a soft thud, a device appeared in front of Chen Chen. It was rough in workmanship and looked like a round podium.

This round podium had exposed wires, rough cuts, and no outer shell structure, so the parts inside were directly exposed to the air.

No one would believe that just such a simple device could allow people to travel through time and space and reach another parallel universe.

As for the missing chip, it was not a problem. Chen Chen could mass-produce the T-800 chip. He would not have to extract another T-800 just to source the chip.

However...

Little X came up skipping. She fiddled with the device in doubt, set the timer to 378 years later, and then looked at Chen Chen with a face of disbelief. “Is this all there is to it? Isn’t it too sloppy?”

“Of course not.”

Chen Chen shook his head. “With a device like this, who the h*ll knows where I’ll end up. I don’t dare to take this risk.”

“Then how do we get to the other space-time where you are?”

X asked suspiciously.

“By setting up another teleportation machine, of course.”

Chen Chen said instinctively, “Even the Gantz sphere from Gantz and the Ark from Doom need two point-to-point teleporters. I think the time machine from Terminator should be the same.

“Although this wasn’t shown in the movie, it can be seen from the generality of the teleportation in the movie that the teleportation’s destination is uncertain. So I think that if we want to teleport accurately, point-to-point teleportation is imperative.”

After speaking, Chen Chen looked at the time machine in front of him. “Take this device apart. I believe that upon disassembly, some useful data will be obtained. Even if I don’t understand the principles it uses, I can memorize these things by rote. Then I’ll go back to that world and assemble another time machine.”

Little X suddenly realized what Chen Chen had in mind.

Indeed, as long as this was the case, it was equivalent to the time machine existing in both worlds of Chen Chen. The forces under Blacklight Biotechnology could then reach the other world by teleporting there.

Therefore, their next task to take apart the time machine and use reverse engineering to analyze performance was handed over to Little X. Chen Chen took advantage of this opportunity to return home. He had been asleep for three months after defeating the Consciousness Stealer, which meant that he had not been home for more than three months. His parents must have been waiting anxiously for a long time.

Besides, Chen Chen was so eager to go home not only for the above reasons but also because of what he had felt in that world.

In that world, Chen Chen had failed to protect his parents and eventually caused them to die in the catastrophe.

One place was like heaven while the other was like hell. The different experiences of the two worlds caused Chen Chen to have mixed feelings and he cherished his time with his parents even more.

At the same time, it also inspired Chen Chen to formulate several sets of retreat plans in advance. If such a world-destroying event occurred in the future, Chen Chen’s parents would retreat to a certain safe place as soon as possible according to the plan, without Chen Chen having to worry.

As for where that safe place was, Chen Chen believed that Mars was one option and alternate dimensions were another.

In other words, Chen Chen must wipe out all dangers in the dimension where the Divine Fusion Empire was located and use it as his own backup base and also as a refuge.

chapters.title: Chapter 562: Blacklight Foundation’S Antarctic Main Base
chapters.body:
Chapter 562: Blacklight Foundation’S Antarctic Main Base

Chapter 562: Blacklight Foundation’s Antarctic Main Base

When Chen Chen reappeared in Eco Science City, he immediately attracted countless prying “eyes”.

In the beginning, a rumor spread among the senior leaders of the Earth Federation, claiming that the chairman of Blacklight Biotechnology was also infected with that kind of fusion virus and was not likely to last long.

When this news reached the ears of the major forces and even some secret factions, they did not believe in it too much at first.

After all, Chen Chen practically had a miraculous golden touch. From the beginning of his business to the present, he was like a legend and life sciences were his specialty. It was almost impossible for such a person to be infected by the fusion virus.

However, later, as the Consciousness Stealer’s virus suddenly disappeared, even Chen Chen stopped showing up and did not attend several Lilith Assemblies. Suddenly, many rumors started drifting around.

Before entering the consciousness battlefield, Chen Chen told Little X that if he did not return for a long time, she could use a clone to take his place. After all, there were still several Chen Chen clones in the Spire Lab.

It was just that Little X was not resigned to doing this. She firmly believed that Chen Chen would win, so she had never done that.

Nonetheless, now that Chen Chen had appeared in his home, this rumor collapsed by itself. Many forces that had begun to shift restlessly now retracted their claws and fangs, and were quietly waiting for the next opportunity...

If it were not for fear of the civilization-level lethal weapons that Chen Chen might possess, how could these countries tolerate the continuous erosion of their interests by Blacklight Biotechnology and even smile at this opponent?

A few hours later, Chen Chen bade farewell to his parents and walked out of the house. He glanced around his parents’ house. The smile in his eyes gradually dissipated and was replaced by an inexplicable coldness.

“X, this group of ‘eyes’ near my parents should also be cleared away.”

Chen Chen said.

“Understood, please be rest assured. Before the sun rises tomorrow morning, I’ll clean up these spies who dared to approach your parents.”

X’s voice came from the headset in reply.

This casual exchange between the two had quickly determined the life and death of over a hundred spies.

“Oh, right!”

Little X’s voice suddenly said again, “During your sleep, the Earth Federation has established a department that contacts the Blacklight Foundation, called the Federal Special Branch. This department is also divided into the Chinese division, Northern American division, North Sea Continent division, and European Division.

“This department works as a fast track, which can accelerate the operations of the Blacklight Foundation. If events such as ‘wormholes in the sea’, ‘Weeping Angels’, and ‘Consciousness Stealers’ that are enough to threaten human civilization occur in the future, this department will contact the local authorities as soon as possible so that the local government can cooperate with the Blacklight Foundation to the fullest extent.”

“Very good, the Federal Special Branch, you said?”

Chen Chen nodded with satisfaction. “With this federal official department, the Blacklight Foundation will be authorized to operate around the globe...”

“One more thing.”

Little X continued to report. “The construction of the main base for the Blacklight Foundation in Antarctica is about to be completed and the current progress has reached 90%. The engineers we sent are updating us every day and the construction will be completed by the end of this year.”

“Very well done.”

Chen Chen nodded. “Tomorrow I plan to go there in person and visit.”

“You want to eliminate the unfavorable rumors of your disappearance.”

Little X guessed. “That wouldn’t be a problem, I’ll make an appointment for you now.”

“Good.”

Following that, Chen Chen returned to his office in the Blacklight Building, sat there for a while, then returned to the Spire and began to dismantle the time machine with Little X.

The next day, Chen Chen boarded a plane and headed toward Northern America. He transferred to a military plane in the Washington area of Northern America and flew to the South Pole.

The trip took more than ten hours. As the plane landed, there was already a dark world of ice and snow outside the window.

The polar circle was divided into polar day and polar night. The date at this time was 1 April 2029, which was during the polar night in the South Pole.

The polar day and polar night were due to the angle of divergence of the earth’s rotation. During a fixed period, sunlight would either be unable to reach this area at all or shine brightly throughout the day. From 23 September to 21 March every year, Antarctica was shrouded in night, with no sun in sight even during the day whereas the North Pole was the opposite.

Chen Chen took a C-17 military transport plane, coming along with the flight that transported materials from Northern America to the South Pole. When he walked off the plane wearing a down jacket, the top personnel from the four major continents immediately came to meet him personally.

“Your Excellency Chen Chen.”

The respective personnel of the four continents stood in a row and Chen Chen stepped forward and shook hands with the four.

“Welcome, Your Excellency Chen Chen, to this inspection. I’m your main guide. My name is Zhao Xi.”

As Chen Chen’s relationship with China was the best, the main person escorting him this time was the highest person in charge from China at the main base. Zhao Xi was a middle-aged man who looked relatively older, about 50 years old, and Chen Chen could tell that he had not received any age-reversal treatment, thus maintaining the true appearance of his age.

“Mr. Zhao Xi, have you never undergone age-reversal therapy?”

Chen Chen could not help asking.

“It’s mainly because this technology is still somewhat risky.”

Zhao Xi smiled. “Although the treatment you created has a high success rate, if the treatment fails, one will have to rest for several years to regain one’s vitality. Therefore, I’ve never done it, but I’ve already made an appointment for the Blacklight Fully-automated Medical Platform you gave us. I’ll be able to use that machine to rehabilitate my body in the second half of next year.”

Chen Chen nodded. He followed Zhao Xi to a deep and huge underground cave. There was an astonishing amount of goods beside the underground cave. At this time, dozens of cranes were constantly transporting the goods.

“As you can see, this is the headquarters of the Blacklight Foundation.”

Looking at the huge deep pit in front of him, Zhao Xi’s eyes had an emotional glimmer. “This place has been under construction for four years and it has been jointly developed by the four continents, using hundreds of billions of dollars worth of manpower and material resources. All this is enough to build the Pyramid of Khufu dozens of times over.

“By now, the massive structure of the headquarters has been completed and all that’s left is to fill in the details. I can’t imagine how we have made it through the past four years...”

Zhao Xi could not help but sigh.

“All this is for the continuation of human civilization.”

Chen Chen nodded. He knew that even if the Spire Experimental Base was compared to the base in front of him, it was practically a mere trinket. After all, this was built as the last fortress of civilization.

Whether in terms of the amount of engineering invested or the manpower and material resources invested, it was in a different league.

While speaking, Chen Chen and the few people accompanying him had already reached an elevator by the edge of the big pit and they walked in.

As the elevator doors closed, without having to press any buttons, the elevator started to descend sharply.

“At present, we only need two minutes to reach the base which is two kilometers down.”

Zhao Xi notified.

Chen Chen only felt the sensation of a rapid fall. The elevator began to accelerate continuously, from 2 to 3 meters per second to more than 20 meters per second. This speed was already close to the speed of a free fall. If one fell at such a speed, even an alien could not survive.

However, when it was about to reach the ground, the elevator began to slow down gradually and there was a rapid sense of gravity returning. Those with poor physiques would likely have thrown up by now. It was only because they were all in good shape that they could bear this.

“Ding!”

As the elevator arrived at the destination, there was only a soft ring and the elevator doors opened again. This time, a huge hall was revealed before Chen Chen.

The hall seemed to be unfinished now. A large hole with a diameter of more than 50 meters was still in the center. At this time, goods were being transported down the hole continuously and there was a busy flow of people here. Nevertheless, most of them were not living people but androids and XTN engineering robots.

“It’s your first time here, so I’ll explain everything in more detail.”

Zhao Xi narrated, “This is the first floor of the main base and the largest activity venue in the main hall. The area of this floor alone will reach one million square meters, which is the size of over a hundred football fields. It will be transformed into an event plaza and a pedestrian street, where everyone can exercise and shop.”

Chen Chen nodded. Indeed, this floor alone had exceeded the area of the Spire Experimental Base.

“This is the pedestrian street...

“This is the shopping area...

“Here is the stadium. Whether it’s track and field events or football and basketball, you can find a venue here...”

The group accompanied Chen Chen around. From Chen Chen’s point of view, it looked like a miniature city and the dome above his head was also extremely high, measuring more than 20 meters above him. If optical equipment was installed, manufacturing a blue sky and white clouds, many people would probably find it difficult to tell that it was artificial.

It was not until more than half an hour later that they finished touring the first floor. They came to the elevator in another corner, preparing to head toward the second floor.

“The entire base is structured like a top and the areas below will gradually shrink. The second floor is only two-thirds the area of the first floor. That will house the living supplies and warehouse areas, which are used to store supplies for daily living.”

Zhao Xi emphasized with some pride. “The materials loaded on the second floor can enable 30,000 people to live here without going out for over a hundred years.”

“A hundred years isn’t that long.”

When Chen Chen heard this, he could not help frowning.

“Of course.”

Zhao Xi nodded. “But if that time comes, we’ll renovate the first floor, transforming those gardens and green areas into test fields for soilless agriculture. At that point, we’ll naturally be able to obtain more resources.”

With that, they had reached the second floor.

Chen Chen noticed that it took twenty seconds to go from the first floor to the second floor.

“This is because the first floor and the second floor aren’t close to each other.”

Sensing Chen Chen’s puzzlement, Zhao Xi continued to explain, “The distance between the first floor and the second floor is over a hundred meters. Even if we lose the first floor, we can blow it up and retreat to the second floor to continue the defense. Strictly speaking, every floor in the base can abandon the floor above and exist independently. This is the function of a doomsday fortress.”

“That’s a good idea.”

Chen Chen nodded in approval.

Then, they descended again and came to the third floor.

As the elevator doors opened, Chen Chen’s eyes were met with column-shaped buildings that looked like fortresses. These buildings stretched to the top of the third floor and there were probably over a hundred of them.

“This floor is the staff dormitory.”

Zhao Xi went on to explain, “We calculated this for a long time. At first, we wanted to fill the third floor with regular buildings as found in a conventional design, but in the end, we gave that up because that kind of dormitory was still too depressing. It’s difficult for people who don’t see the sky all year round to withstand this pressure, so we chose this kind of storied unit building similar to a residential structure.

“There are probably over a hundred-unit buildings, each of which has four floors. The inside will have one of two layouts: one bedroom and one living room, or two bedrooms and one living room. This floor can accommodate a total of 20,000 people. When they look out the window, they’ll see greenery and parks all over the ground instead of cold metal passages.”

They led Chen Chen into one of the buildings, checking out the layout of the rooms inside. In Chen Chen’s view, this was a lively place indeed and he even saw ponds and man-made rivers in the distance.

The top of the dome could also simulate normal sunlight and night, just as if they were on the ground surface.

“From the fourth floor onward, it’s the work area.”

After visiting the third floor, Zhao Xi led Chen Chen onto the elevator to the fourth floor, then the fifth and sixth floors, until the bottom ninth floor.

The entire base had an inverted pyramid layout, with the areas becoming smaller as they went down, but the ninth floor broke this pattern.

The area of the ninth floor was even larger than that of the first floor. There were not only various manufacturing workshops, but also the main control room that controlled the entire main base, and a nuclear fusion reactor.

“At the bottom level, we want to build a heavy industrial site where we can produce the necessities of life that everyone needs, even munitions. At the same time, it’s also the core area of power, which provides electricity and air circulation for the entire base. Unless the first eight floors are lost, this place is invulnerable to attack.”

Looking at the vast ninth floor in front of him, Chen Chen could not help wearing a look of admiration. He suddenly looked at the rails and trains by the side of the ninth floor, then raised his eyebrows. “This is?”

“This is a minecart.”

Zhao Xi explained, “The ninth floor doesn’t exist for its own sake. We used tunnel boring machines to excavate several underground mines at a depth of more than 3,000 meters. These minecarts connect to those mines. If we want to seal off the base in the future, then our resources will naturally be tapped from the ground.

“Plus, some minecarts even lead to underground lakes and can collect underground freshwater resources...”

Chen Chen was even more amazed when he heard these words. He continued his visit for more than an hour before he returned to the surface.

This visit lasted nearly eight hours and his guides were terribly exhausted by the end. Only Chen Chen’s face remained as usual without any change at all.

When Chen Chen was sent off, the few of them exchanged a few casual words, then said goodbye and left.

They were going to report to their higher-ups regarding Chen Chen’s reactions throughout his visit to the main base, without leaving out a single detail.

chapters.title: Chapter 563: L Compound
chapters.body:
Chapter 563: L Compound

Chapter 563: L Compound

The following days were relatively peaceful and quiet again.

The reverse research on the time machine would take at least several months. Over this duration, Chen Chen would routinely head over to the other world to see if any problems had arisen. Fortunately, nothing major had ever come up and he would simply return to his slumber shortly after.

Furthermore, Chen Chen took the cryogenic chamber he left at Shais’ back to the Blacklight City.

Meanwhile, he continued spending most of his time in the quiet room.

Chen Chen placed the quiet room underneath the Indian Ocean. There was nothing much he did in this remote, isolated place apart from honing his Field. Ever since Field melded with the psionic energy, it had gotten rougher around the edges. Chen Chen once was able to magnify into microscopic view up to a thousand times using Field and this value had declined to only eight hundred times now.

As far as Chen Chen was concerned, the forte of Field was not only in its ability to affect substances but also in its ability to grant him microscopic observation.

If applied correctly, Chen Chen could apply Field to such a fine extent that he could alter the anatomical structure of substances. This way, he could alter the shape and properties of certain objects and perform feats such as reducing a rock back to soil or alter the chemical bonds of specific substances.

Going beyond this, Chen Chen could sharpen his accuracy to attain a level of control over atoms which would make it even more powerful. The electromagnetic force between the atoms could be destroyed with a simple wave of his hands and with that, any form of metal or whatever enemy stood before him would be disintegrated into nothingness almost effortlessly.

Further exemplifying this ability would be to delve into the layer of the cell nucleus, which would allow Chen Chen to achieve far greater things. He could generate nuclear fusion phenomena at will or gain complete control over quantum tides, even alter vacuum spaces. At that point, he would be able to fabricate artificial wormholes to cross the universe at will.

However, such extreme precision would require a microscopic magnification of at least two million times. Chen Chen still had a long way to go before he could achieve it.

“Phew!”

Chen Chen suddenly let out a long, dragged-out sigh and opened his eyes while still in his meditation pose.

The moment his eyelids opened, the eyes underneath them seemed to spark with a jolt of electricity. They emitted bright sparks amid the darkness. His deep concentration seemed to have materialized and took form.

“Looks like it’s finally restored to its previous condition...”

Chen Chen shook his head as the spark in his eyes started to dwindle again. He stood up to exercise his joints, then suddenly frowned.

Over this period, the level of his Field had taken a tremendous leap then eventually lost its original momentum again.

It was the same as what he had experienced previously. After Chen Chen’s Field reached the plateau, he found himself in a bottleneck phase where it was extremely difficult to develop it any further.

Chen Chen’s DNA structure had also become saturated to such a point where any absorption of crystalline would not make a difference at this point.

“It seems like I have to either search for alternatives or take another approach and begin to aim for precise control over Field instead.”

Chen Chen shook his head, clearly not satisfied. At his current level, standing up against the Consciousness Stealer would be nigh impossible. After all, the Consciousness Stealer’s psionic energy was a culmination of billions of its victim’s life force.

Chen Chen left the quiet room still pondering about this and returned to the Spire using the Gantz black sphere.

“Sir Godfather, it looks like you’re not in a good mood today.”

Little X pointed out almost immediately as soon as she saw Chen Chen.

Chen Chen nodded and remarked, “It’s the problem with Field, I’m trying to figure out how I can further develop it so I can face the Consciousness Stealer.”

“Hmm...”

Little X pondered for a moment. “I have a good idea, but that will depend on if you’re willing to take such an approach.”

“Intrigue me.”

Chen Chen said, tilting his head to the side.

“If the Consciousness Stealer attained its psionic powers by accumulating the life force of billions of humans, then you should be able to do the same as well.”

Little X noted. “You can infect the people in that world using similar methods and then absorb them. That way, your psionic power would improve dramatically. Since your psionic powers had fused with Field, raising one should improve the other simultaneously, wouldn’t it?”

Chen Chen chuckled upon hearing this suggestion. “I wouldn’t say this cannot be done but it looks like my powers are slightly weaker.”

“After my Field fused with the psionic power, I’ve been sensing some sort of mutation in the ability. I noticed that I cannot infect human consciousness the way the Consciousness Stealer does. To do so, I need to first implant a ‘psionic seed’ just like what I did with Niuceus Laman.”

“I have an alternative for you.”

Little X remarked. “If you cannot infect them using psionic powers, there’s another way we can approach this.”

“Another way?”

Chen Chen was unsure of what to make of this.

“Back when I was performing a blood test on you, I discovered a special element in your blood. The academic name that I have labeled it with is the psionic factor or L factor for short. This new element should be the result of your Field and psionic energy fusing and just like the protoplasmic fluid of the Consciousness Stealer, this L factor has infectious qualities as well!”

“Really?”

Chen Chen was surprised to hear of this. If that was true, would that not essentially make him another Consciousness Stealer as well?

“Well, this infectious quality is mostly dormant and would not impose its effects under usual circumstances. However, I can do something to alter its qualities.”

Little X went on. “After I perform a string of complex procedures, I may be able to extract the L factor and further develop it into a virus variant. Although this virus cannot transmit itself telepathically like the Consciousness Stealer’s, it can be manually injected which will alter the structure of other human’s DNA.”

“Must the subject be humans?”

Chen Chen frowned. Though he was not opposed to the idea of sacrificing a small portion of the human population to preserve the rest of mankind in the long run, there was still a certain limit to how far he was willing to go.

“Of course, why else would the Consciousness Stealer choose humans instead of other creatures?”

Little X snickered and said.

Chen Chen chuckled at this silly remark of his as well. It was evident that human beings were the only high-level intelligent life so far. Even ten thousand pigs and dogs combined could not match the level of consciousness possessed by a single human. Therefore, infecting humans was by far the best choice.

“Human beings living a high-level intelligent life is just one of the deciding factors.”

Little X described, “At the end of the day, a similar DNA template would be required for an effective blood infection. The infection rate of other species may be less than 0.1%.”

Hmmm...

Chen Chen appeared ruminative upon hearing this. “If this is how it has to be, then I suppose it’s worth a shot but something else came to my mind... What you are saying is that the developed virus would be the equivalent of scattering psionic seeds into a Field and allowing others to gain psionic powers, is that it?”

“That’s right, that’s the idea.”

Little X nodded.

A flash appeared in Chen Chen’s eyes. “In that case, there are other uses we can come up with for this virus...”

...

The biotechnology advent of Blacklight Biotechnology had reached such unprecedented heights that even the Umbrella Corporation or the Weyland-Yutani Corporation would have to bow down before its greatness. It only took a few days before Chen Chen obtained a tube of the referred virus from Little X.

It was a transparent vial containing the dark-blue substance.

The vial the virus was packed in was the same as the one used to slot the G virus. With a gentle tap, a needle would be summoned at its end and allow for effortless injection directly into a person’s veins.

The contents of the vial were none other than the L factor essence extracted from Chen Chen’s blood.

“A hundred millimeters of your blood can produce roughly eighty vials of the L factor essence.”

Little X tabulated. “For your current physical condition, you can easily afford to have a hundred millimeters of blood drawn out daily without any issues. It’s a healthy ratio.”

Chen Chen was slightly disturbed by this mention. “X, you’re saying this like I’m supposed to be some kind of experiment subject.”

With that being said, Chen Chen still silently ran through the numbers in his mind. If he made eighty vials of this virus a day, then it would total to almost two thousand and five hundred a month, and close to thirty thousand a year. It was not a jarring number but a century’s accumulated effort would produce three million vials.

At least it was better than the alternative of implanting psionic seed into others directly. At the end of the day, Chen Chen was not the Consciousness Stealer and constantly implanting the psionic seed into others would gradually weaken his Field and cause it to deteriorate eventually.

Chen Chen could also extract larger volumes of blood to increase production, but that would be at the expense of his health.

What came after that was the exciting-as-usual experiment phase. All that planning would amount to nothing if this virus turned out to be ineffective after all.

A felon was escorted into a common laboratory.

As Delta-level subjects in God’s Forbidden Zone, these people were accommodated with well-prepared food and supervised training to keep them in the healthiest state at all times. The subjects brought in were always well-built like professional athletes.

With that said, all the muscles in the world would not help them when faced with the Black Knights. The subject was fixed on a stretcher by two Black Knights without any room for resistance. Then, he was forced to wait for what was to come next.

“What are you trying to do?”

There was a distinct tremor in the felon’s voice as he exclaimed. He surveyed his surroundings and saw an expressionless person approaching him with an array of instruments. It did not take long before he found himself strapped down with a collection of devices such as an electrocardiograph, electric shock device, ventilator, and so on.

The subject became increasingly anxious.

It was around this point when one of the staff in a large white lab coat approached him with a deep blue vial of something. The subject put up a futile attempt of trying to wring himself out of the stretcher when he saw the vial. His instincts told him that it could not be anything other than bad news.

“Tss!”

All the subject heard was the sound of air pressure produced by a syringe being held down. There was not much pain in the arm. He only felt a slight chill where he felt the needle plunged inside that made him shiver.

The blue substance was subsequently pumped into the subject’s body through his veins.

The subject witnessed all this with great agitation, his heart thumping violently against his chest and he asked, “What is this, what did you people inject into me? Is this a human experiment?”

For all the questions that he threw at them, not a single personnel in the laboratory bothered with entertaining him and continued along with their tasks.

Unbeknownst to the subject, they were all Androids. The expendable positions in the Spire had long been replaced by Androids at this point.

Meanwhile, Chen Chen resided in the command room on the second floor and looked at the data feed of the experiment subject. There was a visible increase in the subject’s blood pressure following the injection.

The life force of the subject was being exhausted at a rapid pace.

Meanwhile, the subject’s body temperature began to soar sky-high as well. His breathing became frantic and within ten minutes, his body had become stark red like a boiled lobster!

“The nutrients in the subject’s body are dissolving rapidly!”

Little X turned to look at Chen Chen. “Sir Godfather, should we inject nutrients for him?”

“No need to rush.”

Chen Chen waved his hand and said decisively, “I need to see if it can be supported by the human body without any external assistance. This drug has to be circulated in large volumes in the future and if each injection requires facilitation, then this virus is effectively useless.”

“Oh...”

Having understood Chen Chen’s angle, Little X nodded.

The subject’s body temperature continued to rise. From what was regular temperature at the beginning, it had gradually risen to forty-one degrees. At this temperature, his vitals were at a critical state and his heart thumped violently like a dysfunctional piston. He was fading in and out at this point and the muscles of his entire body twitched uncontrollably.

Still, Chen Chen merely observed this without the slightest intention of helping.

What was strange was that despite these exaggerated physiological changes in the subject’s body, his heart and lung functions were preserved all the same. In addition, they had only grown stronger throughout the process. His heart was thumping with frightful strength at this point.

“Here’s the turning point!”

Little X sensed the same as well and she glowed up as she exclaimed, “It seems the L factor is working and is beginning to infiltrate his DNA structure. This kind of mutation is similar to that of a virus infection...”

The subject’s breathing and heartbeat slowly reverted to normal as Little X said this. His body temperature soon decreased as well, along with his blood pressure.

“Looks like it’s successful?”

Chen Chen rose from his seat and approached the observation window to peer at the floor below. The felon subject was now in a comatose state.

An Android stepped forward and drew a fresh blood sample from the subject and injected it into a nearby device for inspection.

“As expected, there’s been a change in his DNA!’

Little X remarked in awe, “I’ve detected many shifts in his DNA that weren’t there originally, but I haven’t detected the L factor in his blood. This means that you don’t have to worry because although the subject is a carrier of the L factor, it’s not contagious in any manner.”

“Now, let’s hope that he displays the symptoms I hope for.”

After saying that, Chen Chen shut his eyes.

The moment he shut his eyes, Chen Chen sensed something in a faint corner of his mind. He leaned in closer to get a better impression of what it was and sensed that it was an unfamiliar source of conscience. He realized that all he had to do was reach in closer to absorb this conscience and render it as feed for himself to grow stronger.

This was not the first time he had sensed something like this. The first time was when he had granted the Duke of Laman a psionic seed.

More importantly, this injection was the direct equivalent to Chen Chen having planted the psionic seed in this subject. With this, the subject had also attained a supernatural source of power...

Chen Chen slowly opened his eyes and glanced at the expectant Little X.

“Good work.”

Chen Chen praised. “I can feel his presence from here. I can’t believe it only took you one try to obtain a complete product.”

“In that case, we shall perform another batch of experiments to obtain further experimental data.”

Little X smiled sweetly. “We should also name this substance.”

“Name?”

Chen Chen considered for a moment and soon snapped his fingers. “Since it contains the L factor extracted from my blood, how about we name it the ‘L compound’?”

...

chapters.title: Chapter 564: Superpower Emergence
chapters.body:
Chapter 564: Superpower Emergence

Chapter 564: Superpower Emergence

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

Chen Chen only came up with this name because he thought of a TV series he once watched called The Boys.

The backdrop of the TV series was that it was a world where humans and superhumans coexisted in the same society. The civilization of the world was identical to the real world and humans with superpowers were treated just the same as any other commoners would be.

In a world where superpowers were commonplace, there existed individuals with superpowers but did not possess any proper skills to make a proper living and afford rent at all.

There was a financial company known as Vought International that specialized in making celebrities out of people with superpowers. They would dedicate their efforts to branding selected individuals into international stars.

With the influence of Vought International, superhumans no longer had to be reclusive hermits bound to the downtown area, but international celebrities boasting millions of fans. They would participate in business activities arranged by the company and star in reality shows and talk shows, and even in their TV dramas. They were in an entirely separate category of celebrities.

They did occasionally participate in crime-fighting, promoting the agenda of upholding justice, but it was mostly done to upkeep their picture-perfect image. At the core, they were merely products of capitalism sold under the guise of heroism.

Chen Chen had taken a similar approach to penetrate the entertainment market by branding the combat police as the BHB Combative Police Force or otherwise known as the bionic combat police.

Chen Chen gave disabled, retired policemen another chance at glory by transforming them into robocops and had them enforce the law on behalf of local policemen. He would then use this opportunity to create the image of superheroes around these noble, once-retired police forces.

Chen Chen did not do this out of interest in the revenue in the entertainment industry since he had no shortage of funds. All he wanted was to normalize the use of mechanical law enforcement.

As long as the public was in favor of using robots to enforce the law, he could eventually monopolize the police force in many local districts and turn the local police force into a robotic force headed by Blacklight Biotechnology.

Chen Chen’s long-term plan was slowly paying off. With the four major continents endorsing the bionic police, many other regions in Earth Federation began purchasing the XTN robots produced by Blacklight Biotechnology. The use of these XTN robots eventually extended into the army as well.

Over the years, the production of the XTN robot series under Blacklight Biotechnology had been optimized to create a stable military industry chain. It brought Blacklight Biotechnology tens of billions of dollars in profits every year.

Back to the topic at hand, the reason Chen Chen named the drug as L compound was that he had considered the implications of it.

Being injected with this drug was equivalent to having Chen Chen’s psionic seed implanted into a person. The person would receive the gift of psionic powers, but Chen Chen could also use the subject as nourishment for his growth to improve his psionic and Field strength.

This quickly gave rise to another problem. Only roughly thirty-thousand vials of this L compound could be created yearly, which meant thirty-thousand humans with psionic powers. Chen Chen quickly realized the logistic problem being that he did not have enough subjects.

The reason Chen Chen used felons as experiment subjects in the first place was that it was far more affordable than cloning humans. Clones took at least half a year and millions of dollars to fully mature. Meanwhile, acquiring felons from other countries would only cost somewhere in the range of ten thousand to a hundred thousand.

Even after all these years, Chen Chen had only expended several thousand felons for his many unethical types of research. Considering that most countries, especially the influential continents would not put a price tag on their citizens, his reliable source of felons had always been a handful of closely connected countries near Namibia.

Some other small countries or revolutionary armed forces were willing to sell as well, but their subjects were not death row criminals, but the local, poor people from their slum who were brought in forcefully. Naturally, Chen Chen would not accept this.

Chen Chen would not be so shameless as to act like a Nobel saint, but even he would not stoop to such a point of intruding on the basis of human morality.

Since Chen Chen wanted to raise his Field potency but wanted to avoid claiming the lives of the innocent, he naturally arrived at this idea – to build a utopia where commoners and people with superpowers could coexist.

This was something that Chen Chen had given a considerable amount of thought to as well. With the ever-increasing frequency and severity of dangers faced, only improving the technological standards of the world was no longer enough. In this case, strengthening mankind as a whole was a viable route.

A tactful approach had to be taken to achieve this. Things like T-virus, G-virus, and the black goo were ruled out since they were weapons of mass destruction and may be the catalyst to the all-out implosion of mankind. Moreover, it would not be used responsibly by mankind.

Apart from these options, the C24 chromosome was not viable as well since it was just as dangerous as the T-virus.

After ruling out the unappealing options, the L compound was what remained.

Chen Chen planned to use this thing to manufacture the first batch of superhumans. The most critical component for this working out was that there had to be at least a ten percent chance for the L factor in their genes to be inherited by future generations.

This meant that there had to be a certain possibility that the offspring of the people injected with the L compound would be born with psionic powers.

Using this method, there would be more humans with psionic powers in time.

Meanwhile, the lucky ones blessed with psionic power could hone their strength over time. The stronger they became, the more valuable they were when Chen Chen eventually decided to fuse their consciousness with his.

However, Chen Chen decided that he would not absorb the humans with psionic powers, at least not until they died of natural accidents or old age. When that happened, the psionic power they had spent all their lives honing would be submitted to Chen Chen and nourish his psionic growth...

Just like what the Consciousness Stealer did.

This was Chen Chen’s ultimate plan.

Compared to the Consciousness Stealer’s ruthless approach, Chen Chen’s was far more humane comparatively. After several centuries, as more psionicists passed on, Chen Chen’s psionic power would grow exponentially.

Another decisive factor was that Chen Chen had access to two worlds for him to carry out his plans simultaneously...

“The total population in the real world borders around eight billion people. Meanwhile, due to circumstances, the parallel world only has a population of around a billion people.”

A month later, Chen Chen looked at the two thousand vials of L compound packaged together in the first round of production, a pensive look came upon him. “The brainless Black Knights are good for nothing. It does appear that this strategy applied in the real world is a far more reliable approach.”

“Of course!”

Little X chimed in. “Since you’re going for the long haul, well-thought-out approaches like this will always be better. Besides, psionic powers will be greatly beneficial for mankind since they’ll be able to defend themselves in times of crisis.”

“I’m more concerned about social order.”

Chen Chen shook his head. “The existence of psionic power will inevitably bring about social turmoil. I’m worried about the potential of society spiraling out of control because of this, it will not be any good for either Blacklight Biotechnology or human civilization as a whole if something like that were to happen.”

“If that’s what you’re concerned about, we can always start small first.”

Little X advised. “It’ll be like dripping a drop of ink in the ocean. Couldn’t we avoid violent turbulence if we start with making very minor and subtle changes?”

Chen Chen chuckled upon hearing this. “That’s true, a mere drop of ink in the ocean will be almost unnoticeable and in time, we can begin pouring in more ink gradually... So be it, let’s start with releasing a hundred vials of L compound vials during the first month, then we can tweak the numbers from there on.”

“Hm!”

Little X responded, “I think you should be more concerned about the ongoing ‘Mankind Anti-aging Project’. The average human lifespan is long enough as is and is expected to surpass a hundred years very soon. This means that your psionic powers harvesting efficiency will be similarly affected.”

“That’s alright, I can afford the wait.”

Chen Chen shrugged, a faint spark appeared in his eyes as he said this, “The Consciousness Stealer has been slumbering for several centuries and is expected to continue to do so. The worst that can happen is that it wakes up earlier than we expect it to, in which case I can simply abandon the parallel world and retrieve my body from there. The point now is that the significance of this plan working out is more important than the mission of killing the Consciousness Stealer.”

“Understood!”

Little X saw the point Chen Chen was trying to make. It seemed that the discovery of the L compound had changed their trajectory and inspired Chen Chen to set up a thousand-year-plan. Compared to this, the original task of killing the Consciousness Stealer was not as important anymore...

...

John Oppenheim stood in front of a mirror. Greeting him was his reflection, a handsome-looking young man who wore a dazed expression.

John Oppenheim was an ordinary blue-collar worker. He went to an ordinary university and lived an ordinary post-graduate life. He worked in an electronic company as a programmer.

This was the way his life was meant to be, an ordinary lifestyle until the day he died. However, everything changed for him on the day a meteorite fell from the sky...

It happened almost half a year ago.

Half a year ago, a meteorite merely the size of a regular fist landed in his backyard in the middle of the night. When he picked it up upon discovering the next morning, his hand was plastered with an unknown liquid.

The unknown liquid came from within the meteorite.

Shortly after that, John began having strange dream sequences. Before he managed to make sense of these cryptic dreams of his, the military came to his doorstep.

Dozens of military members clad in intimidating protective suits came to his house and took him away. He was then taken to a concentration camp.

Just like that, John was locked up for three whole months.

The entire time he was in the concentration camp, he was not informed of what was happening. However, he had a suspicion that it must have had something to do with the meteorite he came into contact with.

Just as John thought he was doomed to spend the rest of his life in a foreign concentration camp, the military suddenly put him on a plane and took him back to his city.

Shortly after that, the military issued a notice and told him and many others that they had contracted a lethal and highly infectious disease, which was why they had to be quarantined for the last three months.

The good news was that they were the lucky ones that managed to survive it. This terrible plague the meteorite brought had claimed close to two million victims worldwide in the meantime.

Although John Oppenheim came out the other end of the tunnel with his life intact, he was still haunted by weeks of repetitive nightmares. The freakish isolation experiment he experienced had traumatized him and nearly pushed him to the edge of insanity. He only managed to recover from it quite recently.

When John woke up on a certain day, he immediately sensed that something was off.

Everything that happened last night seemed like a mirage. He had the slightest recollection of how he managed to get home after work and how he went back to his bed. Furthermore, there was an aching numbness coming from the back of his neck.

However, even after rubbing his hands over his neck to inspect it, he could not find anything noteworthy.

Unbeknownst to John, there was a tiny red dot on the part where he felt the sting, damage left behind by a needle head...

After that, he looked at the time and saw that it was time to head to the office. Although he managed to keep his life over the last eventful half-year, he did lose his job in the process. It took him two months before he finally landed another job.

For someone like John who was close to his thirties at this point, it was not easy to stumble into this job. He quickly shaved off his patchy stubble and threw himself into a suit before hurrying off to work.

John got into his car in a hurry and drove toward the city center. His house was located in the suburbs, the most common single-family home seen in Northern America. Such houses were extremely common in the suburbs of Northern America, known for their generous space and affordable prices. Most people living in Northern America lived in such houses as well.

It was as much a blessing as it was a curse for him since this meant John had to drive an hour to the city center every day to work. His original plan was to find an apartment in the city nearby the office but failed to find an apartment that satisfied his requirements.

This was the disadvantage of living in the suburbs. After half an hour had passed, John found himself gazing helplessly at the endlessly backed up highway. When the traffic light at the intersection finally turned green, he immediately flicked the turn signal and swung the steering wheel in another direction so he could cut into another lane.

John planned to take an alternative route that should help him shave some time off this lengthy commute.

Unfortunately, the moment he spun the steering wheel and swerved into another lane, he suddenly felt his car quaking quite violently. He heard the all-too-familiar sound of an abrupt crash and he quickly turned around to verify that the back of his car had been hit by a pickup truck.

“Blasted!”

John furiously clapped the steering wheel while spitting profanities.

“You complete idiot, don’t you know how to drive!”

The enraged owner of the pickup truck got out and took rapid, purposeful stomps toward John.

It was a textbook redneck. He had a large turban wrapped around his head to hide his bald spot and a giant patch of beard that would put most dwarves to shame. The lower half of his face and his bulging nose were burning with red.

Irritated, John stepped out of his car as well and made a big show of waving his arms about. “There’s a massive traffic jam ahead and I’m just trying to take a detour, can’t you see me putting on my signal?”

“Little white boy, when did you put on your signal lights?”

The redneck roared. John could immediately smell the strong scent of alcohol.

“Woah, woah, woah...”

John held his arms in front of him in a defensive position and stumbled backward innocently. “Sir, could you not get so close to me. Also, it would be nice if you could be more civil...”
