chapters.title: Chapter 586: An Additional Person
chapters.body:
Chapter 586: An Additional Person

Chapter 586: An Additional Person

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

“Where would the electronic and paper documents of the space warp technology be kept?”

Chen Chen searched through some offices and thought of invading NASA’s intranet in the same way as before. Unfortunately, the computers in these offices were all linked to external networks. Chen Chen had searched for a long time and found no trace of the intranet.

If he could not access the intranet, naturally, he could not find the data on the space warp engine.

Chen Chen noticed that the sky was gradually turning pale with daylight as groups of employees walked toward the building. He knew that if he continued to linger here, he would be discovered.

Although it was not a big deal even if he were discovered, NASA would become more vigilant and guard their secrets more fiercely.

Thus, Chen Chen had to turn around and retreat, leaving the NASA headquarters building.

“Sir Commander, what should we do next?”

X-112 asked, “The space warp engine technology has become the top secret of NASA and even the entire Northern American Continent. I’m afraid that few of those in the entire senior management would know about it and it will be difficult to obtain data if we don’t use some special methods.”

“No rush.”

Chen Chen frowned. “According to the timeline, the plot of the movie has just ended not long ago. Although the destruction of this world is close at hand, all of Earth should still be safe as long as the rescue spacecraft hasn’t returned.

“We have a safety period of about fifty days or so. No matter what, this much time is enough for us to get the space warp engine technology... So, we’ll just do what we always do.”

As he spoke, Chen Chen spread out his hands and a God chip as small as a fingernail appeared in his palm...

...

NASA’s full name was the Northern American Space Agency and the director’s name was Frank.

Frank felt that something strange had happened on the Event Horizon —

Every six hours, he checked the footage sent from the Event Horizon but the image only showed people in the two cryogenic chambers while the rest of the cryogenic chambers were empty.

Frank was thoroughly puzzled by this scene.

“The distress record sent back clearly indicated that three people survived but why are there only two people in the cryogenic chambers?”

Frank felt that his heart was going to burst from anxiety. “Where’s the remaining person? Why didn’t all of them enter the cryogenic chamber?”

He did not know and he did not understand. It would take at least fifty days for the spaceship to return to Earth from Pluto, which meant that no rescue would happen within fifty days. Then, in the bow of the Event Horizon, without any supplies, how was the remaining person going to survive?

Frank wanted to contact them but all the messages he sent out vanished into the void without a response.

In this ambiguous state of affairs, he had been at the headquarters for three days. Finally, he could not stand it anymore and left his work unit.

The full-time driver drove him back to his home. Frank only felt groggy in his head. He set the next alarm directly and lay down on the bed, falling into a deep sleep with no regard for his family’s inquiries.

In the middle of the night, a dark shadow slowly descended from the sky and landed on the lawn of the villa in the suburbs of Washington.

“Click!”

There were a few bright flashes and the black shadow walked into the villa as if there were no one around. Behind him were a few housekeepers who stood rooted to the spot with blank eyes.

“This is it.”

Chen Chen stood in the lobby of the villa, looked up to the second floor, then walked directly in and saw the old man Frank still sleeping in his bed.

The smell in the room was a little filthy, with a faint odor that was unique to the elderly.

“Click.”

After he locked the door casually, Chen Chen took out several medical tools from the bundle he had carried and stuck one of the needles into the old man’s slack arm in front of him.

“Hiss?”

Frank was awakened by the feeling of being pierced by the needle. He blankly looked at the strange man beside the bed. He wanted to exclaim in alarm but found that no matter how he tried, there was no sound coming from his throat.

“You’re very tired now, very tired...”

Chen Chen and the old man gazed at each other. Suddenly, Frank only felt that the eyes of the man in front of him were like two dark bottomless pools. He stared dazedly and then an irresistible wave of sleepiness struck him again.

This time, Frank passed out completely.

At this, Chen Chen nodded in satisfaction. Chen Chen turned the old man over so that the old man was lying on his stomach in bed while Chen Chen picked up the scalpel next to him and started to cut proficiently...

Within a few minutes of work, the old man’s cervical vertebrae were exposed to the air.

Chen Chen used medical cotton to absorb the blood from the old man’s neck and then implanted the God chip into it. After doing all this, he took out tweezers and other tools again to patch back the old man’s skin layer by layer.

He glued the skin using a bio-adhesive. As he bonded layer after layer, soon there was only a one-centimeter-long red mark on the back of the old man’s neck.

Then, Chen Chen took out a bottle of spray again and sprayed it on the old man’s wound.

This spray could activate human stem cells and accelerate wound healing. In reality, it was a military-grade strategic material. After its use, the wound of the old man would disappear completely within two or three days.

After doing all this, Chen Chen turned and left without any delay.

It was not until noon the next day that Frank woke up lazily. He only felt a swollen ache in his head. His body was numb and powerless as if he had experienced a hangover.

“What’s the matter, my body...”

Frank sat up weakly, then tilted his head, and suddenly felt a tingling pain in the back of his neck.

“Hiss...”

The piercing ache even made him inhale sharply.

Frank hurriedly touched the back of his neck, but he could not feel anything. It was just that the skin was aching faintly. As he pressed down gently, the sharp tingling sensation grew stronger.

“Huh?”

Frank stood up a little bewilderedly and walked straight to the bathroom. In the bathroom, he held up a round mirror and at the same time, with the help of the mirror in front of the sink, he saw that there was a light red scratch on the back of his neck.

“When did I get hurt?”

Frank was a little puzzled but then he cast the matter aside because the entire NASA had been going crazy recently, and he had more important work to do.

Thus, after a hurried breakfast at home, Frank had no time to accompany his family before he went to NASA headquarters again, picked up by the full-time driver.

“We still haven’t made contact with the crew member who didn’t enter the cryogenic chamber?”

As soon as he entered the command hall, Frank asked quickly.

“Mr. Director, we have not.”

The group of researchers with bloodshot eyes, who had stayed up all night, immediately replied. Compared to Frank, this group of researchers had to work even harder and many of them had not been home for a week.

“Why hasn’t the person gone in yet?”

Frank only felt a little confused. After so many days, even if someone had not entered the cryogenic chamber at first, they should enter now, unless that person was not on the Event Horizon at all...

However, he did not know that at this moment, everything he saw in his eyes and everything he heard in his ears was observed by Chen Chen in secret.

“It turns out that the Event Horizon began its return four or five days ago...”

Chen Chen sat in a temporarily rented apartment and silently looked at the screen in front of him. The image on the screen was composed of two circles. The two circles were arranged side by side, one on the left and one on the right, with a large section overlapping in the middle. At the same time, the closer the image was to the edge, the more blurry it became, just like a picture taken by two fisheye lenses.

The lenses’ image on the screen was of Frank’s eyes.

Chen Chen silently looked at everything from Frank’s perspective, but his expression became more and more serious. When he saw the rescue plea sent by the Event Horizon, he even stood up in shock. “What? Three people survived?”

At this moment, Chen Chen only felt a chill run down his spine. He knew the plot of Event Horizon very well, so of course, he knew that the ending of the movie should only have two people escaping. How could there be one more person?

Chen Chen continued to look at the list of the three people: Cooper, that was, the black man in the movie with an extraordinary attitude; Lieutenant Starck, that was, the woman who survived, and the remaining one was Dr. Weir!

“Dr. Weir...”

Chen Chen stared at the name, his eyes full of solemnity. With his brain power, it was impossible for Chen Chen to have memory problems. Therefore, it was Dr. Weir who was the problem.

In the movie’s story, Dr. Weir had been eroded by the darkness and turned into a demon-like existence. In the end, the rescue team captain triggered the bomb and died together with Dr. Weir. Only then could the two survivors escape from the ship of the Event Horizon. Chen Chen could not possibly have remembered this plot incorrectly.

However, now, the name of Dr. Weir had appeared on the list of survivors.

“There are only two people in the cryogenic chambers. Is he still looking for a third person?”

Chen Chen also understood Frank’s thoughts at this time and then he shook his head. The third person was Dr. Weir, but Dr. Weir was dead. How could he appear in the cryogenic chamber?

Chen Chen seemed to have foreseen how humankind in this world would be destroyed.

He continued watching the screen. At this time, through Frank’s eyes, he saw the footage sent from the Event Horizon, which was an image of the cryogenic chambers.

The technology of the cryogenic chambers in the dimension of the Event Horizon was the same as reality. One would be injected with a shot of antifreeze agent and then enter cryogenic sleep in the cryogenic chamber. The only difference was that here, the chamber was placed vertically. After someone had entered, the interior was filled with liquid that could withstand strong acceleration, before cryogenic sleep began.

As soon as they woke up, the sleeper would have moved from the earth to the position of Pluto’s orbit, which was extremely convenient.

At this time, due to the transmission limits of the long distance from Pluto to the earth, the image signal was a bit distorted, but one could just about make out the cryogenic chambers. Most of the cryogenic chambers were empty, except for two which contained human figures. It was not clear who the people inside were.

“NASA has sent a rescue spacecraft.”

X-112 suddenly said, “At the same time, the Event Horizon is also returning to Earth on the same route. About 25 days later, the two spacecraft will be able to dock and meet, and the Event Horizon will return to Earth in 50 days.”

Chen Chen thought for a while and seemed to be calculating something. “In this case, we can try to prevent the end of this dimension from happening.”

“What should we do?”

X-112 was suddenly curious.

“Interstellar sweeper.”

Chen Chen said curtly.

The so-called interstellar sweeper was a surface-to-sky super missile developed with the ultra-long-range intelligent missile technology from the dimension of Elysium during the military competition between Blacklight Biotechnology and the Earth Federation in the past decade.

What did “surface-to-sky” mean? It was different from “surface-to-air”. Surface-to-air missiles only included tactical missiles that were shot from the ground to flying targets in the sky while surface-to-sky missiles were strategic missiles that could be shot directly from the ground to high-speed flying targets in outer space.

This missile had four-stage rocket boosters. Each booster stage had a gravity adjustment device, independent infrared and thermal imaging guidance systems, long-range environmental sensors, digital barriers, micro-fusion thrusters... Almost all cutting-edge technologies could be seen on this missile!

The interstellar sweeper had a range of up to 400 million kilometers and could shoot to Mars directly from the earth. Its speed was 11.2 kilometers per second in the atmosphere and the highest speed outside the atmosphere was 30,000 kilometers per second, which was a tenth of the speed of light.

The biggest advantage of this kind of missile was that it was a single-soldier unit. All in all, it did not exceed one hundred kilograms, and it could be wielded by an XTN robot.

It could be said that today, the interstellar sweeper missile was the pinnacle of technological creation by the Blackwatch Corporation.

The moment X-112 heard the name interstellar sweeper, X-112 immediately understood that Sir Commander’s intention must be to destroy the Event Horizon and the rescue spacecraft directly in space before they reached the earth.

Although there were two survivors on the Event Horizon and a dozen innocent people on the newly dispatched rescue spacecraft, Chen Chen could not care about that. He still did not know where the Event Horizon had been, what was brought back from there, and what was the thing that could infect humans and turn them crazy enough to kill each other.

He could not be certain whether the rescue team would be controlled and infected by some mysterious thing after contact with Event Horizon, so he could only eliminate all of these contacts. Perhaps this could create a chance for the survival of human civilization in the dimension of Event Horizon.

Of course, that was all Chen Chen could do. After all, it was just a small effort. If this action could save humankind, of course, that would be ideal. If not, it was not a big deal. It was just that Chen Chen absolutely would not come out and save this plane by himself.

At this time, Frank, who was implanted with the chip without knowing it, went to the bathroom. Chen Chen checked the time and thought it was almost time. He immediately said, “X-112, prepare to make Frank’s consciousness fall into a deep sleep and take over his body, and carry out our first task!”

“Understood.”

X-112 answered immediately.

Immediately, Frank, who was standing in front of the urinal, looked slightly startled. He only felt an overwhelming sense of sleepiness, and then his eyes slowly closed, but only for a moment. In the next second, they opened again!

Still, when they opened once more, the light in his eyes quickly disappeared, and finally became dark.

It was as if he was sleepwalking...

chapters.title: Chapter 587: Black Fog
chapters.body:
Chapter 587: Black Fog

Chapter 587: Black Fog

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

At this moment, Frank’s movements became a little stiff. He slowly pulled up his pants, then woodenly turned and walked out of the bathroom.

“Patter, patter!”

Frank walked out of the bathroom step by step. He looked left and right, then chose the corridor on the right and walked in the direction of the elevator.

After entering the elevator, Frank did not press any button but directly placed his palm on the platform under the buttons.

“Beep!”

Suddenly, there was a notification sound and then a miniature camera popped out of a button.

Frank bent down slightly and looked into the camera with his eyes.

“Identity verified.”

An electronic sound instantly came from the elevator.

Then, the elevator shuddered slightly and descended directly downward, but strangely, although the elevator was going down, the floor displayed on the panel did not change, still indicating the first floor.

About thirty seconds after the elevator fell sharply, it finally slowed down, and then there was a “ding” again. The elevator doors opened on both sides with a “whoosh”.

What greeted Frank’s eyes was a long narrow passage.

“This is it...”

In front of the screen, Chen Chen was slightly excited. The information previously found by X-112 naturally also contained information about this place. At the end of this channel lay NASA’s true secret zone.

This corridor was similar to the laser corridor at the entrance of the Spire Experimental Base. In addition to the defense system, anyone who entered this corridor would be X-ray scanned, examining all objects on their body and even within their body. Therefore, even if someone had stuffed a foreign object in their lower body or hid it in the sole of their shoe, they would not slip through.

Still, Chen Chen was not worried because the God chip was made of biological material after all, and it could be completely integrated with flesh and blood. In addition, it was embedded in the cervical spine, which made it even more difficult to distinguish.

Soon, Frank came straight to the end of the corridor.

“Beep beep beep...”

He had passed the previous detections. At this time, the door at the end of the corridor sensed someone coming and it opened automatically.

“Swish!”

After the door was opened, a ray of light shone out suddenly, and Chen Chen in the distance looked through Frank’s eyes and saw a space larger than the hall of NASA’s command hall.

This was the real secret base of NASA and almost all state secrets were stored here.

“Mr. Frank!”

A group of guards in military uniforms stood up immediately and saluted him.

Frank nodded, did not speak, and walked directly into the hall.

Along the way, Frank met many researchers wearing white lab coats, but fortunately, Frank was the director of NASA after all. Even here, he held one of the highest positions. Several older researchers smiled and said hello immediately when they saw Frank. It seemed that his daily relationships were not too bad but under the control of X-112, Frank still nodded and slipped past them.

Soon, under the control of X-112, Frank came to a tightly closed steel gate.

He verified his identity again. Not only that, but Frank also took out an identity authentication card, which opened the door, revealing the layout of a computer room.

Upon entering the computer room, Frank turned on a computer, entered the password somewhat stiffly, and then all the confidential information that could not be detected in the outside world was thoroughly revealed before his eyes.

“These are the real big secrets of NASA.”

Chen Chen looked at the data intently. These secrets contained the design drawings of various spacecraft engines and included various secret missions in space issued by the government. The oldest one could even be traced back to the evaluation report of the era of the Soviet-American power struggle, but all the information here could only be seen with one’s eyes. There was not even a USB port on the computer, let alone a way for anyone to steal the information.

Still, all this naturally posed no problems for Chen Chen. Frank only had to glance at each piece of data and the content would be automatically recorded by X-112, then processed and converted into a standard electronic file.

Even so, the information on the technology of the space warp engine was as boundless as the ocean. Frank kept reading for two hours, but only a fifth of it had been copied.

“It’s about time.”

Chen Chen counted the time silently and suddenly said, “Let Frank leave. If he spends too much time here, it’ll inevitably rouse everyone’s suspicions.”

“Okay.”

X-112 agreed and then Frank in front of the computer immediately closed the file, turned, and walked out of the computer room.

Then, just like before, Frank returned to the surface and went to the director’s lounge.

Following that, X-112 controlled Frank to go to the bed and then withdrew its control over Frank.

“Huh?”

The next second, Frank opened his eyes slightly again. He looked around with some doubts and then sat up slowly.

“What’s the matter, did I just fall asleep?”

Frank showed a look of astonishment. His memory still lingered at the moment he went to the bathroom. Then, he had felt a wave of drowsiness but he did not expect that when he opened his eyes again, he would be waking up from bed in the lounge.

His mind felt like it was fragmented from being drunk. Frank was a little mystified. This situation had never happened before.

“Is it because I’m old?”

Frank murmured, his face pale for a while.

Nonetheless, after a period of resting in the lounge, Frank walked out of the room again. He did not suspect too much, only assuming that he was old and had a bad memory, but these thoughts were quickly forgotten by him.

He still had more important things to do, things relating to his own politics.

Chen Chen in front of the screen heaved a sigh of relief. Fortunately, Frank did not have many doubts this time.

“We won’t do this next time.”

Chen Chen thought for a while and said, “In the following period, the NASA headquarters will be very busy because of the Event Horizon. Frank will not go home too many times. If this is the case, wait for him to fall asleep in the lounge before manipulating his body to read the information about the space warp engine.”

“Understood, leave this to me.”

X-112 replied.

Indeed, once there were more instances of fragmentation, it would inevitably arouse Frank’s suspicion. If he checked the surveillance out of wariness, he would immediately see that something was wrong. It was safer to do this every time he was asleep.

Over the next twenty days or so, X-112 would take advantage of Frank’s rest time in the work unit to go to the underground secret base to look at the information. During this period, some alarming situations had occurred such as when he happened to enter the underground base and met Frank’s acquaintances who came forward to talk or someone contacted him through the communication device he carried while he was looking up the information, and so on. Still, in the end, disaster was averted and they got all the information about the space warp engine.

At this time, it was only twenty days before the Event Horizon returned to Earth.

This was also the day when the second rescue ship docked with the survivors of the Event Horizon.

“I want to temporarily return to the other side of the portal.”

After lots of thinking, Chen Chen, who finally got the space warp engine data, told X-112, “After this, you’ll continue to monitor NASA’s every move. When I return, I’ll bring the interstellar sweeper missile, understand?”

“Got it. I’ll pay close attention to NASA’s every move.”

X-112 replied.

Chen Chen nodded in satisfaction and temporarily returned to reality with the information about the space warp engine.

Of course, Chen Chen’s goal had been achieved and he could also choose to leave the Event Horizon dimension for good. Nevertheless, Chen Chen was unwilling to do so. After all, this was the first time he had been this close to the secret of the USB flash drive since he had received it. In particular, the appearance of Zhao Shanhe was a great discovery. Chen Chen could dig out more secrets about the USB flash drive and Professor Wang Xi from Zhao Shanhe.

However, just a few hours later, in the NASA command hall, everyone watched the images being uploaded from the computer.

As the distance was still too great, what they saw also had a three-hour delay, but compared to the previous visual information that could only be refreshed once in six hours, the pictures now had reached the speed of 20 frames per minute. It was like watching a PowerPoint report.

“It’s docked, it’s docked!”

At this time, before the huge screen at the top of the command hall, everyone tilted their heads, silently watching the slowly updating pictures. The rescue team’s spacecraft in the footage had successfully docked with the Event Horizon, and there were five members of the rescue team who had already opened the hydraulic chamber and were flying toward the nearby Event Horizon.

Everyone stared at the screen, watching the person who was wearing the camera slowly open the door of the Event Horizon onscreen, then walk in and come to the cryogenic chambers.

Immediately, one of them pressed the button of the cryogenic chambers, awakening the sleeping people in the two cryogenic chambers.

The water level in the two cryogenic chambers dropped rapidly at once, revealing the personnel inside. One of them was a slender blonde woman. If Chen Chen were here at this time, he would find that this was one of the survivors in the movie’s plot, Lieutenant Starck and the other one was the black man, Cooper.

However, after the two were released, Lieutenant Starck suddenly looked at the camera as if she had seen some terrifying scene and screamed!

Of course, in the eyes of the many researchers in the command hall of NASA headquarters, they could only see a silent image that was refreshed every three seconds. As for the sound, they could forget about it.

In the command hall, many people began to discuss. They did not understand why Lieutenant Starck had screamed like she had seen a ghost, but at this moment, the picture sent back from the Event Horizon suddenly turned to static. The image became distorted and became blurry mosaic images.

“What’s the matter, why is the signal so weak?”

Frank turned his head and bellowed loudly to the technician in the signal reception area, “Quickly solve this problem, right now, immediately!”

“Yes, Mr. Director!”

The department in charge of signal reception immediately sweated profusely and kept tapping on the keyboard in front of their console, rustling constantly. However, no matter how hard they tried, the screen above everyone’s heads was refreshed to only display more noise.

Just when everyone was becoming rather anxious, suddenly, the picture was clear again, but at the moment when the clarity was restored, everyone was completely dumbfounded...

They saw that on the image that was sent back to them, there was a human face, one that was smiling maliciously at the camera, with only two black hollows left as the eyes...

...

Half a day later, when the sky dimmed again, Chen Chen came back through the USB drive’s portal again. This time, five figures appeared behind him and these five figures each carried a metal weapon on their backs that was half a human’s height. It was like a cannon with a square barrel and four launch holes.

These five human figures were the latest single-soldier combat humanoid robot soldiers developed by Chen Chen — XTN-032.

XTN-032 was an improved version of the original humanoid combat robot XTN-002. They were all humanoid robots but unlike other types of robots, the biggest advantage of XTN-032 was how balanced it was. It could use all weapons that human soldiers could use and be utilized to full strength in any kind of battlefield.

What they carried on their backs was Blacklight Biotechnology’s single-soldier strategic missile “interstellar sweeper”. Each launcher was equipped with four miniature surface-to-sky interstellar sweeper missiles. Five robots added up to twenty missiles, which must be more than enough to deal with the Event Horizon which did not have any space weapons.

However, as Chen Chen returned through the passage, he immediately received news from X-112. “Sir Commander, just five hours ago, NASA’s rescue team and the returning Event Horizon have successfully docked.”

“Oh, they’ve already docked?”

Chen Chen looked around the beach and as he released his Field energy, all the tourists near the beach dropped to the ground without warning in the dark, losing consciousness. “What’s the situation, have they found Dr. Weir?”

“No...”

X-112’s voice is a bit somber. “Frank has lost contact with me. Not only him, but the entire NASA headquarters has completely lost contact with the outside world. This incident has shocked the White House.”

Chen Chen’s heart sank after hearing these words. “Is it a meme? The video sent by the Event Horizon has a memetic effect?”

“I’m not sure.”

X-112 answered frankly, “I only found some clues using the Black Knights. What is currently known is that the entire NASA headquarters building is shrouded in a peculiar black fog.”

“Black fog?”

Chen Chen’s expression was uncertain. He stopped questioning and after arranging for the XTN robots to head to the Black Knights stationed nearby, he flew directly toward Washington.

More than an hour later, when Chen Chen hurriedly rushed to the sky above Washington, he suddenly saw a sea of ??people and cars at his feet. Thousands of vehicles continued to gush out from Washington and he observed, on both sides of the road, a large number of army soldiers stationed nearby. In the dark night, all this looked like a large swarm of migrating locusts.

Then, Chen Chen took the opportunity to look into the distance, but when he saw the distant scenery clearly, his pupils suddenly shrank!

Even though he was several kilometers away, he could still see the location of the city center. A thick black fog was willfully pervading the city center, constantly spreading in all directions.

The black fog looked very solid and it was very conspicuous even in the dark. What was even more bizarre was that once the black fog covered the buildings in the city, the lights of those buildings were instantly extinguished...

In the darkness, there was a strange, alarming aura. Just by looking at it, Chen Chen had the impression that he was facing the abyss.

Chen Chen’s heart sank. He knew that the catastrophe that occurred in Event Horizon’s dimension had arrived...

chapters.title: Chapter 588: Catastrophe
chapters.body:
Chapter 588: Catastrophe

Chapter 588: Catastrophe

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

“What’s going on?”

Seeing this scene, Chen Chen could not help asking, “Are you sure this happened after the spacecraft docked?”

“Sir Excellency, there’s no mistake. It’s related to the docking of the spacecraft.”

X-112 replied, “Through Frank’s sight, I’ve recorded the footage from the Event Horizon when the spacecraft docked, but this video has been sealed by me.”

Chen Chen nodded. X-112’s approach was correct. The dimension of Event Horizon was suspected to have unknown memes. The black fog in front of them was evidence. After all, besides a memetic effect that could be transmitted through information, Chen Chen had no other way to explain why the NASA headquarters would become a building filled with black fog.

“This swathe of black fog first emerged at NASA headquarters and then it continued to diffuse over such a huge range.”

X-112 continued to explain, “At present, this stretch of black fog is still expanding and it’s almost reaching the area where I’m hiding.”

“Retreat then.”

Chen Chen said, “Let the Black Knights take you away from where you are and exit the city of Washington.”

Chen Chen said casually and then he slowly approached the black fog from mid-air.

Through his mid-air flight, Chen Chen roughly calculated that the black fog was now elliptic in shape, about three kilometers in diameter, and about one kilometer high, blanketing one-fifth of Washington. Not only that, as time went by, the black fog’s scope was getting larger.

According to his calculations based on the borders of the black fog, the center of the black fog was indeed the location of NASA headquarters.

Chen Chen even personally approached the black fog within a range of 80 meters and then used Field to explore the inside of the black fog. Though it appeared to be mere vapor, it had blocked Chen Chen’s Field energy.

However, Chen Chen realized that it could not fully block him. There was still a trace of power penetrating the black fog but only to an extremely shallow extent. Moreover, the penetration was not from his Field power, but his psionic powers.

Seeing this, Chen Chen frowned. It was almost unbelievable that it was able to completely shield his Field energy. After all, everything was composed of the four fundamental forces, yet this black fog seemed to be beyond the scope of electromagnetic force. On the contrary, the psionic energy that functioned as a supplementary power for Chen Chen could penetrate it.

Chen Chen even noted that the black fog had swallowed up several citizens who were too slow in fleeing, thinking that they would be able to escape by hiding in their houses. These citizens originally watched the black fog approaching and kept screaming, but as soon as the black fog rushed over them, their screams for help disappeared without a trace!

As if the black fog was isolating reality, entrapping the group of people in another world...

Chen Chen frowned when he saw this. He turned his head slightly and a huge bus was lifted with a rumble and thrown straight into the black fog. However, after the front of the bus had reached into the black fog, Chen Chen suddenly exerted some force and tried to pull the bus out of the black fog.

“Twang...”

There was a sound of metal distortion. As his Field energy erupted, Chen Chen pulled the bus out vigorously and immediately, half of the bus was pulled out!

That was right, only half of it was left.

Chen Chen stared in shock but saw that the front of the bus that had penetrated the black fog had completely vanished and there were signs of forcible tearing at the fracture.

The black fog turned out to be like a living thing, silently devouring anything that entered its territory, and even the steel could not break free...

“Interesting.”

Chen Chen snorted coldly. He once again took out a miniature reality anchor from the bundle behind him, used his Field energy, and rammed it into the ground!

“Boom!”

The reality anchor was immediately plunged into the ground. At the same time, an invisible wave surged out and the next second, the huge black fog a hundred meters away suddenly stopped!

Chen Chen was only giving it a shot because he noticed that the black fog could isolate Field energy but not psionic powers, so he thought of using the reality anchor. Nonetheless, he had never expected that the reality anchor would fix the black fog in place!

“The black fog isn’t moving...”

Chen Chen looked at all this in astonishment, only to see that the black fog that had been surging up and bulging on all sides suddenly had its range reduced significantly. Then, its edges gradually calmed down, just like the waves on the beach, when the turbulent water would suddenly slow down and gradually turn calm.

The reality anchor had suppressed the black fog!

A miniature reality anchor had a suppression radius of five kilometers, so it could cover all the black fog that only had a diameter of only three kilometers. It was more than enough.

Of course, if the black fog had been given a period to expand, things might not have worked out.

Seeing this, Chen Chen took a deep breath. He became more and more curious about the origin of the black fog.

Of course, Chen Chen was not arrogant enough to go directly into the black fog to explore. He just followed the suggestions of X-112 and came to a house in the suburbs. The residents here had been evacuated by the military. There were only a few people within a radius of dozens of miles who were still reluctant to leave.

In the room, two Black Knights were still standing slackly in the same place. One of the Black Knights was holding an X-112 storage device in his hands.

Chen Chen took the X-112 from the Black Knight, turned on a computer next to him and plugged it in, and then asked, “What happened when the Event Horizon docked with the rescue spacecraft during the time I was away?”

“After the two sides connected, an image appeared.”

X-112 replied, “It was fine at the beginning. It was almost the same as the plot of the movie. The rescue team opened the cryogenic chambers and released the two survivors, Lieutenant Starck and Cooper, but the next second, Lieutenant Starck stared at the camera and screamed suddenly. Then there was interference with the signal... When the interference was over, everyone at NASA saw an image.”

X-112 was silent for a while. “Frank lost the connection with me after that image and the black fog began to appear from NASA headquarters. As for what happened after the connection was lost, I don’t know.”

“Is that so?”

Chen Chen wore a contemplative expression. “It seems that this must be a phenomenon that would only occur due to a memetic effect. It looks like that image is the key point.”

Chen Chen did not ask what the content of the image was and X-112 did not describe it either because both of them understood the strangeness of memetic effects. Some memes would not only transmit by the sense of “sight” but also would infect you indiscriminately as long as you know of its existence or others described its characteristics to you.

This kind of meme was difficult to guard against.

“Let the Black Knights test it.”

Chen Chen thought for a while and looked at the two Black Knights behind him. “I want to see if the Black Knights who have no brains and can’t think can also be tricked by this meme.”

With that, Chen Chen asked X-112 to load the video into the computer. Then, he pulled out X-112 again, opened the window, turned, and left the room, retreating to a distance of eighty meters.

At this distance, his Field energy could completely cover the entire room through the open window and observe the situation of the two Black Knights after they had watched the footage. This was also one of the best ways to handle the memetic effect.

“Let’s start.”

Following Chen Chen’s order, X-112 began to control one of the Black Knights sitting in front of the computer and at the same time, clicked on the video that was left on the screen while Chen Chen reflexively allowed his Field to go around the computer screen, making it impossible for him to observe what appeared on the computer.

The two Black Knights watched the video little by little.

Time trickled by, second by second.

Suddenly, at the end of the video, the two Black Knights shuddered and at the same time, the computer emitted a ripping sound, and the two Black Knights suddenly collapsed to the ground slowly.

“Sir Commander, I’ve lost contact with the two Black Knights!”

X-112 immediately notified.

Chen Chen was observing all this through his Field even more attentively and then, he saw a black fog slowly gushing out of the computer.

“Rumble!”

The black fog tumbled out from the computer screen in an instant, flooding the entire room little by little, and at the same time, repelling Chen Chen’s Field energy!

It turned out that the black fog had appeared like this...

Chen Chen withdrew his Field energy a little bit and silently looked at the house 80 meters away, only to see endless black fog quickly gushing from the windows and the cracks in the door. Gradually, a new mass of black fog formed!

“Suppress it!”

Seeing this, Chen Chen immediately took out a miniature reality anchor from the bundle behind him and inserted it directly into the ground. Suddenly, the black fog that had just formed was fixed in place again and no longer expanded.

“A miniature reality anchor’s capacitor can only supply power for about twelve hours.”

X-112 reminded. “Should I order the remaining Black Knights stationed in Miami to bring more reality anchors?”

“Very well.”

Chen Chen nodded. “Be careful not to be discovered by the Northern American authorities.”

The reason Chen Chen did this was of course, not to be the savior. He was only testing the power of Event Horizon. As for any cooperation with the government, it would restrict Chen Chen’s actions and make Chen Chen unable to fully explore using this opportunity.

However, when Chen Chen was silently testing out the dimension of Event Horizon, the officials who had already withdrawn from the White House at this time had already taken the Air Force One presidential plane and left the Washington area.

“What? The black fog has stopped spreading?”

The President of Northern America watched the video that was sent to him. It was a footage taken from a high altitude. In the video, the black fog no longer showed signs of surging forward but had completely turned into a dome-shaped fog with smooth edges.

The fog straddled the center of Washington like a black egg, which was very prominent even in the dark.

Its blackness was far more concentrated and conspicuous than the dark night.

“D*mn it, what the h*ll is this...”

The president of Northern America gritted his teeth at the black fog and then looked at the man in uniform beside him. “General Bob, are you sure that the black fog erupted from NASA headquarters?”

“I can’t be mistaken.”

The person called General Bob was a middle-aged man with gray hair but a cold expression. He nodded solemnly and said, “We can confirm this according to the bird’s-eye view that was sent, Mr. President, please look — this is the map of downtown Washington. Overlap this with the bird’s-eye view taken now and we can see what’s in the area shrouded by the black fog...

“Although the black fog is elliptic when viewed from the side like a large bowl tipped over the ground, when you look at it from a high altitude, you’ll find that the black fog is perfectly round. Just calculate the radius of the black fog and we can surmise where the center of the black fog is.

“And our prediction was right — the center of the black fog is the headquarters of NASA!”

Hearing this, the President of Northern America nodded slightly. He worriedly asked, “What’s the number of people who’ve gone missing in this incident?”

“The numbers are temporarily unavailable.”

General Bob shook his head with a serious expression. “Because the black fog appeared so suddenly, we can only do our best to evacuate the crowd but ever since the black fog has spread, at least several blocks have been swallowed up. Moreover, it’s also quite late at night now, so I’m afraid the number of missing people isn’t optimistic...”

The reason they called it the number of missing people rather than the number of casualties was that even they did not know what would happen to the people who had been enveloped by the black fog. At first, they sent troops and armored vehicles into the black fog, but no matter how many people went in, they would instantaneously lose contact with them.

Thus, this scared them so much that they no longer dared to send anyone into the black fog area.

“Mr. President, please don’t be discouraged.”

The Secretary of Defense on the side suddenly walked over and interjected. “It’s good news now that the black fog has stopped spreading. The most frightening thing would be that the spread of the black fog cannot be stopped. That would be troublesome.”

“I just want to know what that black fog is now.”

The president nodded with a sorrowful expression.

“That’s easy enough.”

The Secretary of Defense reminded. “NASA is an agency directly under the White House and all daily activity logs there are stored in the Department of Defense’s secret server, which we can check.”

Hearing this, the president’s eyes lit up. Yes, he had been overwhelmed by worries. He completely forgot that everything at NASA headquarters was under the control of the Department of Defense and could be inspected at any time.

Following many high-level orders, the log report was soon retrieved and played in the President’s exclusive vehicle, Air Force One.

“This is... The docking between the Event Horizon and the rescue team?”

Everyone immediately saw the scene recorded in the video and also saw the huge screen in the command hall that was in the scene.

“It’s a common procedure. The Event Horizon has disappeared for seven years. This is a rescue operation reported by NASA and then approved by me.”

The president of the Northern American Continent looked at the video in front of him, frowned, and faintly had an ominous hunch. “Could it be that all of this has something to do with the Event Horizon?”

With that, he looked at the sky beside him but saw that there were already twinkling lights under his feet.

“Where are we?”

The president of Northern America turned around and looked at the officials around him who were staring at the screen together.

“We have now entered the sky over New York City.”

The Secretary of Defense glanced out the window. “We’re about to arrive at our destination — the Islip secret military base.”

However, at this moment, the Secretary of Defense suddenly opened his mouth and widened his eyes, staring at the screen.

“Secretary Franklin?”

The Northern American president asked suspiciously, but the other party was still gripped by surprise as if the expression on his face was frozen by death, with an inexplicable weirdness.

At the same time, it was not only the Secretary of Defense but also General Bob and others next to him who also wore the same look.

Seeing this, the president of Northern America only felt a chill in his heart. He slowly turned his head and looked at the screen in front of him...

“Boom!”

Over New York City, a large passenger plane with black fog constantly pouring out of the cabin, plunged into the city center...

chapters.title: Chapter 589: Black Slate
chapters.body:
Chapter 589: Black Slate

Chapter 589: Black Slate

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

“Black Knights, go in.”

Chen Chen waved his hand and a Black Knight walked straight toward the fixed black fog not far away.

However, when the Black Knight walked into the black fog, there was nothing left, not an echo. No signal was received either.

Chen Chen hesitated over whether to continue sending Black Knights in.

“Sir Commander, the latest update.”

X-112 suddenly notified, “The Air Force One which the President of Northern America boarded was confirmed to have met an accident and crashed into the eastern part of Manhattan in New York City. At the same time, the black fog was also generated after the crash and had spread over the crash site.”

Hearing this news, Chen Chen only felt as if his head was exploding. “That’s to say, a third black fog spot has appeared...

“If this continues, I’m afraid that black fog will appear everywhere in the world.”

X-112 said, “That image isn’t that classified and many high-level officials have viewing authority. To explore the secrets of NASA headquarters, there will be many people watching that scene.”

Chen Chen could not help frowning when he heard this. Indeed, this was a problem. It was not that Northern American officials were trying to kill themselves. It was just that who in the world would think that an image in a video could kill people or even destroy the entire planet?

If he told this to anyone on the street, they would only think he was crazy.

“Send the Black Knights over and confine it.”

Chen Chen had to say, “We can only confine it in a few locations and if there are more, there’s nothing we can do.”

“Perhaps we can inform the senior management of Northern America?”

X-112 asked, “Telling them these key points may prevent them from continuing to die.”

“But the top leaders in Northern America have already disappeared.”

Chen Chen said helplessly, “So, who should we tell?”

“...”

X-112 was also speechless.

During the conversation, an army with tanks had begun to move toward the place from a distance. As the black fog had stopped expanding, the military was preparing to set up a defensive net to seal the area.

Seeing this, Chen Chen directly took off and hid inside a high-rise residential building.

Still, when he had just left, he saw five people stealthily walking out of a building not far away, a little closer to the edge of the black fog.

“It’s them?”

Chen Chen raised his eyebrows and looked over quickly.

At this time, Zhao Shanhe led his team out of the building and slowly approached the place.

“What’s the matter, the subspace countercurrent has stopped spreading?”

Looking at the black fog that had completely calmed down in front of him, Zhao Shanhe’s eyes glinted with puzzlement. “Even our original Force can’t be used. It feels like it must be that person...”

In his mind, the face of the mysterious young man he had encountered before flashed suddenly. He still remembered that when they fought a month ago, the man had used an instrument to block their hard-earned acquisition from the Star Wars dimension, the Force.

“Boss!”

Just then, a surprised voice came from not far away. “Look here!”

Zhao Shanhe and the others quickly went to the person’s side, only to see a young man pointing at his feet, where there was a deep and dark hole. It was a hole that had penetrated the concrete pavement directly as if a massive force had pounded something deep underground.

“It’s that guy’s handiwork...”

Zhao Shanhe’s pupils contracted. He still remembered that the mysterious young man had driven an instrument into the ground and then the Force in their bodies was blocked.

“It’s this instrument that has blocked the diffusion of subspace countercurrents.”

The young man reminded. “He came prepared!”

“Cough...”

With a bandage wrapped around her chest, the woman who kept coughing also suddenly said, “I still remember that person. He has a more complex and powerful power than our original Force. You can see that a piece of equipment with the thickness of an arm was punched three hundred meters deep underground. This is something our Force cannot do!”

This woman was the one who was sent flying by Chen Chen’s Field before, but she who had crashed through a wall in the first place could now move around freely. Zhao Shanhe’s team also had some kind of powerful recovery method.

“At that time, all of us were restrained by him and Karl died instantly. Fortunately, he didn’t kill us at that time. If he wanted to kill all of us, we wouldn’t have been able to live until now...”

Another person said, “Only Boss’ Civilization Monolith can just about suppress him.”

“Can this instrument be taken out?”

The third person’s eyes were a bit fiery. “It can restrain the subspace countercurrent and at the same time, it can also block our force. I want to see what kind of machine it is...”

“Let me see...”

The young man who first found the opening of the hole bent down slightly and took out a laser measuring pen to shine into the gap. Then he said in surprise, “Three hundred meters. This small hole with only the thickness of an arm is three hundred meters deep!”

“So deep?”

Everyone looked astounded not by the depth of this hole, but by the ability of the other party to plunge an instrument to a depth of three hundred meters in an instant.

“Cough... D*mn it, it’s too difficult to take it out. It can’t be done without some large equipment!”

The woman could not help complaining.

Just as they were gathered here, there was a sudden roar of engines not far away and several armored vehicles and hundreds of soldiers under Northern America drove in quickly.

“You guys! What are you doing!”

The distant army quickly approached, seemingly intended to surround them.

“Boss, the army is here!”

Everyone frowned and looked around.

“Humph!”

Zhao Shanhe shook his head, shot a disgruntled look at the deep hole by his feet, and then spread out one hand. Suddenly, a palm-sized black slate appeared in his hand out of thin air!

“Hum!”

An invisible wave swept in all directions and the group of soldiers in the distance suddenly screamed with their heads in their arms and even the tanks lost control and crashed into the surrounding buildings!

At the same time, because the fluctuations had spread in all directions, some of the fluctuations extended directly into the solid black fog, but the next second, at the moment a mass of black fog came into contact with the fluctuations, the black fog dissipated instantly!

Seeing this scene, Chen Chen’s pupils shrank as he hid and watched from the building. He stared at the black slate, his eyes gleaming with a greedy light. “That slate has such a huge effect...”

However, when Chen Chen was taken aback by the function of the black slate, he suddenly discovered that the five of them were all walking in the direction of the black fog.

“Could it be...”

Chen Chen’s expression flickered, showing a look of surprise. “Are they going to head in?”

Sure enough, after the team of five headed by Zhao Shanhe had defeated the army, they walked directly in the direction of the black fog, and then disappeared into the black fog...

“Huh!”

Chen Chen took a deep breath, only feeling that his heart was about to burst. He had never thought that the movie 2001: A Space Odyssey had such a huge secret hidden in it. After all, even Chen Chen could do nothing to the black fog. The reality anchor could only suppress the black fog but could not dissolve it, yet the black slate did just that.

“X-112, I need to return to reality.”

Chen Chen could not be bothered with Zhao Shanhe’s team. He just quickly commanded, “After I return to reality, the passage may be temporarily closed but don’t worry, I’ll open the passage again in a few days at most.”

“Understood. I’ll keep watch.”

X-112 said, “But if you leave, how should we replace the batteries of these reality anchors? After all, the army will soon surround this place.”

“Forget about them for now.”

Chen Chen resolutely said, “Wait for my return before anything else!”

“Yes!”

After Chen Chen finished speaking, he flew directly out of the window and flew in the direction of Miami...

An hour later, Chen Chen returned to the end dimension, then took the time machine again to return to reality.

When Chen Chen returned to the end dimension again, he had brought an ordinary USB flash drive with him and the USB flash drive contained the movie 2001: A Space Odyssey.

Immediately, he went straight to the computer, closed the portal leading to Event Horizon, and plugged the ordinary USB flash drive into the computer. Then, he transferred the old movie that was released in 1968 to the mysterious USB flash drive.

After that, Chen Chen quickly played the movie and stopped at the scene where the black slate appeared in the opening.

Following this, Chen Chen decisively stretched out his arm toward the screen as if he had done it a million times. He wanted to take out the black slate in the movie!

This stone slab was extremely important to Chen Chen.

Nevertheless, when Chen Chen reached in with his entire arm, his expression suddenly shifted... This was because he did not touch anything.

“Not there?”

Chen Chen was groping around the other side incredulously, but no matter what, all he could touch was air!

This was a situation that had never happened before ever since he obtained the USB drive and started extracting items from movies...

After he retracted his arm again, Chen Chen’s expression was a little gloomy. He thought for a while and dragged the progress bar again to freeze the frame on the moon where the black slate appeared for the second time in the movie.

Then, he tried again...

He still could not touch anything.

Then, he simply dragged the progress bar to the end and tried again.

Still nothing...

Chen Chen withdrew his arm and fell completely silent.

“Why not? Could it be that...”

Chen Chen looked at the USB flash drive inserted in the computer, but he remembered that no matter where he went, even in his dreams, the USB flash drive would return to him. It could be said that it was completely bound to himself. Could it be that this black slate was similar or that it even possessed the same characteristic of being utterly unique?

This uniqueness meant that in the multiverse, across endless time and space, there was only one.

As the black slate had been taken away by Zhao Shanhe, it was impossible for Chen Chen to get a second slate from the movie.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen had no choice but to close the movie and instead reopened Event Horizon.

A few minutes later, under the ocean at Miami Beach, a black channel appeared again, and then Chen Chen stepped in and came back into the world.

“You’re back, Sir Commander.”

X-112’s voice suddenly came from the headset.

“How long have I been away?”

Chen Chen looked at the time. Of course, he knew that he had only been away for slightly more than an hour but he needed to verify whether the Event Horizon dimension he accessed this time had become a different world.

“You were gone for one hour, thirteen minutes, and thirty-six seconds.”

X-112 replied.

Hearing this, Chen Chen nodded.

He flew up quickly, rushing toward Washington while thinking silently. Zhao Shanhe knew a lot more than Chen Chen himself. Chen Chen questioned himself and realized, if he were Zhao Shanhe, he would not possibly rush into the black fog so rashly without first knowing what it was.

However, Zhao Shanhe and his team went in without hesitation, so they knew some information about the black fog.

This information was something Chen Chen so seriously lacked that though his strength had long surpassed Zhao Shanhe, he still dared not act rashly.

“At least I was right in coming to this dimension.”

Chen Chen murmured in a low voice. It could be said that this was the first time he was this close to the secret of the USB flash drive. Perhaps here, he could find out what happened in the past and why Zhao Shanhe would die and what kind of relationship Professor Wang Xi had with the USB flash drive...

When Chen Chen returned to Washington, he saw that tens of thousands of soldiers had been stationed around the black fog. These troops tightly encircled the black fog and stretched out a ten-kilometer warning tape.

Even the position of the reality anchor set up by Chen Chen had been discovered by the group of soldiers but they did not seem to understand exactly what was inside this extremely deep pit.

As for the second black fog created by Chen Chen in the suburbs, because the reality anchor was erected early enough, the black fog had not spread out of the range of the house so far, so it had not been discovered by the army.

Seeing this, Chen Chen simply re-installed a miniature reality anchor two kilometers away from the black fog. As the suppression radius of the miniature reality anchor was five kilometers, it could cover the entire black fog at a distance of two kilometers and it was much more convenient to replace its battery.

Subsequently, Chen Chen forcibly rushed into the army array and recovered the previous reality anchor that was discovered by the army.

Now that ten years had passed, Blacklight Biotechnology still had not produced the mysterious element 126, so the zircon in every reality anchor was extremely precious and must not be lost.

To Chen Chen, taking back the reality anchor under the guard of a group of soldiers was not a big deal, but the group of soldiers suffered a bad fright. To them, a figure who could be a man or a ghost forcibly rushed into the army. After wounding hundreds of soldiers and a few armored vehicles, it took something from the big mysterious hole they had found and then left calmly. This development deeply upset everyone and made the military leaders more frightened than ever.

Chen Chen did not leave either. On the contrary, he sent more Black Knights to sneak into downtown Washington, watching silently from the rear of the army, waiting for Zhao Shanhe’s group to appear again.

That was right. In Chen Chen’s opinion, since the other party was confident enough to enter the black fog, naturally there would be a day when they came out. If this was a waiting game, Chen Chen could afford to wait.

Even so, this wait took him no less than twenty days.

On the day when the Event Horizon returning from Pluto was about to enter the range of the interstellar sweeper missile, Chen Chen suddenly received a new update reported by X-112.

Zhao Shanhe had appeared in New York City!

chapters.title: Chapter 590: Zhao Shanhe
chapters.body:
Chapter 590: Zhao Shanhe

Chapter 590: Zhao Shanhe

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

“What, Zhao Shanhe has appeared in New York?”

When Chen Chen heard the news, he immediately showed a look of surprise. He turned his head and looked at the giant egg-shaped black fog in the distance outside the window. “Those two black patches of fog are interlinked?”

Although Chen Chen was not expecting this situation, he was not too taken aback. After all, he had even seen a cosmic life form such as the Consciousness Stealer and even the USB flash drive could shuttle between various major dimensions. What else could astonish him?

At this time, X-112 sent a video. In the video, he could only see a black fog that was much larger than the one in Washington, blanketing Manhattan, New York City. Countless soldiers were stretching out a warning tape not far away.

However, at this moment, a portion of the black fog protruded slightly and then two small figures came out of it and were immediately arrested by the soldiers on the spot.

The camera zoomed in and Chen Chen could see that one of the two figures was Zhao Shanhe and the other was a woman with a bandage on her chest. However, both of them looked to be in a pitiful state. Their bodies were full of scars and even their clothes were in tatters and could not cover them up.

Chen Chen even faintly made out that they seemed to be keeping their eyes closed and there were rows of black things on their eyes.

It was just that Chen Chen could not see what those black things were. After all, the distance was still too great.

“They have been captured by the army of Northern America and the two of them didn’t resist.”

X-112 said, “I’ve dispatched the Black Knights to secretly track the army team that escorted them to see where they will be sent.”

“Only two of them survived?”

Chen Chen frowned. It was obvious that there was a certain danger in the black fog of the image. There were five people under Zhao Shanhe and he himself made six, but one was killed by a furious Chen Chen before.

Now five people had entered the black fog, but only two came out alive.

“I’m going to figure out what they experienced inside.”

As he spoke, Chen Chen turned his head and looked at the Black Knight on the side. “X-112, the Event Horizon and the rescue spacecraft that are returning together have entered the range of the interstellar sweeper, right? If so, begin the attack and take down the two spaceships.”

“Yes.”

X-112 agreed and Chen Chen flew out of the window and hurried toward New York.

Following Chen Chen’s orders, five XTN robots stationed in Miami slowly rose out of the ocean.

The build of these robots was a bit like the Terminator and there were hints of Alita and NS-5 too. Each robot had an over-the-top square cannon muzzle on the back and four pure black missile warheads protruded faintly from the muzzle with a fierce murderous aura.

“Beep!”

When the five robots surfaced, the barrel of one of the robots lifted slightly and aimed directly at the blue sky and a long and narrow sight popped out from the muzzle behind the robot. It began to collect electromagnetic waves from various types of aircraft outside the atmosphere.

“Discovering target...”

“Target confirmed, locking aim!”

“Target has been locked...”

“Successive launches at a frequency of once every five minutes... Three, two, one!”

“Whoosh!”

Suddenly, a flare of fire shot up from the sea and directly penetrated the atmosphere at an extreme speed!

“One kilometer, two kilometers, three kilometers...”

As the robot kept speaking, the number indicating the altitude of the missile was flipping rapidly like a stopwatch and it rose to a height of thirty or forty kilometers in one minute!

“It has broken through the stratosphere and is starting to accelerate...”

“...”

“Discarding the first-stage thruster, activating the second-stage thruster!”

“...”

“It has broken through the middle layer and is entering the ionosphere. Discarding the second-stage thruster, activating the third-stage thruster!”

At a speed that humans practically could never reach, the first missile arrived in outer space and quickly circled the earth like a slingshot, accelerating to an extreme through gravity, then shot toward the Event Horizon that was hundreds of millions of kilometers away!

“Discarding the third-stage thruster, activating the maximum power of the fourth-stage nuclear-powered thruster! It’s expected to reach its speed limit in three hours and fifteen minutes and hit the target in three hours, fifty minutes, and twenty-three seconds...”

When this data report was announced, five minutes had passed.

“The Interstellar Sweeper Missile 1-2 is ready to launch...”

To prevent any accidents, Chen Chen prepared a total of 20 interstellar sweeper missiles for the two spacecraft targets. They were launched one after another at intervals of five minutes. In less than two hours, all twenty missiles had flown into the vast starry sky...

The missile launch scene naturally shocked the entire Earth Federation, but with the speed of the interstellar sweepers, forget about interception, they could not even figure out the missile launch site before the five XTN robots disappeared again without a trace.

When these missiles were continuously launched, Chen Chen also came to the sky over New York City.

At a distance of several dozen kilometers, Chen Chen had already seen the huge black fog, which was even more conspicuous during the day, just like a huge black mountain straddling the center of New York City. Its pitch blackness did not reflect the slightest light and it was stifling to the extreme.

Now, the black fog had long been known to the entire world, which had fallen into panic, but Chen Chen did not pay attention to the public opinion in this world. After all, it was not his original world. Chen Chen naturally felt a sense of detachment.

The reason he was willing to take action before was only to use this place as a testing ground. He wanted to see if his intervention could change the destiny of humanity being destroyed.

“Just half an hour ago, Zhao Shanhe and the woman were escorted to the secret military base in Islip.”

X-112 notified, “The two didn’t resist and out of the two, the woman was injured more seriously.”

Chen Chen frowned. He stopped at the top of a building and looked at the location of Long Island. Of course, he who had stolen the information knew that the Islip secret military base was located in Long Island, New York, about 80 kilometers away.

“Since they have been caught, let’s use the God chip to meet him.”

Chen Chen said.

He had a faint hunch that told him, although Zhao Shanhe’s group of people was not as strong as his own, Chen Chen did not believe that the other party would be caught so easily. Maybe this was also part of the other party’s plan.

Thinking of this, he flicked his sleeves, turned, and disappeared from the rooftop.

...

“Who are you two? What is that black fog?”

In a clean, well-lighted interrogation room, Zhao Shanhe was lazily leaning against a chair with his hands in handcuffs. In front of him, a bald interrogator stood up and asked aggressively, “What is your plan? Tell me everything!”

“I’ve told you over and over.”

Zhao Shanhe rubbed his eyes. There were several black scars on his upper and lower eyelids as if his eyelids had been sewn shut.

At this time, he smiled lazily. “I came from another dimension to save you. Your world is on the verge of extinction. Only I can save you.”

“Do you think I’ll believe that?”

The interrogator looked at Zhao Shanhe with a cold expression. “Why did you sew your eyes shut when you entered the black fog? What’s in the black fog?”

Zhao Shanhe could not help sighing and shook his head slightly. Then, he stretched his hands under the table, spread them apart, and a palm-sized black slab faintly flickered on the palm of his right hand.

Suddenly, the bald interrogator’s eyes dimmed slightly as if he had been hypnotized. In the next second, he slowly turned around and left the interrogation room.

After a while, several soldiers walked in, gripped Zhao Shanhe by the arms, and escorted him back to his prison.

“It’s rather comfortable here...”

As soon as Zhao Shanhe returned to the prison, he stretched out lazily, then lay down directly on his bed, humming a little song while thinking about the next countermeasure...

Before he knew it, he fell asleep.

“Patter, patter...”

He did not know how much time had passed. Zhao Shanhe vaguely heard the faint sound of footsteps. The footsteps came from the end of the corridor, approaching from a distance, and soon stopped outside his confinement room.

“Why are they here again?”

Zhao Shanhe, who had been deep in sleep, frowned impatiently. He did not move and directly activated the power of the black slate, trying to control the opponent to turn away but all this while, he still did not hear the opponent’s footsteps leaving.

“Thud, thud, thud...”

The soft sound of knocking on the iron door rang out slowly, echoing in the prison.

“Hmm?”

Zhao Shanhe activated the black slate several times in a row, but the entire time, the black slate could not drive away the opponent. Thus, he opened his eyes suddenly and looked at the door with vigilance.

At the grille of the black iron gate, a middle-aged man who seemed to be a high-ranking official was standing outside the gate with a sullen face, looking at Zhao Shanhe with a blank stare.

Zhao Shanhe was shocked. He turned over, got up, and asked, “Who are you?”

“We met some time ago.”

The other party’s lips and teeth squirmed slowly, but the words spoken were stiff as if this was not a living person. “Zhao Shanhe, I can cooperate with you.”

“You are...”

Zhao Shanhe squinted his eyes and looked at the man alertly. “You are... That young man, the previous owner of the dimension key!”

“Hahaha...”

Hearing this, the other man laughed but the laughter still had a stiff rhythm. “You’ve come to your senses so quickly? That’s right, I’m him.”

“How are you doing this?”

Zhao Shanhe looked even more astounded. “Technology? Or some mysterious power?”

“We can discuss this issue later.”

The other party woodenly replied, “Seeing that only two of you came back, it seems that you’ve failed to remove the antimeme. In that case, you can try to cooperate with me. The result might be different.”

Zhao Shanhe’s expression flickered and then he sneered. “How do you want to cooperate?”

“Tell me everything you know.”

The other party said, “In exchange, I’ll save you first.”

“If I wanted to come out, I’d have come out long ago.”

Zhao Shanhe snorted coldly and said, “The reason I’m willing to be caught by them is just to let them treat Jenny’s injury. When Jenny recovers, I can take Jenny away at any time.”

Hearing this, the slack-looking senior official was silent for a moment and then said, “I understand. In that case, I’ll wait for you outside.”

As he spoke, he slowly withdrew from the edge of the grille, and then disappeared from behind the gate.

It was not until the senior official disappeared that Zhao Shanhe frowned, wearing a thoughtful look.

The high-ranking official was naturally the result of Chen Chen’s use of the God chip. At this time, in a small town more than ten kilometers away from Islip’s secret military base, Chen Chen slowly opened his eyes and frowned. “Zhao Shanhe said that he could leave at any time?”

“Could it be the power of that black slate?”

X-112 asked, “I’ve checked the information from Islip’s secret military base and I’m sure that all the items he was carrying have been confiscated. Since he’s imprisoned there, it’s impossible for him to carry anything else with him.”

“Indeed, when I first encountered him, I also realized this.”

Chen Chen nodded. “When I checked the videos of Zhao Shanhe’s interrogation, I saw that something was wrong. In each interrogation, the interrogators didn’t find anything meaningful at all and then gave up halfway. However...

“That black slate seemed to appear out of thin air. He hadn’t had anything in his hand before, but when he opened his palm, there was an extra black slate in his hand.

“From the movie 2001: A Space Odyssey, it can be seen that the black slate has the ability to disrupt people’s thoughts. This weird interrogation recording is likely thanks to his manipulation of the black slate...”

“If this is the case, if you cooperate with him, I’m afraid you have to be careful.”

X-112 was a little worried. “Does it disturb people’s thinking ability? This ability is probably very terrifying.”

“Don’t worry, I’ve faced it before.”

Chen Chen nodded. “There’s probably a limit to its ability to control thoughts. He can control ordinary people but he can’t control me. Otherwise, I’d have been killed when I fought him for the first time.”

Despite saying that, Chen Chen still shook his head a little ruefully. Unfortunately, he could not get a second black slate from the movie, which meant that he had narrowly missed out on this powerful movie prop.

“So, what shall we do next?”

X-112 asked.

“We wait.”

Chen Chen looked up at the sky, and said, “Anyway, we have fired the missiles and destroyed the Event Horizon. The threat from beyond the skies has been vanquished. So, what if we wait for him a little longer?”

...

One month later.

When Chen Chen had been in Event Horizon’s dimension for three months, Zhao Shanhe and a woman who had healed slowly walked out of the Islip secret military base.

The two of them walked out brazenly and a group of troops opened the doors to release the two.

“Phew!”

Zhao Shanhe exhaled. He turned his head and looked at the tightly guarded military base while controlling several high-ranking officials to personally drive the military car to them.

“Get in the car.”

Zhao Shanhe said to the woman beside him. Then, he got into the car first and both of them were directly chauffeured out of the military base.

Nonetheless, just as the military vehicle was halfway on its journey, a young man landed from a high altitude and stood in front of the military vehicle.

“Screech!”

The military car stopped directly in front of the young man.

“You’re finally out.”

chapters.title: Chapter 591: The Usb Drive’S Secret
chapters.body:
Chapter 591: The Usb Drive’S Secret

Chapter 591: The USB Drive’s Secret

Translator: EndlessFantasy Translation Editor: EndlessFantasy Translation

The appearance of Chen Chen naturally aroused the vigilance of the soldiers in the car.

Several guns popped out but the next second, there was a dazed flash in the eyes of the two high-ranking military officials. They lowered their arms and then got out of the car, vacating their seats.

Chen Chen smiled and sat directly in the back seat of the car.

At this point, there was only one soldier who was driving. In the front passenger seat was the woman who had followed Zhao Shanhe while Chen Chen and Zhao Shanhe were sitting in the back seat of the military vehicle.

Immediately, the vehicle drove in the direction of the center of New York City again.

“I have to say that the power of your Civilization Monolith is baffling.”

Chen Chen looked at Zhao Shanhe beside him. “It manipulates people’s thoughts and makes them a marionette who will do whatever you want. It’s no wonder that you can create the Earth Federation by yourself. With this ability alone, you’re already invincible.”

“But the power of the Civilization Monolith cannot affect you.”

Zhao Shanhe took a deep look at Chen Chen. He raised his palms slightly and was also secretly wary of Chen Chen. “However, during this period, I’ve realized that there are still many flaws in your story. I dare to assert that you’re not the previous dimension key owner at all! So, who on Earth are you?”

Chen Chen nodded. “I hadn’t planned on lying to you for long. Yes, I’m indeed not the previous owner of the dimension key, but all you need to know is that I can help you.”

“If someone who doesn’t even reveal his identity wants to help you, would you believe him?”

Zhao Shanhe chuckled. “What’s more, why do you have to cooperate with me? It’s nothing more than a plot against me.”

Hearing this, Chen Chen could not help laughing. A pitying look glimmered in his eyes. “Do you want to know who I am? If I tell you, I’m afraid you’ll regret it.”

Zhao Shanhe still looked fixedly at Chen Chen as if he would not cooperate with Chen Chen if Chen Chen did not reveal his identity.

Seeing this, Chen Chen thought for a while and then he said, “Tell me, what year was it in the real world when you came to the dimension of Event Horizon?”

Zhao Shanhe frowned but still said, “It was 2002, why?”

“I’m from 2040.”

Chen Chen said immediately, “I’m 38 years old this year and have owned the dimension key for 20 years. At that time, I was a student of the Institute of Biological Sciences at Shangdu Jiao Tong University. When I obtained the dimension key, it was in Professor Wang Xi’s class...”

Hearing Chen Chen’s words, Zhao Shanhe’s expression changed drastically. Even the woman in the front seat turned around in disbelief and glared at Chen Chen. “No, you’re lying to us, right? You must’ve done a background check on Boss, so you fabricated such a lie to deceive us!”

Chen Chen ignored the woman in the front seat. He just looked at Zhao Shanhe silently.

Zhao Shanhe’s expression was slightly somber. He stared at Chen Chen’s eyes, trying to see if Chen Chen showed any signs of lying, but he was disappointed.

Chen Chen’s eyes were pure black, like a dark bottomless pool. One was unable to discern any truths or lies at all.

“No, you must be lying to us, you must be!”

The woman in the front seat screamed. She even snapped and was about to pull out her lightsaber, but Zhao Shanhe stopped her instantly.

Seeing this, Chen Chen continued slowly. “You created the Earth Federation in 1993 but because of an antimemetic infection, the entire Earth Federation completely forgot your existence. Only one quantum computer is left maintaining a fragile alliance...

“Because you knew that you might die, you left a video in ‘Adam’. The video hints at a crisis that had been brewing in the dark. At the same time, you suspect that your predecessor is Professor Wang Xi, but you can’t say it explicitly for some reason. Therefore, you can only use such means to store your student certificate on the campus server. Only by investigating the entire network could someone find the only trace of your existence and Professor Wang Xi’s name is also written there...”

Chen Chen said successively, “You’ve left a hint that you’re going to the dimension of Event Horizon in the video, am I right?”

Hearing this, Zhao Shanhe was completely silent.

Chen Chen understood that it was normal for the two of them to react so strongly because if what he said was true, it meant one thing — Zhao Shanhe died in the dimension of Event Horizon.

“No, maybe it’s a parallel universe!”

The woman in the front seat was still unwilling to believe all this. She emphasized. “It’s a parallel universe, right? Aren’t the worlds that we constantly shuttle through all parallel universes? We shouldn’t be dying here, absolutely not!”

However, Zhao Shanhe only shook his head with a wry smile. Only he could understand that the parallel universes did exist, but the dimension key was unique, like the Civilization Monolith in his possession. If the mysterious young man in front of him was the next owner, there was only one reason, and that was that he died...
